title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Fasting adipose insulin sensitivity/resistance indices one call: Revised_QUICKI, McAuley_index, Adipo_inv, Belfiore_inv_FFA, TyG_inv, TG_HDL_C_inv, sex-specific VAI/LAP (inverted negative = worse). Inputs fasting; common glucose/insulin/TG/HDL unit conversions handled internally.

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: need multiple adipose IR markers metabolic phenotyping clustering. fasting glucose, insulin, lipids, adiposity (waist/BMI); sex optional improves VAI/LAP labeling. want built-plausibility screening NA policy controls.

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Required columns: G0 (mmol/L), I0 (pmol/L), TG (mmol/L), HDL_c (mmol/L), FFA (mmol/L), waist (cm), bmi (kg/m^2). Fasting values ; post-prandial inputs misstate IR. Units conversion: glucose mmol/L, insulin pmol/L, TG mmol/L, HDL mmol/L, FFA mmol/L, waist cm, BMI kg/m^2. different, convert first. Optional helpful: sex (1/2 labels) understand VAI/LAP variant applies.

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Use small subset simulated data. Swap sim_small data.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Map required inputs; left-hand keys fixed.
code: col_map <- list(   G0 = "G0",   I0 = "I0",   TG = "TG",   HDL_c = "HDL_c",   FFA = "FFA",   waist = "waist",   bmi = "BMI" )

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: adipo_is() returns tibble computed index columns — original data. Bind back source rows dplyr::bind_cols() needed. Values ending _inv inverted negative = worse adipose IR. sex variants (VAI_Men/Women, LAP_Men/Women) always returned; pick appropriate column per subject based biological sex:
code: ais <- adipo_is(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   normalize = "none" )  # Group 1 — non-sex-specific indices ais %>%   slice_head(n = 5) %>%   select(Revised_QUICKI, McAuley_index, Adipo_inv, Belfiore_inv_FFA, TyG_inv) #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   Revised_QUICKI McAuley_index Adipo_inv Belfiore_inv_FFA TyG_inv #>            <dbl>         <dbl>     <dbl>            <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1          0.338          4.92     -8.06           -0.221   -9.67 #> 2          0.444         10.2      -1.79           -0.716   -8.07 #> 3          0.348          7.18     -7.64           -0.232   -8.43 #> 4          0.369          7.84     -5.59           -0.303   -8.23 #> 5          0.394          5.62     -5.06           -0.330   -8.51  # Group 2 — sex-specific and ratio indices ais %>%   slice_head(n = 5) %>%   select(TG_HDL_C_inv, VAI_Men_inv, VAI_Women_inv, LAP_Men_inv, LAP_Women_inv) #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   TG_HDL_C_inv VAI_Men_inv VAI_Women_inv LAP_Men_inv LAP_Women_inv #>          <dbl>       <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1        -5.55      -3.04          -4.61     -106.          -128.  #> 2        -1.35      -0.783         -1.19      -25.0          -30.0 #> 3        -1.95      -0.991         -1.51      -19.3          -26.8 #> 4        -1.43      -0.815         -1.24      -24.0          -30.5 #> 5        -2.36      -1.10          -1.67       -9.84         -21.3 # Bind sex back from source, then derive a single VAI and LAP per subject. # Adjust the if_else condition to match your sex coding (e.g. 1/2, "M"/"F"). ais_with_sex <- dplyr::bind_cols(dplyr::select(sim_small, sex), ais)  ais_with_sex %>%   dplyr::mutate(     VAI_inv = dplyr::if_else(sex == 1, VAI_Men_inv, VAI_Women_inv),     LAP_inv = dplyr::if_else(sex == 1, LAP_Men_inv, LAP_Women_inv)   ) %>%   dplyr::select(sex, Revised_QUICKI, TyG_inv, VAI_inv, LAP_inv) %>%   slice_head(n = 5)

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Compare NA policies small slice.
code: demo <- sim_small[1:6, c("G0", "I0", "TG", "HDL_c", "FFA", "waist", "BMI")] demo$G0[2] <- NA        # missing glucose  ais_keep <- adipo_is(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   normalize = "none",   verbose = FALSE )  ais_omit <- adipo_is(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "omit",   normalize = "none",   verbose = FALSE )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(ais_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(ais_omit),   keep_preview = ais_keep %>% select(ends_with("_inv")) %>% slice_head(n = 3) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 6 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 5 #>  #> $keep_preview #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>   VAI_Men_inv VAI_Women_inv TG_HDL_C_inv TyG_inv LAP_Men_inv LAP_Women_inv #>         <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>         <dbl> #> 1      -3.04          -4.61        -5.55   -9.67      -106.         -128.  #> 2      -0.783         -1.19        -1.35   NA          -25.0         -30.0 #> 3      -0.991         -1.51        -1.95   -8.43       -19.3         -26.8 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: Adipo_inv <dbl>

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Use normalize put indices common scale modelling dimension reduction: Available options: "none" (default — raw scale), "z" (mean 0 / SD 1), "range" (0–1 min–max), "robust" (median / IQR), "inverse" (1/x).
code: ais_z <- adipo_is(   data      = sim_small,   col_map   = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   normalize = "z"      # z-score: mean 0, SD 1 )  ais_z %>%   slice_head(n = 5) %>%   select(Revised_QUICKI, McAuley_index, TyG_inv, Adipo_inv) #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   Revised_QUICKI McAuley_index TyG_inv Adipo_inv #>            <dbl>         <dbl>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1        -0.442         -0.721  -1.37     0.0702 #> 2         1.38           2.34    1.49     1.06   #> 3        -0.260          0.575   0.835    0.137  #> 4         0.0915         0.954   1.20     0.459  #> 5         0.519         -0.318   0.692    0.543

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Set verbose = TRUE emit three structured messages per call: Preparing inputs — start--function signal. Column map — confirms data column required key resolved . Example: adipo_is(): column map: G0 -> 'G0', I0 -> 'I0', TG -> 'TG', ... Results summary — shows many rows computed successfully (non-NA) per output column. Example: adipo_is(): results: Revised_QUICKI 28/30, VAI_Men_inv 28/30, McAuley_index 27/30, ... verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") ("debug") active session messages print: Enable globally entire session: options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform"). Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) options(healthmarkers.verbose = "none").
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  invisible(adipo_is(   data          = sim_small[1:5, ],   col_map       = col_map,   na_action     = "keep",   normalize     = "none",   verbose       = TRUE )) #> adipo_is(): reading input 'data' — 5 rows × 519 variables #> adipo_is(): col_map (7 columns — 7 specified) #>   G0                ->  'G0' #>   I0                ->  'I0' #>   TG                ->  'TG' #>   HDL_c             ->  'HDL_c' #>   FFA               ->  'FFA' #>   waist             ->  'waist' #>   bmi               ->  'BMI' #> adipo_is(): computing markers: #>   Revised_QUICKI       [G0, I0, FFA] #>   VAI_Men_inv          [waist, bmi, TG, HDL_c] #>   VAI_Women_inv        [waist, bmi, TG, HDL_c] #>   TG_HDL_C_inv         [TG, HDL_c] #>   TyG_inv              [TG, G0] #>   LAP_Men_inv          [waist, TG] #>   LAP_Women_inv        [waist, TG] #>   McAuley_index        [I0, TG] #>   Adipo_inv            [FFA, I0] #>   Belfiore_inv_FFA     [I0, FFA] #> adipo_is(): results: id 5/5, Revised_QUICKI 5/5, VAI_Men_inv 5/5, VAI_Women_inv 5/5, TG_HDL_C_inv 5/5, TyG_inv 5/5, LAP_Men_inv 5/5, LAP_Women_inv 5/5, McAuley_index 5/5, Adipo_inv 5/5, Belfiore_inv_FFA 5/5  options(old_opt)  # restore

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: seven required mappings must provided; missing mappings columns abort. na_action controls row handling: keep propagates NA outputs; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts. Built-conversions: glucose 18 (mg/dL), insulin /6 (muU/mL), TG 88.57 (mg/dL), HDL *38.67 (mg/dL); FFA/waist/BMI unchanged.

title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices
text: Non-fasting samples inflate TG/glucose distort TyG/VAI/LAP. Mis-specified units (e.g., mg/dL glucose without converting) skew every index; convert upstream lab units differ. Missing sex block computation one VAI/LAP applies per subject; interpret accordingly. modelling, use normalize = "z" (z-score), "range" (0–1), "robust" (median/IQR); default "none" keeps raw index scales. See Normalise outputs section . ## Validation notes Revised_QUICKI: typical adults ~0.3–0.6; lower worse IR. Computed log10(I0 [mU/L]) + log10(G0 [mg/dL]) + log10(FFA [mmol/L]). McAuley_index: typical adults ~3–10; higher better insulin sensitivity. Uses ln(TG [mmol/L]) ln(I0 [mU/L]) — TG kept mmol/L internal mg/dL conversion. TyG_inv: typically −8 −12 adults; negative = worse IR. Computed TG G0 mg/dL. LAP_Men_inv / LAP_Women_inv: computed −(WC−threshold) × TG [mmol/L] (Kahn 2005). Typical values −10 −80; negative = worse. VAI_Men_inv / VAI_Women_inv: uses TG HDL mmol/L Amato 2010 reference constants (1.03, 1.31, 0.81, 1.52). Typical range −1 −5; negative = worse. Spot-check TG_HDL_C_inv: equal −(TG_mg/dL / HDL_mg/dL).

title: Adiposity SDS
text: Required: reference list (ref) mean sd > 0 per variable, matching columns data (via col_map$vars identity names). Units: data units must match reference (e.g., BMI kg/m^2, waist cm, height cm). Policies: choose na_action (keep/omit/error) sds_cap + extreme_action cap large SDS magnitudes. Optional: return_summary = TRUE get diagnostics, diagnostics = TRUE warnings, verbose = TRUE progress.

title: Adiposity SDS
text: Use small slice packaged simulated data. Replace sim_small data.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)

title: Adiposity SDS
text: Map variable column names.
code: col_map <- list(   vars = list(     BMI = "BMI",     waist = "waist",     height = "height"   ) )

title: Adiposity SDS
text: adiposity_sds() returns tibble <var>_SDS columns — original data. Bind back dplyr::bind_cols() needed. Interpretation: <var>_SDS (x - mean)/sd; values near 0 average, positive , negative reference. extreme_action = "cap" trims SDS values beyond sds_cap.
code: ref <- list(   BMI = c(mean = mean(sim_small$BMI, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(sim_small$BMI, na.rm = TRUE)),   waist = c(mean = mean(sim_small$waist, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(sim_small$waist, na.rm = TRUE)),   height = c(mean = mean(sim_small$height, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(sim_small$height, na.rm = TRUE)) )  asds <- adiposity_sds(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   ref = ref,   na_action = "keep",   extreme_action = "cap",   sds_cap = 6,   verbose = FALSE )  new_cols <- setdiff(names(asds), names(sim_small)) asds %>% slice_head(n = 5) %>% select(all_of(new_cols)) #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   BMI_SDS waist_SDS height_SDS #>     <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1   0.696     0.506      0.625 #> 2   0.279     0.617      1.15  #> 3  -0.209    -0.935      0.654 #> 4  -0.354    -0.182      1.13  #> 5  -0.734    -2.05      -1.11

title: Adiposity SDS
text: Compare NA policies SDS capping.
code: demo <- sim_small[1:8, c("BMI", "waist", "height")] demo$BMI[3] <- NA        # missing  demo_keep <- adiposity_sds(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   ref = ref,   na_action = "keep",   extreme_action = "cap",   sds_cap = 5,   verbose = FALSE )  demo_omit <- adiposity_sds(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   ref = ref,   na_action = "omit",   extreme_action = "cap",   sds_cap = 5,   verbose = FALSE )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(demo_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(demo_omit),   capped_preview = demo_keep %>% select(ends_with("_SDS")) %>% slice_head(n = 3) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 8 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 7 #>  #> $capped_preview #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>   BMI_SDS waist_SDS height_SDS #>     <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1   0.696     0.506      0.625 #> 2   0.279     0.617      1.15  #> 3  NA        -0.935      0.654

title: Adiposity SDS
text: Set return_summary = TRUE get list SDS tibble plus counts omitted rows, raw extreme adjustments, SDS extremes:
code: res <- adiposity_sds(   data           = demo,   col_map        = col_map,   ref            = ref,   na_action      = "omit",   extreme_action = "cap",   sds_cap        = 5,   return_summary = TRUE,   verbose        = FALSE )  res$summary #> $rows_in #> [1] 8 #>  #> $rows_out #> [1] 7 #>  #> $omitted_rows #> [1] 1 #>  #> $total_extreme #> [1] 0 #>  #> $per_var #>   variable n_missing n_extreme #> 1      BMI         0         0 #> 2    waist         0         0 #> 3   height         0         0 head(res$data)     # SDS tibble #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   BMI_SDS waist_SDS height_SDS #>     <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1   0.696     0.506      0.625 #> 2   0.279     0.617      1.15  #> 3  -0.354    -0.182      1.13  #> 4  -0.734    -2.05      -1.11  #> 5   1.53     -0.972     -1.36  #> 6  -1.77     -1.12      -0.761 res$warnings       # any diagnostic messages collected #> [1] "adiposity_sds(): 'BMI' missingness 12.5%"

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: Sex-stratified SDS (z-scores) anthropometric measures using separate male/female references, NA policies flexible variable mapping.

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: need sex-specific standardization modeling flagging outliers. study men women want SDS relative sex-appropriate reference means/SDs. want built-options missing data handling plausibility screening.

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: Required: sex (coded M/F 1/2 mapping) least one anthropometric variable present ref$M ref$F. Reference list: ref$M ref$F must cover identical variable names, mean sd > 0. Units: use units data reference (e.g., BMI kg/m^2, waist cm, height cm). Optional: allow_partial = TRUE skip missing variables instead erroring.

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: Use small slice packaged simulated data. Replace sim_small data.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path)  # Normalize sex labels to M/F and ensure both sexes appear in the demo slice sim_small <- sim %>%   mutate(sex = case_when(     as.character(sex) %in% c("M", "F") ~ as.character(sex),     sex %in% c("male", "Male", "m") ~ "M",     sex %in% c("female", "Female", "f") ~ "F",     sex %in% c(1L, "1") ~ "M",     sex %in% c(2L, "2") ~ "F",     TRUE ~ NA_character_   )) %>%   filter(!is.na(sex)) %>%   slice_head(n = 200)

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: adiposity_sds_strat() returns tibble <var>_SDS columns — original data. Use dplyr::bind_cols() rejoin. Spot-check row 1 (male): BMI_SDS = (25.2 − 24.5) / 3.8 = 0.1842. Row 2 (female): BMI_SDS = (21.8 − 22.1) / 4.2 = -0.0714.
code: # Hard-coded reference values (typical population means/SDs) ref <- list(   M = list(BMI = c(mean = 24.5, sd = 3.8), waist = c(mean = 88, sd = 12)),   F = list(BMI = c(mean = 22.1, sd = 4.2), waist = c(mean = 76, sd = 11)) )  df_demo <- data.frame(   sex   = c("M", "F", "M", "F"),   BMI   = c(25.2, 21.8, 27.1, 19.5),   waist = c(85, 72, 95, 68) )  col_map <- list(sex = "sex", vars = list(BMI = "BMI", waist = "waist"))  sds_out <- adiposity_sds_strat(df_demo, col_map = col_map, ref = ref) sds_out #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   BMI_SDS waist_SDS #>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  0.184     -0.25  #> 2 -0.0714    -0.364 #> 3  0.684      0.583 #> 4 -0.619     -0.727

title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: Map sex variable column names.
code: col_map <- list(   sex = "sex",   vars = list(     BMI = "BMI",     waist = "waist",     height = "height"   ) )

what: Walkthrough with simulated data (build sex-specific refs)
title: Adiposity SDS (Sex-Stratified)
text: Interpretation: <var>_SDS standardized sex-specific mean/SD; values near 0 average sex, positive , negative .
code: # Build sex-specific references from the demo slice ref_sex <- sim_small |>   group_by(sex) |>   summarise(     BMI = list(c(mean = mean(BMI, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(BMI, na.rm = TRUE))),     waist = list(c(mean = mean(waist, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(waist, na.rm = TRUE))),     height = list(c(mean = mean(height, na.rm = TRUE), sd = sd(height, na.rm = TRUE)))   )  if (!all(c("M", "F") %in% ref_sex$sex)) {   stop("Demo data slice must contain both M and F; normalize or increase sample.") }  ref <- list(   M = list(     BMI = ref_sex$BMI[[ref_sex$sex == "M"]],     waist = ref_sex$waist[[ref_sex$sex == "M"]],     height = ref_sex$height[[ref_sex$sex == "M"]]   ),   F = list(     BMI = ref_sex$BMI[[ref_sex$sex == "F"]],     waist = ref_sex$waist[[ref_sex$sex == "F"]],     height = ref_sex$height[[ref_sex$sex == "F"]]   ) )  asds_strat <- adiposity_sds_strat(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   ref = ref,   na_action = "keep",   allow_partial = FALSE,   prefix = "",   verbose = FALSE )  new_cols <- setdiff(names(asds_strat), names(sim_small)) asds_strat %>%   slice_head(n = 5) %>%   select(all_of(new_cols))

code: demo <- sim_small[1:8, c("sex", "BMI", "waist", "height")] demo$BMI[3] <- NA         # missing  demo_keep <- adiposity_sds_strat(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   ref = ref,   na_action = "keep",   allow_partial = FALSE,   prefix = "" )  demo_omit <- adiposity_sds_strat(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   ref = ref,   na_action = "omit",   allow_partial = FALSE,   prefix = "" )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(demo_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(demo_omit),   keep_preview = demo_keep %>% select(ends_with("_SDS")) %>% slice_head(n = 3) )

title: Allostatic Load
text: Compute composite Allostatic Load (AL) score: biomarker exceeding threshold contributes 1 count. multiple biomarkers rule >; single biomarker >=. Returns tibble single integer column AllostaticLoad. Optional extreme screening applies columns whose names contain “sds”.

title: Allostatic Load
text: want simple count biomarkers exceeding risk cut-points. small set biomarkers clear thresholds control. may include pre-computed SDS columns optionally cap implausible SDS values.

title: Allostatic Load
text: Thresholds: named list scalar numeric cutoffs; names must match columns (mapped via col_map). Data: matching biomarker columns; units align thresholds supply. Policies: na_action (keep treats missing unflagged; omit drops rows; error aborts). Optional: return_summary = TRUE counts flags/omissions; verbose progress.

title: Allostatic Load
text: Use small subset simulated data. Replace sim_small data.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)

title: Allostatic Load
text: Provide thresholds , needed, map column names.
code: thresholds <- list(sbp = 130, dbp = 85, CRP = 3) col_map <- list(sbp = "sbp", dbp = "dbp", CRP = "CRP")

title: Allostatic Load
text: Interpretation: AllostaticLoad count biomarkers thresholds. >1 biomarker rule >; single biomarker >=.
code: al <- allostatic_load(   data = sim_small,   thresholds = thresholds,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE )  al %>% slice_head(n = 5) #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   id    AllostaticLoad #>   <chr>          <int> #> 1 P001               1 #> 2 P002               1 #> 3 P003               0 #> 4 P004               0 #> 5 P005               2

title: Allostatic Load
text: Demonstrate row handling.
code: demo <- sim_small[1:8, c("sbp", "dbp", "CRP")] demo$sbp[3] <- NA  al_keep <- allostatic_load(   data = demo,   thresholds = thresholds,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE )  al_omit <- allostatic_load(   data = demo,   thresholds = thresholds,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "omit",   verbose = FALSE )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(al_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(al_omit),   head_keep = al_keep %>% slice_head(n = 3) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 8 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 7 #>  #> $head_keep #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>   AllostaticLoad #>            <int> #> 1              1 #> 2              1 #> 3              0

title: Allostatic Load
text: return_summary = TRUE returns list(data, summary, warnings) instead bare tibble. summary element contains rows, biomarkers, total_flags, mean_flags.
code: df_rs <- data.frame(   SBP = c(118, 142, 130, 155, 120),   DBP = c(76,   92,  85,  98,  78),   CRP = c(1.2,  4.8, 2.1, 5.0, 0.9) ) thr_rs <- list(SBP = 130, DBP = 85, CRP = 3)  res <- allostatic_load(   data      = df_rs,   thresholds = thr_rs,   na_action  = "keep",   return_summary = TRUE,   verbose    = FALSE ) res$summary #> $rows #> [1] 5 #>  #> $biomarkers #> [1] 3 #>  #> $total_flags #> [1] 6 #>  #> $mean_flags #> [1] 1.2 res$data #> # A tibble: 5 × 1 #>   AllostaticLoad #>            <int> #> 1              0 #> 2              3 #> 3              0 #> 4              3 #> 5              0

title: Allostatic Load
text: Set verbose = TRUE emit three structured messages per call: Preparing inputs — start--function signal. Column map — confirms data column threshold key resolved . Example: allostatic_load(): column map: SBP -> 'SBP', DBP -> 'DBP', ... Results summary — shows many rows computed successfully (non-NA) per output column. Example: allostatic_load(): results: AllostaticLoad 30/30, ... verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") active: Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) "none".
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  df_v <- data.frame(SBP = c(118, 142), DBP = c(76, 92)) allostatic_load(df_v, thresholds = list(SBP = 130, DBP = 85), verbose = TRUE) #> allostatic_load(): reading input 'df_v' — 2 rows × 2 variables #> allostatic_load(): col_map (2 columns — 2 inferred from data) #>   SBP               ->  'SBP'    (inferred) #>   DBP               ->  'DBP'    (inferred) #> allostatic_load(): computing markers: #>   AllostaticLoad [SBP, DBP] #> allostatic_load(): results: AllostaticLoad 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>   AllostaticLoad #>            <int> #> 1              0 #> 2              2  options(old_opt)

title: Allostatic Load
text: Threshold names must match columns (mapped); missing columns abort. na_action: keep treats missing unflagged (flag = 0), omit drops rows, error aborts missing. Multi-biomarker rule >; single-biomarker rule >=.

title: Allostatic Load
text: Threshold units mismatched data units misflag results; align units first. Passing one biomarker changes rule >=; set thresholds accordingly. na_action = keep leaves missing inputs unflagged (zero); use omit stricter handling.

title: Allostatic Load
text: Spot-check one biomarker: flag = 1 value exceeds threshold (equals one biomarker), else 0. Sum per-biomarker flags equal AllostaticLoad row.

title: Allostatic Load
text: Run hm_col_report(your_data) check biomarker columns auto-detected building col_map. See Multi-Biobank Compatibility article recognised synonyms.
code: hm_col_report(your_data)

title: Allostatic Load
text: adiposity_sds() adiposity_sds_strat() SDS creation. health_markers() health_summary() broader panels.

title: ALM/BMI Index
text: Compute ALM/BMI flag low muscle mass using FNIH cut-points (men < 0.789; women < 0.512) optional NA extreme handling.

title: ALM/BMI Index
text: need quick sarcopenia risk flag based ALM relative body size. ALM (kg), BMI (kg/m^2), sex, want FNIH cut-points applied automatically. want option cap implausible ALM/BMI values control missing-data policy.

title: ALM/BMI Index
text: Required columns: alm (kg), bmi (kg/m^2), sex (m/f/1/0; values yield NA flag). Defaults: ALM kg, BMI kg/m^2. unit conversion performed. Policies: na_action (keep/warn/ignore behave like keep; omit drops; error aborts). FNIH cut-points built ; nothing configure beyond column mapping.

title: ALM/BMI Index
text: Use small subset simulated data. derive simple ALM proxy illustration.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)  # Example proxy for ALM (replace with measured ALM when available) sim_small <- sim_small %>%   mutate(ALM_kg = pmax(10, pmin(35, weight * 0.35)))

title: ALM/BMI Index
text: Map columns; sex normalized first letter (m/f/1/0).
code: col_map <- list(   alm = "ALM_kg",   bmi = "BMI",   sex = "sex" )

title: ALM/BMI Index
text: Interpretation: alm_bmi_ratio ALM/BMI. low_muscle_mass TRUE sex-specific FNIH cut-point; NA sex ratio NA.
code: alm_out <- alm_bmi_index(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE )  alm_out %>%   slice_head(n = 5) %>%   select(alm_bmi_ratio, low_muscle_mass) #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   alm_bmi_ratio low_muscle_mass #>           <dbl> <lgl>           #> 1         1.09  FALSE           #> 2         1.16  FALSE           #> 3         1.10  FALSE           #> 4         1.16  FALSE           #> 5         0.892 NA

title: ALM/BMI Index
text: Compare NA handling.
code: demo <- sim_small[1:8, c("ALM_kg", "BMI", "sex")] demo$ALM_kg[3] <- NA  alm_keep <- alm_bmi_index(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE )  alm_omit <- alm_bmi_index(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "omit",   verbose = FALSE )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(alm_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(alm_omit),   preview = alm_keep %>% select(alm_bmi_ratio, low_muscle_mass) %>% slice_head(n = 3) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 8 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 7 #>  #> $preview #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   alm_bmi_ratio low_muscle_mass #>           <dbl> <lgl>           #> 1          1.09 FALSE           #> 2          1.16 FALSE           #> 3         NA    NA

title: ALM/BMI Index
text: Set verbose = TRUE emit three structured messages per call: Preparing inputs — start--function signal. Column map — confirms data column required key (alm, bmi, sex) resolved . Example: alm_bmi_index(): column map: alm -> 'ALM_kg', bmi -> 'BMI', sex -> 'Sex' Results summary — shows many rows computed successfully (non-NA) per output column. Example: alm_bmi_index(): results: alm_bmi_index 30/30, low_alm_bmi_flag 30/30, ... verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") active: Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) "none".
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  df_v <- data.frame(   ALM_kg = c(14.0, 9.5),   BMI    = c(24.0, 22.0),   Sex    = c("male", "female") ) alm_bmi_index(df_v,               col_map = list(alm = "ALM_kg", bmi = "BMI", sex = "Sex"),               verbose  = TRUE) #> alm_bmi_index(): reading input 'df_v' — 2 rows × 3 variables #> alm_bmi_index(): col_map (3 columns — 3 specified) #>   alm               ->  'ALM_kg' #>   bmi               ->  'BMI' #>   sex               ->  'Sex' #> alm_bmi_index(): computing markers: #>   alm_bmi_ratio    [ALM / BMI] #>   low_muscle_mass  [ratio < sex-specific FNIH cut-point] #> alm_bmi_index(): results: alm_bmi_ratio 2/2, low_muscle_mass 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   alm_bmi_ratio low_muscle_mass #>           <dbl> <lgl>           #> 1         0.583 TRUE            #> 2         0.432 TRUE  options(old_opt)

title: ALM/BMI Index
text: Required columns must mapped; missing columns abort. na_action controls row handling (keep/warn/ignore behave like keep; omit drops; error aborts). Sex must normalize m/f/1/0 flag evaluate; otherwise flag NA.

title: ALM/BMI Index
text: Feeding ALM pounds BMI non-metric units misclassify; keep ALM kg BMI kg/m^2. Using ALM proxy (like weight * 0.35) illustrative ; use measured ALM possible. na_action = keep leaves missing inputs NA; choose omit stricter pipelines.

title: ALM/BMI Index
text: Spot-check one row: ratio = ALM/BMI; compare FNIH thresholds (0.789 men, 0.512 women) verify low_muscle_mass. Expect low_muscle_mass NA sex normalized.

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: Compute atherogenic risk ratios lipid panel: - AIP = log10(TG / HDL_c) - Castelli Risk Index (CRI_I) = TC / HDL_c - Castelli Risk Index II (CRI_II) = LDL_c / HDL_c ratios unitless; use consistent lipid units (mg/dL mmol/L).

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: triglycerides HDL cholesterol want AIP. also total /LDL cholesterol want Castelli indices. need strict missing-data handling.

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: Packages: HealthMarkers, dplyr (display). Data frame lipid columns; required keys: TG HDL_c. Optional: TC LDL_c. Numeric lipids; non-numeric coerced warnings; non-finite become NA. Decide row policy: na_action = keep (default), omit, error.

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: Replace example slice data frame.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: TG HDL_c required. TC LDL_c optional needed Castelli indices.
code: col_map <- list(   TG = "TG",   HDL_c = "HDL_c",   TC = "TC",   LDL_c = "LDL_c" )

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: Defaults keep rows missing lipids return NA ratios.
code: aip_out <- atherogenic_indices(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE )  new_cols <- setdiff(names(aip_out), names(sim_small)) head(select(aip_out, all_of(new_cols))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>       AIP CRI_I CRI_II #>     <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1  0.384   4.95   2.85 #> 2 -0.229   4.75   3.48 #> 3 -0.0710  5.52   4.13 #> 4 -0.204   3.02   1.73 #> 5  0.0134  2.96   1.49 #> 6 -0.118   3.38   2.04

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: col_map: named list TG, HDL_c (required), optional TC, LDL_c. Missing required keys error. na_action: keep (default, rows retained; ratios become NA), omit (drop rows NA used lipids), error (abort NA). verbose: emit step messages.

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: Non-numeric lipids coerced numeric; NA introduced warned. Non-finite become NA. Zero HDL_c denominators yield NA ratios warning; Castelli indices use HDL_c divisor. Row policy: keep vs omit vs error via na_action.

title: Atherogenic Indices
code: demo <- sim_small demo$HDL_c[c(2, 5)] <- NA  aip_keep <- atherogenic_indices(demo, col_map, na_action = "keep") #> atherogenic_indices(): reading input 'demo' — 30 rows × 519 variables #> atherogenic_indices(): col_map (4 columns — 4 specified) #>   TG                ->  'TG' #>   HDL_c             ->  'HDL_c' #>   TC                ->  'TC' #>   LDL_c             ->  'LDL_c' #> atherogenic_indices(): computing markers: #>   AIP        [log10(TG / HDL_c)] #>   CRI_I      [TC / HDL_c [if TC available]] #>   CRI_II     [LDL_c / HDL_c [if LDL_c available]] #> atherogenic_indices(): results: id 30/30, AIP 28/30, CRI_I 28/30, CRI_II 28/30 aip_omit <- atherogenic_indices(demo, col_map, na_action = "omit") #> atherogenic_indices(): reading input 'demo' — 30 rows × 519 variables #> atherogenic_indices(): col_map (4 columns — 4 specified) #>   TG                ->  'TG' #>   HDL_c             ->  'HDL_c' #>   TC                ->  'TC' #>   LDL_c             ->  'LDL_c' #> atherogenic_indices(): computing markers: #>   AIP        [log10(TG / HDL_c)] #>   CRI_I      [TC / HDL_c [if TC available]] #>   CRI_II     [LDL_c / HDL_c [if LDL_c available]] #> atherogenic_indices(): results: id 28/28, AIP 28/28, CRI_I 28/28, CRI_II 28/28  list(   keep_rows = nrow(aip_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(aip_omit),   sample_ratios = head(select(aip_keep, AIP, CRI_I, CRI_II)) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 30 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 28 #>  #> $sample_ratios #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>       AIP CRI_I CRI_II #>     <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1  0.384   4.95   2.85 #> 2 NA      NA     NA    #> 3 -0.0710  5.52   4.13 #> 4 -0.204   3.02   1.73 #> 5 NA      NA     NA    #> 6 -0.118   3.38   2.04

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: Zero negative HDL_c denominators yield NA ratios warning. Filter remove values calling.
code: demo2 <- sim_small demo2$TG[5] <- 12000  # extreme TG # Note: extreme values will produce extreme ratio outputs; pre-filter if needed head(select(atherogenic_indices(demo2, col_map = col_map), AIP, CRI_I, CRI_II)) #> atherogenic_indices(): reading input 'demo2' — 30 rows × 519 variables #> atherogenic_indices(): col_map (4 columns — 4 specified) #>   TG                ->  'TG' #>   HDL_c             ->  'HDL_c' #>   TC                ->  'TC' #>   LDL_c             ->  'LDL_c' #> atherogenic_indices(): computing markers: #>   AIP        [log10(TG / HDL_c)] #>   CRI_I      [TC / HDL_c [if TC available]] #>   CRI_II     [LDL_c / HDL_c [if LDL_c available]] #> atherogenic_indices(): results: id 30/30, AIP 30/30, CRI_I 30/30, CRI_II 30/30 #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>       AIP CRI_I CRI_II #>     <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1  0.384   4.95   2.85 #> 2 -0.229   4.75   3.48 #> 3 -0.0710  5.52   4.13 #> 4 -0.204   3.02   1.73 #> 5  3.88    2.96   1.49 #> 6 -0.118   3.38   2.04

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: AIP: log10(TG / HDL_c) CRI_I: TC / HDL_c (NA TC mapped/present) CRI_II: LDL_c / HDL_c (NA LDL_c mapped/present) Rows drop na_action = “omit” na_action = “error” encounters missing.

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: Keep lipid units consistent; ratios unitless inputs must share units. Mapping omissions TG HDL_c throw errors; missing TC/LDL_c just yield NA Castelli indices. Zero HDL_c produces NA ratios warning; address true zeros computing. Use na_action = “error” pipelines must fail fast missing lipids.

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: Spot-check AIP: TG = 150 HDL_c = 50, expected AIP = log10(3) ~ 0.477. CRI_I decrease HDL_c rises lipids unchanged; similar CRI_II. Confirm rows NA TG/HDL_c dropped na_action = “omit”.

title: Atherogenic Indices
text: health_summary() combined marker panels. glycemic_markers() glucose insulin-derived indices.

title: BODE Index
text: Compute 0-10 BODE score (BMI, Obstruction, Dyspnea, Exercise capacity) FEV1 % predicted, 6-minute walk distance, mMRC dyspnea, BMI. Accepts percent-predicted column derives raw/predicted FEV1. Higher scores indicate worse prognosis. Component scoring: FEV1% (>=65 -> 0, 50-64 -> 1, 36-49 -> 2, <=35 -> 3); 6MWD meters (>=350 -> 0, 250-349 -> 1, 150-249 -> 2, <=149 -> 3); mMRC (0-1 -> 0, 2 -> 1, 3 -> 2, 4 -> 3); BMI (>21 -> 0, <=21 -> 1).

title: BODE Index
text: spirometry plus 6MWD, mMRC, BMI COPD risk stratification. want derive FEV1% raw/predicted FEV1 use existing percent-predicted column.

title: BODE Index
text: Packages: HealthMarkers, dplyr (display). Data columns: choose exactly one FEV1 source: fev1_pct (fev1 + fev1_pred) fev1_pp; plus sixmwd, mmrc, bmi. Numeric inputs; non-numeric coerced warnings; non-finite become NA. Row policy via na_action: keep (default), omit, error; warn/ignore behave like keep emit messages.

title: BODE Index
text: Replace example slice data frame.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)

text: use FEV1 percent-predicted example data.
code: col_map <- list(   fev1_pct = "FEV1pct",   sixmwd = "sixmwd",   mmrc = "mmrc",   bmi = "BMI" )

title: BODE Index
text: Defaults keep rows missing inputs return NA scores.
code: bode_out <- bode_index(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE )  new_cols <- setdiff(names(bode_out), names(sim_small)) head(select(bode_out, all_of(new_cols))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   bode_index fev1_score walk_score mmrc_score bmi_score #>        <int>      <int>      <int>      <int>     <int> #> 1          3          1          2          0         0 #> 2          0          0          0          0         0 #> 3          3          0          0          3         0 #> 4          0          0          0          0         0 #> 5          2          0          2          0         0 #> 6          1          0          0          1         0

title: BODE Index
text: col_map: pick one FEV1 source (fev1_pct fev1 + fev1_pred fev1_pp) plus sixmwd, mmrc, bmi; missing required keys error. na_action: keep (default), omit (drop rows missing required inputs), error (abort missing); warn/ignore act like keep warn. verbose: emit step messages.

title: BODE Index
text: Non-numeric inputs coerced; NA introduced warned. Non-finite become NA. Missing required inputs yield NA scores na_action keep/warn/ignore; rows drop omit; error aborts.

title: BODE Index
code: demo <- sim_small demo$mmrc[c(2, 6)] <- NA  a_keep <- bode_index(demo, col_map, na_action = "keep") #> bode_index(): reading input 'demo' — 30 rows × 519 variables #> bode_index(): col_map (6 columns — 4 specified, 2 inferred from data) #>   fev1_pct          ->  'FEV1pct' #>   sixmwd            ->  'sixmwd' #>   mmrc              ->  'mmrc' #>   bmi               ->  'BMI' #>   fev1_pp           ->  'FEV1pct'    (inferred) #>   fev1              ->  'FEV1'    (inferred) #> bode_index(): computing markers: #>   bode_index  [0-10 COPD severity score] #> bode_index(): results: id 30/30, bode_index 28/30, fev1_pct 30/30, fev1_score 30/30, walk_score 30/30, mmrc_score 28/30, bmi_score 30/30 a_omit <- bode_index(demo, col_map, na_action = "omit") #> bode_index(): reading input 'demo' — 30 rows × 519 variables #> bode_index(): col_map (6 columns — 4 specified, 2 inferred from data) #>   fev1_pct          ->  'FEV1pct' #>   sixmwd            ->  'sixmwd' #>   mmrc              ->  'mmrc' #>   bmi               ->  'BMI' #>   fev1_pp           ->  'FEV1pct'    (inferred) #>   fev1              ->  'FEV1'    (inferred) #> bode_index(): computing markers: #>   bode_index  [0-10 COPD severity score] #> bode_index(): results: id 28/28, bode_index 28/28, fev1_pct 28/28, fev1_score 28/28, walk_score 28/28, mmrc_score 28/28, bmi_score 28/28  list(   keep_rows = nrow(a_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(a_omit),   sample_scores = head(select(a_keep, bode_index, fev1_score, walk_score, mmrc_score, bmi_score)) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 30 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 28 #>  #> $sample_scores #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   bode_index fev1_score walk_score mmrc_score bmi_score #>        <int>      <int>      <int>      <int>     <int> #> 1          3          1          2          0         0 #> 2         NA          0          0         NA         0 #> 3          3          0          0          3         0 #> 4          0          0          0          0         0 #> 5          2          0          2          0         0 #> 6         NA          0          0         NA         0

title: BODE Index
text: Extreme inputs produce extreme scores. Pre-filter implausible values calling.
code: demo2 <- sim_small demo2$sixmwd[5] <- 20   # extreme low walk demo2$BMI[6] <- 80      # extreme high BMI  head(select(bode_index(demo2, col_map = col_map, na_action = "keep"), bode_index, fev1_score, walk_score, mmrc_score, bmi_score)) #> bode_index(): reading input 'demo2' — 30 rows × 519 variables #> bode_index(): col_map (6 columns — 4 specified, 2 inferred from data) #>   fev1_pct          ->  'FEV1pct' #>   sixmwd            ->  'sixmwd' #>   mmrc              ->  'mmrc' #>   bmi               ->  'BMI' #>   fev1_pp           ->  'FEV1pct'    (inferred) #>   fev1              ->  'FEV1'    (inferred) #> bode_index(): computing markers: #>   bode_index  [0-10 COPD severity score] #> bode_index(): results: id 30/30, bode_index 30/30, fev1_pct 30/30, fev1_score 30/30, walk_score 30/30, mmrc_score 30/30, bmi_score 30/30 #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   bode_index fev1_score walk_score mmrc_score bmi_score #>        <int>      <int>      <int>      <int>     <int> #> 1          3          1          2          0         0 #> 2          0          0          0          0         0 #> 3          3          0          0          3         0 #> 4          0          0          0          0         0 #> 5          3          0          3          0         0 #> 6          1          0          0          1         0

title: BODE Index
text: bode_index (0-10) Component scores: fev1_score, walk_score, mmrc_score, bmi_score fev1_pct (derived mapped) Rows drop na_action = “omit” na_action = “error” aborts.

title: BODE Index
text: Map exactly one FEV1 source; providing multiple percent-predicted sources allowed. Keep units consistent: FEV1 liters deriving percent, 6MWD meters, BMI kg/m^2. mMRC must 0-4; true zeros valid. warn/ignore behave like keep; choose omit error strict pipelines.

title: BODE Index
text: Spot-check: FEV1% = 40, 6MWD = 200, mMRC = 3, BMI = 20 yields scores 2 + 2 + 2 + 1 = bode_index 7. Verify rows NA mmrc retained vs dropped according na_action. Confirm percent-predicted derivation: fev1_pp match 100 * fev1 / fev1_pred test row.

title: BODE Index
text: Set verbose = TRUE emit three structured messages per call: Preparing inputs — start--function signal. Column map — confirms data column required key resolved . Example: bode_index(): column map: fev1_pct -> 'FEV1pct', sixmwd -> 'Walk_m', mmrc -> 'mMRC', bmi -> 'BMI' Results summary — shows many rows computed successfully (non-NA) per output column. Example: bode_index(): results: bode_index 30/30, ... verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") active: Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) "none".
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  df_v <- data.frame(   FEV1pct = c(68, 45, 30),   Walk_m  = c(400, 280, 140),   mMRC    = c(1, 2, 3),   BMI     = c(24, 19, 18) ) bode_index(   df_v,   col_map = list(fev1_pct = "FEV1pct", sixmwd = "Walk_m", mmrc = "mMRC", bmi = "BMI"),   verbose = TRUE ) #> bode_index(): reading input 'df_v' — 3 rows × 4 variables #> bode_index(): col_map (5 columns — 4 specified, 1 inferred from data) #>   fev1_pct          ->  'FEV1pct' #>   sixmwd            ->  'Walk_m' #>   mmrc              ->  'mMRC' #>   bmi               ->  'BMI' #>   fev1_pp           ->  'FEV1pct'    (inferred) #> bode_index(): computing markers: #>   bode_index  [0-10 COPD severity score] #> bode_index(): results: bode_index 3/3, fev1_pct 3/3, fev1_score 3/3, walk_score 3/3, mmrc_score 3/3, bmi_score 3/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 6 #>   bode_index fev1_pct fev1_score walk_score mmrc_score bmi_score #>        <int>    <dbl>      <int>      <int>      <int>     <int> #> 1          0       68          0          0          0         0 #> 2          5       45          2          1          1         1 #> 3          9       30          3          3          2         1  options(old_opt)

text: spirometry_markers() broader spirometry-derived metrics. pulmo_markers() additional pulmonary markers.

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: Compute OSTA, ALMI, FMI, BMD_Tscore DXA anthropometry, optionally pass bone-turnover markers (TBS, HSA, PINP, CTX, BSAP, Osteocalcin). Inputs coerced numeric; non-finite become NA. BMD_Tscore uses supplied reference mean SD; SD must positive.

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: DXA outputs (ALM, FM, BMD) plus age, weight, height, reference BMD stats. want OSTA mass indexes (ALMI/FMI) alongside BMD T-scores. may additional bone markers pass unchanged.

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: Packages: HealthMarkers, dplyr (display). Required columns: age, weight, height (meters), ALM, FM (kg), BMD (g/cm^2), BMD_ref_mean, BMD_ref_sd (positive). Optional pass-: TBS, HSA, PINP, CTX, BSAP, Osteocalcin (coerced numeric mapped present). Row policy via na_action: keep (default), omit, error missing/non-finite required inputs.

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: simulated data lack DXA values; create simple placeholders illustration ; replace measurements.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)  # Illustrative stand-ins; replace with real DXA outputs sim_small$ALM <- pmax(12, pmin(30, sim_small$weight * 0.32)) sim_small$FM  <- pmax(8,  pmin(40, sim_small$weight * 0.28)) sim_small$BMD <- 0.9 + (sim_small$age - mean(sim_small$age, na.rm = TRUE)) * 0.001 sim_small$BMD_ref_mean <- 1.0 sim_small$BMD_ref_sd   <- 0.12

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: Required keys plus optional markers (commented ).
code: col_map <- list(   age = "age",   weight = "weight",   height = "height",   ALM = "ALM",   FM = "FM",   BMD = "BMD",   BMD_ref_mean = "BMD_ref_mean",   BMD_ref_sd   = "BMD_ref_sd"   # Optional: TBS = "TBS", HSA = "HSA", PINP = "PINP", CTX = "CTX", BSAP = "BSAP", Osteocalcin = "Osteocalcin" )

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: Defaults keep rows missing inputs return NA derived scores.
code: bm_out <- bone_markers(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep" ) #> bone_markers(): reading input 'sim_small' — 30 rows × 519 variables #> bone_markers(): col_map (13 columns — 8 specified, 5 inferred from data) #>   age               ->  'age' #>   weight            ->  'weight' #>   height            ->  'height' #>   ALM               ->  'ALM' #>   FM                ->  'FM' #>   BMD               ->  'BMD' #>   BMD_ref_mean      ->  'BMD_ref_mean' #>   BMD_ref_sd        ->  'BMD_ref_sd' #>   TBS               ->  'TBS'    (inferred) #>   PINP              ->  'PINP'    (inferred) #>   CTX               ->  'CTX'    (inferred) #>   BSAP              ->  'BSAP'    (inferred) #>   Osteocalcin       ->  'Osteocalcin'    (inferred) #> bone_markers(): computing markers: #>   OSTA       [(weight - age) * 0.2] #>   ALMI       [ALM / height^2] #>   FMI        [FM / height^2] #>   BMD_Tscore [(BMD - ref_mean) / ref_sd] #> bone_markers(): results: id 30/30, OSTA 30/30, ALMI 30/30, FMI 30/30, BMD_Tscore 30/30, TBS 30/30, HSA 0/30, PINP 30/30, CTX 30/30, BSAP 30/30, Osteocalcin 30/30  new_cols <- setdiff(names(bm_out), names(sim_small)) head(select(bm_out, all_of(new_cols))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>    OSTA     ALMI      FMI BMD_Tscore   HSA #>   <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>      <dbl> <dbl> #> 1  7.56 0.000958 0.000897     -0.799    NA #> 2 12.1  0.000903 0.000832     -1.00     NA #> 3  9.4  0.000865 0.000757     -1.00     NA #> 4  7.74 0.000840 0.000735     -0.919    NA #> 5 -4.02 0.000772 0.000676     -0.642    NA #> 6  1.5  0.00117  0.00103      -0.631    NA

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: col_map: required mappings age, weight, height, ALM, FM, BMD, BMD_ref_mean, BMD_ref_sd; optional TBS/HSA/PINP/CTX/BSAP/Osteocalcin pass mapped. na_action: keep (default, retain rows; derived scores become NA inputs missing), omit (drop rows missing/non-finite required inputs), error (abort missing/non-finite required inputs).

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: Non-numeric inputs coerced; NA introduced warned. Non-finite become NA. BMD_ref_sd height must positive function aborts. Row policy: keep vs omit vs error governed na_action.

age) * 0.2] #>   ALMI       [ALM / height^2] #>   FMI        [FM / height^2] #>   BMD_Tscore [(BMD - ref_mean) / ref_sd] #> bone_markers(): results: OSTA 8/8, ALMI 6/8, FMI 8/8, BMD_Tscore 8/8, TBS 0/8, HSA 0/8, PINP 0/8, CTX 0/8, BSAP 0/8, Osteocalcin 0/8 a_omit <- bone_markers(demo, col_map, na_action = "omit") #> bone_markers(): reading input 'demo' — 8 rows × 8 variables #> bone_markers(): col_map (8 columns — 8 specified) #>   age               ->  'age' #>   weight            ->  'weight' #>   height            ->  'height' #>   ALM               ->  'ALM' #>   FM                ->  'FM' #>   BMD               ->  'BMD' #>   BMD_ref_mean      ->  'BMD_ref_mean' #>   BMD_ref_sd        ->  'BMD_ref_sd' #> bone_markers(): computing markers: #>   OSTA       [(weight - age) * 0.2] #>   ALMI       [ALM / height^2] #>   FMI        [FM / height^2] #>   BMD_Tscore [(BMD - ref_mean) / ref_sd] #> bone_markers(): results: OSTA 6/6, ALMI 6/6, FMI 6/6, BMD_Tscore 6/6, TBS 0/6, HSA 0/6, PINP 0/6, CTX 0/6, BSAP 0/6, Osteocalcin 0/6  list(   keep_rows = nrow(a_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(a_omit),   preview = head(select(a_keep, OSTA, ALMI, FMI, BMD_Tscore)) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 8 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 6 #>  #> $preview #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>    OSTA      ALMI      FMI BMD_Tscore #>   <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1  7.56  0.000958 0.000897     -0.799 #> 2 12.1  NA        0.000832     -1.00  #> 3  9.4   0.000865 0.000757     -1.00  #> 4  7.74  0.000840 0.000735     -0.919 #> 5 -4.02 NA        0.000676     -0.642 #> 6  1.5   0.00117  0.00103      -0.631

text: Supply optional keys (TBS, HSA, PINP, CTX, BSAP, Osteocalcin) col_map include output; unmapped optional columns return NA.
code: demo2 <- demo demo2$PINP <- rnorm(nrow(demo2), mean = 55, sd = 20) col_map_opt <- c(col_map, list(PINP = "PINP"))  bm_with_opt <- bone_markers(   data = demo2,   col_map = col_map_opt,   na_action = "keep" ) #> bone_markers(): reading input 'demo2' — 8 rows × 9 variables #> bone_markers(): col_map (9 columns — 9 specified) #>   age               ->  'age' #>   weight            ->  'weight' #>   height            ->  'height' #>   ALM               ->  'ALM' #>   FM                ->  'FM' #>   BMD               ->  'BMD' #>   BMD_ref_mean      ->  'BMD_ref_mean' #>   BMD_ref_sd        ->  'BMD_ref_sd' #>   PINP              ->  'PINP' #> bone_markers(): computing markers: #>   OSTA       [(weight - age) * 0.2] #>   ALMI       [ALM / height^2] #>   FMI        [FM / height^2] #>   BMD_Tscore [(BMD - ref_mean) / ref_sd] #> bone_markers(): results: OSTA 8/8, ALMI 6/8, FMI 8/8, BMD_Tscore 8/8, TBS 0/8, HSA 0/8, PINP 8/8, CTX 0/8, BSAP 0/8, Osteocalcin 0/8  head(select(bm_with_opt, OSTA, ALMI, FMI, BMD_Tscore, PINP)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>    OSTA      ALMI      FMI BMD_Tscore  PINP #>   <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>      <dbl> <dbl> #> 1  7.56  0.000958 0.000897     -0.799 27.0  #> 2 12.1  NA        0.000832     -1.00  60.1  #> 3  9.4   0.000865 0.000757     -1.00   6.25 #> 4  7.74  0.000840 0.000735     -0.919 54.9  #> 5 -4.02 NA        0.000676     -0.642 67.4  #> 6  1.5   0.00117  0.00103      -0.631 78.0

text: Ensure BMD_ref_sd > 0 height > 0; otherwise function aborts row filtering. Replace placeholder ALM/FM/BMD values actual DXA measurements; keep units consistent (height meters; masses kg; BMD g/cm^2). Optional markers numeric-coerced; missing mapped, return NA columns.

text: Manual check: OSTA age 70 weight 60 (60 - 70) * 0.2 = -2. ALMI equal ALM / height^2; FMI equals FM / height^2. BMD_Tscore drop ~1 BMD one SD reference.

title: Bone Turnover Markers
text: sarc_f_score() alm_bmi_index() sarcopenia-related assessments. frax_score() fracture risk estimation.

title: Calculate SDS
text: Standardize selected variables SDS (z-scores) using supplied reference means SDs. Supports row-retention policies, extreme SDS handling, optional diagnostic list output.

title: Calculate SDS
text: need z-scores one variables using chosen reference. want explicit control missing-row handling extreme SDS treatment. may need diagnostic list summarizing missing extreme counts.

title: Calculate SDS
text: Packages: HealthMarkers, dplyr (display). Data columns: variables listed vars must exist numeric/coercible; id_col optional. Reference table ref: columns variable, mean, sd; one finite mean positive sd per var. Row policy: na_strategy = keep (default), omit, error. Extreme policy: check_extreme + extreme_strategy (cap/warn/error/NA) sds_cap threshold.

title: Calculate SDS
text: Build small example reference BMI systolic BP; replace variables reference stats.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30) %>%   dplyr::select(id, BMI, sbp)  vars <- c("BMI", "sbp") ref <- tibble::tibble(   variable = vars,   mean = c(mean(sim_small$BMI, na.rm = TRUE), mean(sim_small$sbp, na.rm = TRUE)),   sd   = c(sd(sim_small$BMI, na.rm = TRUE),   sd(sim_small$sbp, na.rm = TRUE)) )

title: Calculate SDS
text: Defaults keep rows missing inputs return NA SDS cells.
code: sds_tbl <- calc_sds(   data = sim_small,   vars = vars,   ref = ref,   id_col = "id",   na_strategy = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_strategy = "cap",   sds_cap = 6,   verbose = FALSE )  new_cols <- setdiff(names(sds_tbl), names(sim_small)) head(select(sds_tbl, id, all_of(new_cols))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   id    BMI_sds sbp_sds #>   <chr>   <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 P001    0.696  -1.66  #> 2 P002    0.279   0.482 #> 3 P003   -0.209  -1.06  #> 4 P004   -0.354  -0.765 #> 5 P005   -0.734   0.732 #> 6 P006    1.53   -0.915

title: Calculate SDS
text: vars: character vector columns standardize; must present unique. ref: table variable/mean/sd; one row per var; sd must positive. na_strategy: keep (retain rows, NA SDS), omit (drop rows missing vars), error (abort missing vars). check_extreme + extreme_strategy: cap/warn/error/NA |SDS| > sds_cap; cap applied symmetrically. return: data (default tibble) list (adds summary warning strings). id_col: optional ID diagnostics .

title: Calculate SDS
text: Non-numeric vars coerced numeric warnings; non-finite become NA. Bypass vs drop vs fail controlled na_strategy.

title: Calculate SDS
code: demo <- sim_small[1:8, ] demo$BMI[3] <- NA  a_keep <- calc_sds(demo, vars, ref, na_strategy = "keep") a_omit <- calc_sds(demo, vars, ref, na_strategy = "omit")  list(   keep_rows = nrow(a_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(a_omit),   preview = head(select(a_keep, BMI_sds, sbp_sds)) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 8 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 7 #>  #> $preview #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   BMI_sds sbp_sds #>     <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1   0.696  -1.66  #> 2   0.279   0.482 #> 3  NA      -1.06  #> 4  -0.354  -0.765 #> 5  -0.734   0.732 #> 6   1.53   -0.915

title: Calculate SDS
text: Cap, warn, error, set NA SDS beyond chosen limit.
code: demo2 <- demo demo2$sbp[5] <- 500  # extreme SBP  a_cap <- calc_sds(   data = demo2,   vars = vars,   ref = ref,   na_strategy = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_strategy = "cap",   sds_cap = 4 )  head(select(a_cap, BMI_sds, sbp_sds)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   BMI_sds sbp_sds #>     <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1   0.696  -1.66  #> 2   0.279   0.482 #> 3  NA      -1.06  #> 4  -0.354  -0.765 #> 5  -0.734   4     #> 6   1.53   -0.915

title: Calculate SDS
text: Request per-variable missing/extreme counts warnings.
code: sds_list <- calc_sds(   data = sim_small,   vars = vars,   ref = ref,   na_strategy = "keep",   check_extreme = TRUE,   extreme_strategy = "warn",   sds_cap = 5,   return = "list",   verbose = FALSE )  str(sds_list$summary) #> List of 5 #>  $ rows_in      : int 30 #>  $ rows_out     : int 30 #>  $ omitted_rows : int 0 #>  $ total_extreme: int 0 #>  $ per_var      :'data.frame':   2 obs. of  3 variables: #>   ..$ variable : chr [1:2] "BMI" "sbp" #>   ..$ n_missing: int [1:2] 0 0 #>   ..$ n_extreme: int [1:2] 0 0 unique(sds_list$warnings) #> character(0)

title: Calculate SDS
text: Added _sds columns (tibble default) Optional list: data, summary (row counts, omitted rows, extremes, per-variable missing/extreme), warnings Rows drop na_strategy = “omit” na_strategy = “error” aborts.

title: Calculate SDS
text: ref must align vars use units; sd must positive finite. Lower sds_cap limit leverage outliers plots; use extreme_strategy = “error” strict QC. Provide id_col make troubleshooting easier reviewing diagnostics.

title: Calculate SDS
text: Manual check: mean BMI 25 sd 4, BMI 29 yield (29-25)/4 = 1.0. Confirm rows missing BMI retained vs dropped according na_strategy. Verify capping clamps |SDS| sds_cap symmetrically.

title: Calculate SDS
text: Set verbose = TRUE emit three structured messages per call: Preparing inputs — start--function signal. Column map — lists variable passed via vars. Example: calc_sds(): column map: BMI -> 'BMI', sbp -> 'sbp' Results summary — shows many rows computed successfully (non-NA) per _sds output column. Example: calc_sds(): results: BMI_sds 28/30, sbp_sds 30/30, ... verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") active: Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) "none".
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  ref_v <- data.frame(variable = c("BMI", "sbp"), mean = c(25, 120), sd = c(4, 15)) df_v  <- data.frame(id = 1:3, BMI = c(24, 29, 30), sbp = c(118, 121, 135)) calc_sds(   data = df_v,   vars = c("BMI", "sbp"),   ref  = ref_v,   id_col = "id",   na_strategy = "keep",   extreme_strategy = "cap",   sds_cap = 6,   verbose = TRUE ) #> calc_sds: starting on 3 row(s), 2 variable(s) (id: id) #> calc_sds(): col_map: BMI -> 'BMI', sbp -> 'sbp' #> Computing SDS for 2 variable(s)... #> calc_sds: completed #> calc_sds(): results: BMI_sds 3/3, sbp_sds 3/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 5 #>      id   BMI   sbp BMI_sds sbp_sds #>   <int> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1     1    24   118   -0.25 -0.133  #> 2     2    29   121    1     0.0667 #> 3     3    30   135    1.25  1  options(old_opt)

title: Calculate SDS
text: infer_cols() infer data types prior scoring. calc_sds() used helpers; pair health_summary() broader panels.

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
text: Compute 19-condition Charlson Comorbidity Index (CCI) without age points. Handles binary indicators, optional bounds screening, NA policies.

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index

code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)  # Illustrative binary flags set.seed(123) sim_small <- sim_small %>% mutate(   mi = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.1),   chf = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.1),   pvd = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.1),   stroke = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.1),   dementia = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.05),   copd = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.15),   rheum = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.05),   ulcer = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.05),   mild_liver = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.05),   diabetes = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.2),   diab_comp = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.05),   hemiplegia = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.02),   renal = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.08),   cancer = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.08),   leukemia = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.01),   lymphoma = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.01),   sev_liver = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.02),   metastatic_cancer = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.01),   hiv = rbinom(n(), 1, 0.01) )

title: Charlson Comorbidity Index
text: Set verbose = TRUE emit three structured messages per call: Preparing inputs — start--function signal. Column map — confirms data column comorbidity key resolved . Example: charlson_index(): column map: mi -> 'mi', chf -> 'chf', ... Results summary — shows many rows computed successfully (non-NA) per output column. code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  patient <- tibble::tibble(   mi=0, chf=0, pvd=0, stroke=0, dementia=0, copd=0, rheum=0, ulcer=0,   mild_liver=0, diabetes=1, diab_comp=1, hemiplegia=0, renal=1,   cancer=0, leukemia=0, lymphoma=0, sev_liver=0, metastatic_cancer=0, hiv=0 ) cm_v <- as.list(stats::setNames(names(patient), names(patient))) charlson_index(patient, col_map = cm_v, verbose = TRUE) #> charlson_index(): reading input 'patient' — 1 rows × 19 variables #> charlson_index(): col_map: mi -> 'mi', chf -> 'chf', pvd -> 'pvd', stroke -> 'stroke', dementia -> 'dementia', copd -> 'copd', rheum -> 'rheum', ulcer -> 'ulcer', mild_liver -> 'mild_liver', diabetes -> 'diabetes', diab_comp -> 'diab_comp', hemiplegia -> 'hemiplegia', renal -> 'renal', cancer -> 'cancer', leukemia -> 'leukemia', lymphoma -> 'lymphoma', sev_liver -> 'sev_liver', metastatic_cancer -> 'metastatic_cancer', hiv -> 'hiv' #> charlson_index(): computing markers: #>   charlson_index [mi, chf, pvd, stroke, dementia, copd, rheum, ulcer, mild_liver, diabetes, diab_comp, hemiplegia, renal, cancer, leukemia, lymphoma, sev_liver, metastatic_cancer, hiv] #> charlson_index(): results: charlson_index 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   charlson_index #>            <int> #> 1              4  options(old_opt)

code: library(HealthMarkers) library(dplyr)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)  set.seed(123) # Illustrative labs (mg/dL Ca, g/dL Alb) sim_small$Ca <- pmax(7.5, pmin(11.5, rnorm(nrow(sim_small), 9.2, 0.6))) sim_small$Alb <- pmax(2.5, pmin(5.0, rnorm(nrow(sim_small), 4.0, 0.4)))

title: Corrected Calcium
text: Prefer explicit units; auto mode may warn convert SI inferred. Ensure Ca Alb units match chosen mode; mismatched units mis-scale results. Downstream consumers expecting mg/dL keep units = “conventional” convert explicitly.

title: Corrected Calcium
code: # Payne formula spot check (conventional): Ca = 9.0, Alb = 3.0 => 9.0 + 0.8*(4-3) = 9.8 v1 <- corrected_calcium(data.frame(Ca = 9.0, Alb = 3.0), col_map, units = "conventional") stopifnot(abs(v1$corrected_calcium - 9.8) < 1e-10)  # SI round-trip: Ca = 2.25 mmol/L, Alb = 35 g/L # working: Ca_mg = 2.25*4 = 9.0, Alb_gdl = 35/10 = 3.5 # corrected_mg = 9.0 + 0.8*(4 - 3.5) = 9.4; corrected_mmol = 9.4/4 = 2.35 v2 <- corrected_calcium(data.frame(Ca = 2.25, Alb = 35), col_map, units = "si") stopifnot(abs(v2$corrected_calcium - 2.35) < 1e-10)  cat("Payne conventional:", v1$corrected_calcium, "mg/dL\n") #> Payne conventional: 9.8 mg/dL cat("Payne SI round-trip:", v2$corrected_calcium, "mmol/L\n") #> Payne SI round-trip: 2.35 mmol/L

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: Compute indices display newly calculated columns (full input data). preview shows newly computed indices. _inv columns inverted larger negatives indicate greater insulin resistance; QUICKI, Raynaud, Isi_basal decrease resistance worsens. rows retained na_action = "keep".
code: fis <- fasting_is(   data = sim_small,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   normalize = "none",   verbose = FALSE )  fis_new <- setdiff(names(fis), names(sim_small)) head(dplyr::select(fis, dplyr::all_of(fis_new))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 10 #>   Fasting_inv Raynaud HOMA_IR_inv  FIRI QUICKI Belfiore_basal Ig_ratio_basal #>         <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>          <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1      -11.4     3.52       -57.1  51.4  0.140        0.00156        -0.101  #> 2       -4.25    9.41       -18.9  17.0  0.165        0.00470        -0.0425 #> 3       -9.77    4.10       -42.1  37.9  0.146        0.00211        -0.101  #> 4       -8.43    4.74       -34.5  31.0  0.150        0.00258        -0.0917 #> 5      -14.6     2.75       -44.3  39.9  0.145        0.00200        -0.212  #> 6       -7.88    5.07       -28.7  25.8  0.155        0.00309        -0.0963 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: Isi_basal <dbl>, Bennett <dbl>, HOMA_IR_rev_inv <dbl>

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: Fasting_inv: inverted fasting insulin (higher negative = higher insulin) Raynaud: 40 / insulin HOMA_IR_inv, HOMA_IR_rev_inv: inverted HOMA formulations FIRI: fasting insulin resistance index QUICKI: insulin sensitivity index (higher = sensitive) Belfiore_basal: 2 / (insulin x glucose + 1) Ig_ratio_basal: -insulin/glucose (inverted) Isi_basal: 10000 / (glucose x insulin) Bennett: 1 / (log insulin x log glucose)

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: Required columns (map via col_map): G0 glucose (mmol/L), I0 insulin (pmol/L). Internal conversions: glucose x18 -> mg/dL; insulin /6 -> muU/mL. Missingness: na_action controls row handling (keep/omit/error). Normalization: normalize = "none" default; options apply columnwise scaling computation.

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: Show row handling behaves tiny slice. na_action = "keep" preserves six rows (NA-derived outputs row missing G0), na_action = "omit" drops row.
code: demo <- sim_small[1:6, c("G0", "I0")] demo$G0[2] <- NA  fis_keep <- fasting_is(demo, col_map, na_action = "keep") fis_omit <- fasting_is(demo, col_map, na_action = "omit")  list(   keep_rows = nrow(fis_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(fis_omit),   keep_head = head(dplyr::select(fis_keep, dplyr::all_of(fis_new))) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 6 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 5 #>  #> $keep_head #> # A tibble: 6 × 10 #>   Fasting_inv Raynaud HOMA_IR_inv  FIRI QUICKI Belfiore_basal Ig_ratio_basal #>         <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>          <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1      -11.4     3.52       -57.1  51.4  0.140        0.00156        -0.101  #> 2       -4.25    9.41        NA    NA   NA           NA              NA      #> 3       -9.77    4.10       -42.1  37.9  0.146        0.00211        -0.101  #> 4       -8.43    4.74       -34.5  31.0  0.150        0.00258        -0.0917 #> 5      -14.6     2.75       -44.3  39.9  0.145        0.00200        -0.212  #> 6       -7.88    5.07       -28.7  25.8  0.155        0.00309        -0.0963 #> # ℹ 3 more variables: Isi_basal <dbl>, Bennett <dbl>, HOMA_IR_rev_inv <dbl>

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: required inputs must mapped present; missing mappings columns abort. na_action: keep leaves NA-derived outputs; omit drops rows; error stops missing input. normalize optional post-hoc scaling; set "none" preserve native scales.

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: Set verbose = TRUE emit three structured messages per call: Preparing inputs — start--function signal. Column map — confirms data column required key resolved . Example: fasting_is(): column map: G0 -> 'G0', I0 -> 'I0' Results summary — shows many rows computed successfully (non-NA) per output column. Example: fasting_is(): results: Fasting_inv 28/30, HOMA_IR_inv 28/30, ... verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") active: Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) "none".
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  invisible(fasting_is(   data    = sim_small[1:5, ],   col_map = col_map,   verbose = TRUE )) #> fasting_is(): reading input 'data' — 5 rows × 519 variables #> fasting_is(): col_map (2 columns — 2 specified) #>   G0                ->  'G0' #>   I0                ->  'I0' #> fasting_is(): computing markers: #>   Fasting_inv          [G0, I0] #>   Raynaud              [G0, I0] #>   HOMA_IR_inv          [G0, I0] #>   FIRI                 [G0, I0] #>   QUICKI               [G0, I0] #>   Belfiore_basal       [G0, I0] #>   Ig_ratio_basal       [G0, I0] #>   Isi_basal            [G0, I0] #>   Bennett              [G0, I0] #>   HOMA_IR_rev_inv      [G0, I0] #> fasting_is(): results: id 5/5, Fasting_inv 5/5, Raynaud 5/5, HOMA_IR_inv 5/5, FIRI 5/5, QUICKI 5/5, Belfiore_basal 5/5, Ig_ratio_basal 5/5, Isi_basal 5/5, Bennett 5/5, HOMA_IR_rev_inv 5/5  options(old_opt)

title: Fasting insulin sensitivity indices
text: Non-numeric inputs coerced numeric; coercion introduces NAs triggers warning. pipelines, prefer na_action = "omit" "error" avoid silent gaps; use "keep" exploratory summaries. Set normalize align scales across indices feeding distance-based models; leave "none" interpretability. See ?fasting_is full argument reference diagnostics.

title: Frailty index
text: Compute deficit-based frailty index (FI) via di::di() HealthMarkers validation, NA/extreme handling, optional rescaling, tidy output. default, numeric columns auto-selected deficits cols = NULL (excluding age present). Higher FI indicates deficits (roughly 0–1).

title: Frailty index
text: deficit variables (binary/logical [0,1]-scaled) need standard FI quickly. want guardrails: NA policy, --range screening/capping, optional rescaling FI computation. plan consume FI tibble (return = “data”) keep original di::di list (return = “list”).

title: Frailty index
text: Dependency: suggested package di must installed; examples calls skipped/stop otherwise. Deficits: supply via cols (character vector) allow auto-selection numeric columns cols = NULL (age excluded provided). Optional age: kept plotting/bins excluded deficit selection. na_action: ignore/warn/error (legacy) plus aliases keep (like ignore) omit (drops rows missing selected deficit). rescale: default TRUE; rescales numeric deficits [0,1] FI computation. return: list (di object) data (tibble di plus selected deficits [+ age]).

title: Frailty index
text: examples run di package installed. Expected inputs: deficits ideally scaled [0,1] (logical/binary). age optional; present excluded auto-selected deficits.
code: library(HealthMarkers)  has_di <- requireNamespace("di", quietly = TRUE)  demo_df <- data.frame(   age = c(70, 75, 80, 78, 74, 69, 72, 77),   d1  = c(0, 1, 1, 0, 0, 1, 0, 1),   d2  = c(0.2, 0.8, 1.0, 0.4, 0.1, 0.9, 0.0, 0.7),   d3  = c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE, TRUE) )

title: Frailty index
text: can supply cols let function auto-select numeric deficits cols = NULL. name explicitly keep age optional plotting.

title: Frailty index
text: Return tidy tibble (return = "data") can preview FI alongside deficits. FI ranges 0 (deficits) toward 1 (deficits present). rescale = TRUE allows non-binary numeric inputs; na_action = "keep" retains rows even deficits contain NA (none slice).
code: if (has_di) {   fi_tbl <- frailty_index(     data = demo_df,     cols = cols,     age = "age",     rescale = TRUE,     na_action = "keep",     return = "data",     verbose = FALSE   )    head(dplyr::select(fi_tbl, di, dplyr::all_of(cols))) } else {   message("Install the 'di' package to run the examples.") } #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>       di    d1    d2 d3    #>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <lgl> #> 1 0.4        0   0.2 TRUE  #> 2 0.6        1   0.8 FALSE #> 3 1          1   1   TRUE  #> 4 0.467      0   0.4 TRUE  #> 5 0.0333     0   0.1 FALSE #> 6 0.967      1   0.9 TRUE

title: Frailty index
text: Deficits: binary/logical [0,1]-scaled; supply via data cols (let auto-select numeric deficits cols = NULL). age (optional): excluded auto-selected deficits; useful plotting/bins. na_action: keep/ignore retain rows missing deficits; omit drops rows; error stops missing selected deficit; warn emits warnings. return: data yields tibble (di + selected deficits [+ age]); list returns di::di object.

title: Frailty index
text: Demonstrate row dropping, capping, tidy return. na_action = "keep", rows remain (missing deficits propagate FI). na_action = "omit", rows missing selected deficit dropped.
code: if (has_di) {   demo_miss <- demo_df   demo_miss$d2[3] <- NA       # introduce missing   demo_miss$d1[2] <- 1.2      # out-of-range; rescale will normalise it    fi_keep <- frailty_index(     data = demo_miss,     cols = cols,     age = "age",     rescale = TRUE,     na_action = "keep",     return = "data",     verbose = FALSE   )    fi_omit <- frailty_index(     data = demo_miss,     cols = cols,     age = "age",     rescale = TRUE,     na_action = "omit",     return = "data",     verbose = FALSE   )    list(     keep_rows = nrow(fi_keep),     omit_rows = nrow(fi_omit),     keep_example = head(dplyr::select(fi_keep, di, dplyr::all_of(cols)))   ) } else {   message("Install the 'di' package to run the examples.") } #> $keep_rows #> [1] 8 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 7 #>  #> $keep_example #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>       di    d1    d2 d3    #>    <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <lgl> #> 1 0.407    0     0.2 TRUE  #> 2 0.630    1.2   0.8 FALSE #> 3 0.917    1    NA   TRUE  #> 4 0.481    0     0.4 TRUE  #> 5 0.0370   0     0.1 FALSE #> 6 0.944    1     0.9 TRUE

title: Frailty index
text: di package must installed; otherwise calls stop (examples skip missing). Provide deficits via cols rely auto-selection numeric columns (age excluded present). Choose na_action control row retention vs. dropping missing deficits. return = "data" gives tibble (di + selected deficits [+ age]); return = "list" returns raw di::di output.

title: Frailty index
text: Set verbose = TRUE emit structured messages. function uses cols vector rather named col_map, column map message lists selected deficit columns directly: Preparing inputs — row count, deficit count, whether age provided. Column map — lists selected deficit column names. Example: frailty_index(): column map: d1, d2, d3 Results summary (return = "data") — shows many rows di non-NA. Example: frailty_index(): results: di 8/8 return = "list", range FI vector reported instead. verbose = TRUE emits "inform" level; also need options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") active: Reset options(healthmarkers.verbose = NULL) "none".
code: if (has_di) {   old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")    invisible(frailty_index(     data    = demo_df,     cols    = cols,     age     = "age",     return  = "data",     verbose = TRUE   ))    options(old_opt) } else {   message("Install the 'di' package to run the examples.") } #> frailty_index(): preparing inputs (8 rows, 3 deficits, age provided) #> frailty_index(): col_map: d1, d2, d3 #> frailty_index(): results: di 8/8

title: Frailty index
text: Keep deficits close [0,1]; rescale = TRUE normalises mild deviations FI computation. Choose na_action = "omit" "error" stricter pipelines; keep/ignore exploratory summaries. return = "data" pairs FI selected deficits quick binding downstream tibbles. Set verbose = TRUE see selected deficits, NA scan counts, completion range.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Compute simplified, non-validated approximation FRAX 10-year fracture risk (major osteoporotic hip). Normalizes sex labels, applies HM-CS missing/extreme policies, returns placeholder risks; proprietary FRAX algorithm.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: need placeholder fracture-risk proxy demos pipelines acknowledging clinical FRAX. want explicit NA extreme-value handling (age/BMD bounds) column mapping flexibility. plan swap real FRAX later; keeps interfaces stable meantime.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Required columns (map via col_map): age (years), sex (codes starting m/f 1/0). Optional binary risk factors: prior fracture, parent fracture, glucocorticoids, rheumatoid, secondary osteoporosis, current smoker, high alcohol. Optional: femoral neck BMD T-score (bmd). na_action: keep/ignore/warn retain rows NA outputs; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Illustrative demo data ; replace cohort.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Provide column names; left-hand keys fixed.
code: col_map <- list(   age = "Age",   sex = "Sex",   prior_fracture  = "prior_fx",   parent_fracture = "parent_fx",   steroids        = "steroids",   rheumatoid      = "rheumatoid",   secondary_op    = "secondary",   smoker          = "smoker",   alcohol         = "alcohol",   bmd             = "bmd" )

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: age: age years (required). sex: sex code; first letter m/f 1/0 (required; normalized male/female). Optional binary risk factors: prior fracture, parent fracture, glucocorticoids, rheumatoid, secondary osteoporosis, current smoker, high alcohol. bmd: femoral neck T-score (optional); placeholder adjustment present.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Return placeholder risks normalized age/sex/BMD used. Interpretation: frax_major_percent frax_hip_percent placeholder probabilities (clinical). frax_sex_norm shows normalized sex; frax_age_used frax_bmd_tscore reflect capping/blanking applied extreme checks.
code: frax_out <- frax_score(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE )  head(dplyr::select(frax_out, frax_major_percent, frax_hip_percent, frax_sex_norm, frax_age_used, frax_bmd_tscore)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   frax_major_percent frax_hip_percent frax_sex_norm frax_age_used #>                <dbl>            <dbl> <chr>                 <dbl> #> 1               15.5              8.5 female                   65 #> 2               14.4              8.9 male                     72 #> 3               19               13.3 female                   80 #> 4               10.4              6   female                   68 #> 5               11.8              7.6 male                     74 #> 6                7                3.5 female                   60 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: frax_bmd_tscore <dbl>

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Required: age (years) sex codes mapped col_map. Optional: binary risk factors BMD T-score. Non-numeric inputs coerced; non-finite values become NA. na_action: keep/ignore/warn retain rows NA outputs; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Show row handling small slice. na_action = "keep" retains rows even missing/unknown sex age (outputs become NA). na_action = "omit" drops rows missing required inputs.
code: demo_miss <- demo demo_miss$Age[3] <- NA      # missing age demo_miss$Sex[4] <- "X"    # unknown sex -> NA  keep_res <- frax_score(   data = demo_miss,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE )  omit_res <- frax_score(   data = demo_miss,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "omit",   verbose = FALSE )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(keep_res),   omit_rows = nrow(omit_res),   preview = head(dplyr::select(keep_res, frax_age_used, frax_bmd_tscore, frax_major_percent, frax_hip_percent)) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 6 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 4 #>  #> $preview #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   frax_age_used frax_bmd_tscore frax_major_percent frax_hip_percent #>           <dbl>           <dbl>              <dbl>            <dbl> #> 1            65            -2                 15.5              8.5 #> 2            72            -1                 14.4              8.9 #> 3            NA            -2.5               NA               NA   #> 4            68            -1.5               NA               NA   #> 5            74            -0.8               11.8              7.6 #> 6            60            NA                  7                3.5

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Placeholder approximation ; validated FRAX calculator clinical use. must map age sex; missing mappings columns abort. Choose na_action enforce relax completeness; error strict, omit drops, keep retains. verbose = TRUE surfaces progress QA messages.

title: FRAX fracture risk
text: Set verbose = TRUE (healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") surface three structured messages call: preparing inputs, column map, results summary.
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") df_v <- data.frame(Age = c(65, 72), Sex = c("F", "M"),                    bmd = c(-2.0, -1.0)) frax_score(   df_v,   col_map = list(age = "Age", sex = "Sex", bmd = "bmd"),   verbose = TRUE ) #> frax_score(): reading input 'df_v' — 2 rows × 3 variables #> frax_score(): col_map (3 columns — 2 specified, 1 inferred from data) #>   age               ->  'Age' #>   sex               ->  'Sex' #>   bmd_t             ->  'bmd'    (inferred) #> frax_score(): computing markers: #>   frax_major_percent [age, sex, risk factors, bmd] #>   frax_hip_percent [age, sex, risk factors, bmd] #> frax_score(): results: frax_major_percent 2/2, frax_hip_percent 2/2, frax_sex_norm 2/2, frax_age_used 2/2, frax_bmd_tscore 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   frax_major_percent frax_hip_percent frax_sex_norm frax_age_used #>                <dbl>            <dbl> <chr>                 <dbl> #> 1                8.5              4.7 female                   65 #> 2                5.4              3.3 male                     72 #> # ℹ 1 more variable: frax_bmd_tscore <dbl> options(old_opt)
