title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: want quick tour core helpers all_health_markers() orchestrator. need see column inference vs explicit mapping data. prefer ready--run code snippets (optional dependencies required examples).

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Many clinical/metabolic markers: glycemic, lipid, renal, inflammatory, liver, anthropometric/obesity, atherogenic indices, frailty/Charlson, sweat/urine panels, micronutrients, pulmonary indices, . Single-purpose helpers (e.g., cvd_marker_aip(), sweat_markers(), fasting_is()) plus all_health_markers() run multiple groups . Column mapping diagnostics hm_col_report() override via col_map labels differ. Light missing-data utilities (impute_missing()) prep data computing markers.

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Targeted helper: cvd_marker_aip(sim_small) renal_markers(sim_small). Multiple groups: all_health_markers(sim_small, = c("glycemic", "lipid")). Everything: all_health_markers(sim_small, = "") (wide output; filter afterward). Insulin sensitivity/resistance: fasting_is(), ogtt_is(), adipo_is(), include insulin groups all_health_markers() col_map glucose/insulin.

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: all_health_markers(data, = ...) — run multiple groups markers . cvd_marker_aip(data, col_map = list(TG = "TG", HDL_c = "HDL_c")) — atherogenic index plasma. renal_markers(data) — renal eGFR-related outputs. glycemic_markers(data) — glycemic metrics; pair fasting_is() / ogtt_is() / adipo_is() insulin indices. sweat_markers(data) urine_markers(data) — alternate biofluid panels.

what: Load the package and simulated data
title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Inputs: package plus bundled RDS file inst/extdata/simulated_hm_data.rds. Outputs: two data frames—sim (full) sim_small (first 30 rows fast examples).
code: if (!requireNamespace("HealthMarkers", quietly = TRUE)) {   if (requireNamespace("pkgload", quietly = TRUE)) {     pkgload::load_all("..")   } else {     stop("Install HealthMarkers (or pkgload for development) before knitting.", call. = FALSE)   } } library(HealthMarkers)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path)  # Work with a small subset using slice_head sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)  dim(sim_small) #> [1]  30 519

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers

what: Data readiness: columns at a glance

text: functions infer columns automatically. names differ, provide col_map. Typical defaults:

previous_headings: Data readiness: columns at a glance
title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Call hm_col_report() running computation see full column-mapping report data: report shows: - key — internal name functions use - data_column — matched column data - matched — exact, case-insensitive, col contains synonym, synonym contains col - ready--paste col_map template keys found Column inference usually enough; data use different labels, provide col_map override.
code: # See which columns are matched and how — no computation performed hm_col_report(my_data)  # Show all 258 keys, including unmatched ones hm_col_report(my_data, show_unmatched = TRUE)  # Capture the matched col_map to pass directly to any function cm <- hm_col_report(my_data, verbose = FALSE) all_health_markers(my_data, col_map = cm)

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Input: data explicit column aliases. : provides TG/HDL names compute AIP columns differ. Output: tibble AIP result.
code: lipid_cols <- list(TG = "TG", HDL_c = "HDL_c")  aip <- cvd_marker_aip(sim_small, col_map = lipid_cols, na_action = "keep") aip_new <- setdiff(names(aip), names(sim_small)) head(dplyr::select(aip, dplyr::all_of(aip_new))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   model   value #>   <chr>   <dbl> #> 1 AIP    0.384  #> 2 AIP   -0.229  #> 3 AIP   -0.0710 #> 4 AIP   -0.204  #> 5 AIP    0.0134 #> 6 AIP   -0.118

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: functions accept na_action. shows outputs differ required inputs missing. na_action aliases: "ignore" backward-compatible alias "keep" (behavior; retained older code continues work). "warn" also alias "keep" additionally emits missingness warning. reading another function’s help page see "ignore" listed first choices, behaves identically "keep". want impute computing markers:
code: missing_demo <- sim_small[1:5, c("TG", "HDL_c")] missing_demo$TG[2] <- NA  # Keep rows (produces NA where inputs are missing) cvd_marker_aip(missing_demo, col_map = lipid_cols, na_action = "keep") #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   model   value #>   <chr>   <dbl> #> 1 AIP    0.384  #> 2 AIP   NA      #> 3 AIP   -0.0710 #> 4 AIP   -0.204  #> 5 AIP    0.0134  # Drop rows with required NA cvd_marker_aip(missing_demo, col_map = lipid_cols, na_action = "omit") #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   model   value #>   <chr>   <dbl> #> 1 AIP    0.384  #> 2 AIP   -0.0710 #> 3 AIP   -0.204  #> 4 AIP    0.0134 imputed <- impute_missing(sim_small, method = "mice", m = 1, maxit = 3) head(imputed)

what: Imputation options (what happens under the hood)
title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: impute_missing() preprocessing helper; marker functions impute internally. Run first, call calculators. method = "mice": multiple imputation chained equations via mice; control m (imputations) maxit (iterations). method = "missForest": nonparametric random-forest imputation via missForest; good mixed data types. method = "mean" | "median" | "constant": simple deterministic fills numeric columns. Output: data frame imputed values replacing missing entries; pass all_health_markers() helper (e.g., cvd_marker_aip(), renal_markers()).

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Compute sweat markers; uses auto-inferred column names verbose NA diagnostics.
code: sweat <- sweat_markers(sim_small, verbose = FALSE) sweat_new <- setdiff(names(sweat), names(sim_small)) head(dplyr::select(sweat, dplyr::all_of(sweat_new))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   Na_K_ratio sweat_rate #>        <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1       5.63    0.00347 #> 2       8.22    0.0199  #> 3       3.49    0.00640 #> 4       7.12    0.00572 #> 5       5.74    0.00194 #> 6       7.96    0.00710

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers

code: # Provide only required columns; optional columns are absent glyc_min <- sim_small[, c("HDL_c", "TG", "BMI")]  # verbose = TRUE lists which optional markers were skipped glyc_out <- glycemic_markers(glyc_min, verbose = TRUE)

text: Input: sim_small; groups: glycemic, lipid, renal, inflammatory. Output: tibble markers (first rows shown).

title: Getting Started with HealthMarkers
text: Input: sim_small; output: urine markers rows containing required inputs.
code: urine <- urine_markers(sim_small, na_action = "omit") urine_new <- setdiff(names(urine), names(sim_small)) head(dplyr::select(urine, dplyr::all_of(urine_new))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>   albuminuria_stage microalbuminuria  UPCR U_Na_K_ratio NGAL_per_gCr #>   <fct>             <fct>            <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 A3                normal            694.        1.45         1633. #> 2 A3                normal            958.        1.8          2156. #> 3 A3                normal           1905.        0.598        5536. #> 4 A3                normal           3559.        2.12         3360. #> 5 A3                normal           3575.        1.23         4402. #> 6 A3                normal           2873.        2.59         2818. #> # ℹ 6 more variables: KIM1_per_gCr <dbl>, NAG_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   Beta2Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, A1Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, IL18_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   L_FABP_per_gCr <dbl>

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Compute SPISE, METS_IR, prediabetes/diabetes flags (HbA1c), HOMA_CP, LAR, ASI, TyG_index fasting labs anthropometry. Required inputs: HDL_c (mmol/L), TG (mmol/L), BMI (kg/m^2); optional glucose/HbA1c/C-peptide/insulin/leptin/adiponectin present. Uses packaged simulated data runnable examples.

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: fasting lipids BMI want quick insulin resistance/sensitivity proxies. can supply glucose/HbA1c/C-peptide/insulin/adipokines available enrich outputs. need explicit NA handling.

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Required (mapped via col_map): HDL_c (mmol/L), TG (mmol/L), BMI (kg/m^2). Optional: glucose, HbA1c, C_peptide, G0, I0, leptin, adiponectin. Unit notes: TyG converts TG mg/dL (x88.57) glucose mg/dL (x18); HOMA_CP uses (G0 * (C_peptide/6)) / 22.5. na_action: keep/ignore/warn retain rows NA outputs; omit drops rows missing used inputs; error aborts.

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Use small subset simulated data (30-50 rows). Swap sim_small data frame practice.
code: library(HealthMarkers)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Map required optional inputs column names; left-hand keys fixed.

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: HDL_c: HDL cholesterol (mmol/L) required TG: triglycerides (mmol/L) required BMI: body mass index (kg/m^2) required glucose: fasting glucose (mmol/L) HbA1c: hemoglobin A1c (mmol/mol) C_peptide: C-peptide (pmol/L) G0: fasting glucose (mmol/L) HOMA_CP I0: fasting insulin (pmol/L) ASI leptin: leptin (ng/mL) adiponectin: adiponectin (ng/mL)
code: col_map <- list(   HDL_c = "HDL_c",   TG = "TG",   BMI = "BMI",   glucose = "glucose",   HbA1c = "HbA1c",   C_peptide = "C_peptide",   G0 = "G0",   I0 = "I0",   leptin = "leptin",   adiponectin = "adiponectin" )

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Compute markers show newly created columns. Interpretation: SPISE METS_IR summarize insulin sensitivity/resistance; TyG_index rises higher TG/glucose; HOMA_CP approximates insulin resistance via C-peptide glucose; LAR ASI relate adipokines insulin; HbA1c flags populate prediabetes/diabetes available. Rows retained na_action = "keep".
code: gm <- glycemic_markers(   data     = sim_small,   col_map  = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   verbose  = FALSE )  gm_new <- setdiff(names(gm), names(sim_small)) head(dplyr::select(gm, dplyr::all_of(gm_new))) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   SPISE METS_IR prediabetes HOMA_CP   LAR   ASI TyG_index #>   <dbl>   <dbl>       <int>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  4.85    336.           0      NA    NA    NA      9.67 #> 2  7.11    369.           1      NA    NA    NA      8.07 #> 3  7.51    289.           0      NA    NA    NA      8.43 #> 4  8.29    164.           0      NA    NA    NA      8.23 #> 5  8.38    116.           0      NA    NA    NA      8.51 #> 6  5.09    165.           0      NA    NA    NA      8.44

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Required: HDL_c (mmol/L), TG (mmol/L), BMI (kg/m^2). Auto-derived: BMI weight (kg) + height (m/cm) BMI absent. Optional present/mapped: glucose, HbA1c, C_peptide, G0, I0, leptin, adiponectin. Auto-derived: glucose G0 (vice versa) one absent. TyG converts TG mg/dL (88.57) glucose mg/dL (18) internally. HOMA_CP uses (G0 * (C_peptide/6)) / 22.5; verify units C-peptide. Missingness: na_action controls row handling (keep/omit/error/warn/ignore).

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Providing weight height instead BMI works automatically: Similarly, providing G0 without glucose enables METS_IR TyG_index via alias:
code: df_no_bmi <- data.frame(   HDL_c   = c(1.0, 1.2),   TG      = c(1.3, 1.5),   weight  = c(70, 85),    # kg   height  = c(175, 180),  # cm   glucose = c(5.5, 6.0) ) gm_precomp <- glycemic_markers(df_no_bmi, verbose = FALSE) gm_precomp$SPISE  # computed via auto-derived BMI #> [1] 8.649504 7.229832 df_g0_only <- data.frame(   HDL_c = 1.2, TG = 1.3, BMI = 24, G0 = 5.5 ) gm_alias <- glycemic_markers(df_g0_only, verbose = FALSE) c(METS_IR = gm_alias$METS_IR, TyG_index = gm_alias$TyG_index) #>   METS_IR TyG_index  #> 330.35250   8.64813

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: data frame uses different column names (e.g. pglu0 instead G0), pass col_map redirects . function materialises alias runs pre-computation normally. Similarly, map weight / height differently-named anthropometric columns BMI derived automatically:
code: # Data has 'pglu0' instead of 'G0' df_mapped <- data.frame(   HDL_c = 1.2, TG = 1.3, BMI = 24.0, pglu0 = 5.5 ) gm_mapped <- glycemic_markers(   df_mapped,   col_map = list(G0 = "pglu0"),   # only need to specify what differs   verbose = FALSE ) c(METS_IR = gm_mapped$METS_IR, TyG_index = gm_mapped$TyG_index) #>   METS_IR TyG_index  #> 330.35250   8.64813 df_wh <- data.frame(   HDL_c = 1.1, TG = 1.4, wt_kg = 80, ht_cm = 175, glucose = 5.8 ) gm_wh <- glycemic_markers(   df_wh,   col_map = list(weight = "wt_kg", height = "ht_cm"),   verbose = FALSE ) gm_wh$SPISE   # BMI was derived from wt_kg / ht_cm #> [1] 7.254687

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: need specify keys differ dictionary defaults. key omit, glycemic_markers() auto-infers mapping common synonym patterns (e.g. hdlc, hdl_cholesterol, hdl.c resolve HDL_c). Provide partial col_map just non-standard entries:
code: # Column names match common synonyms — no col_map needed for those df_syn <- data.frame(   hdlc = 1.0, trig = 1.3, bmi = 24.0, pglu0 = 5.5 ) gm_syn <- glycemic_markers(   df_syn,   col_map = list(G0 = "pglu0"),   # only override the non-obvious one   verbose = FALSE ) c(SPISE = gm_syn$SPISE, METS_IR = gm_syn$METS_IR, TyG_index = gm_syn$TyG_index) #>     SPISE   METS_IR TyG_index  #>   8.10289        NA   8.64813

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Show row handling small slice. na_action = "keep" preserves rows (missing inputs flow NA outputs); na_action = "omit" drops rows missing/invalid used inputs.
code: demo <- sim_small[1:6, c("HDL_c", "TG", "BMI")] demo$glucose   <- c(5.5, 6.2, 7.0, 5.8, 6.0, 5.4) demo$HbA1c     <- c(42, NA, 41, 45, 44, 40) demo$C_peptide <- c(300, 450, 500, 400, 380, 360) demo$G0        <- demo$glucose demo$I0        <- c(60, 90, 120, 80, 75, 70) demo$leptin    <- c(10, 12, 15, 11, 9, 13) demo$adiponectin <- c(8, 7, 6, 9, 10, 7)  cm2 <- list(   HDL_c = "HDL_c", TG = "TG", BMI = "BMI",   glucose = "glucose", HbA1c = "HbA1c",   C_peptide = "C_peptide", G0 = "G0", I0 = "I0",   leptin = "leptin", adiponectin = "adiponectin" )  gm_keep <- glycemic_markers(data = demo, col_map = cm2, na_action = "keep",  verbose = FALSE) gm_omit <- glycemic_markers(data = demo, col_map = cm2, na_action = "omit",  verbose = FALSE)  list(keep_rows = nrow(gm_keep), omit_rows = nrow(gm_omit)) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 6 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 5

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Required inputs must present/mapped derivable (BMI weight/height); missing required columns abort. na_action governs whether missing/invalid inputs drop rows, warn, error. Optional columns drive optional markers; absent inputs yield NA markers. Range checks informational — values outside physiological plausibility ranges noted verbose output altered.

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Set verbose = TRUE (default) surface five structured messages: column mapping, optional inputs (present/missing indices affect), pre-computation actions, full list markers computed inputs, per-column results summary.
code: df_v <- data.frame(HDL_c = 1.0, TG = 1.3, BMI = 24, glucose = 5.6) glycem_out <- glycemic_markers(   df_v,   col_map = list(HDL_c = "HDL_c", TG = "TG", BMI = "BMI", glucose = "glucose"),   verbose = TRUE ) #> glycemic_markers(): reading input 'df_v' — 1 rows × 4 variables #> glycemic_markers(): col_map (5 columns — 4 specified, 1 inferred from data) #>   HDL_c             ->  'HDL_c' #>   TG                ->  'TG' #>   BMI               ->  'BMI' #>   glucose           ->  'glucose' #>   G0                ->  'glucose'    (inferred) #> glycemic_markers(): optional inputs #>   present:  glucose, G0 #>   missing:  HbA1c, C_peptide, I0, leptin, adiponectin #>   indices -> NA: #>   prediabetes -> NA  [missing: HbA1c] #>   diabetes -> NA  [missing: HbA1c] #>   HOMA_CP -> NA  [missing: C_peptide] #>   LAR -> NA  [missing: leptin, adiponectin] #>   ASI -> NA  [missing: adiponectin, I0] #> glycemic_markers(): computing markers: #>   SPISE           [HDL_c, TG, BMI] #>   METS_IR         [glucose, TG, BMI, HDL_c] #>   prediabetes     NA [HbA1c missing] #>   diabetes        NA [HbA1c missing] #>   HOMA_CP         NA [C_peptide/G0 missing] #>   LAR             NA [leptin/adiponectin missing] #>   ASI             NA [adiponectin/I0 missing] #>   TyG_index       [TG, glucose] #> glycemic_markers(): results: SPISE 1/1, METS_IR 0/1, prediabetes 0/1, diabetes 0/1, HOMA_CP 0/1, LAR 0/1, ASI 0/1, TyG_index 1/1

title: Glycemic and metabolic markers
text: Start fasting lipids BMI (weight/height); add glucose/HbA1c/C-peptide/insulin/adipokines richer markers. Provide weight height instead BMI BMI directly available — computed automatically. Convert units beforehand data expected mmol/L/pmol/L/ng/mL units. stricter pipelines, use na_action = "omit" "error"; keep/warn exploratory work. Use verbose = TRUE (default) detailed output messages diagnostics. See ?glycemic_markers full argument details marker definitions.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: Guide three aggregators: - all_insulin_indices(): fasting/OGTT/adipose/tracer insulin sensitivity/resistance panels. - metabolic_markers(): selectable bundle (insulin, adiposity_sds, cardio, lipid, liver, glycemic, MetS) appended data. - all_health_markers(): registry-driven dispatcher across marker groups optional column inference insulin inclusion. wrappers use guards skip missing inputs/optional deps instead crashing verbose = FALSE.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: want one call run multiple marker groups, optional insulin panels. need column inference default can supply col_map nonstandard names. prefer graceful skipping (hard errors) inputs optional packages absent.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: data: data.frame/tibble columns required groups select. col_map: optional mapping; recommended insulin/lipid groups nonstandard names. : groups compute. metabolic_markers() supports insulin, adiposity_sds, cardio, lipid, liver, glycemic, mets. all_health_markers() covers larger registry (lipid, renal, inflammatory, liver, obesity, insulin panels, etc.). include_insulin: whether add insulin panels all_health_markers(); requires glucose/insulin columns. na_action: keep/omit/error (plus ignore/warn supported) forwarded underlying helpers. Optional dependencies: groups rely suggested packages (e.g., CVD risk backends). Missing deps skipped NA outputs.

title: Health marker aggregators

code: library(HealthMarkers)  sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim <- readRDS(sim_path) sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(sim, n = 30)

text: groups infer columns automatically. Provide col_map names differ using insulin panels.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: TG, HDL_c, LDL_c, TC: lipid inputs (mmol/L) BMI: body mass index (kg/m^2) G0, I0: fasting glucose (mmol/L) insulin (pmol/L) insulin panels ALT, AST: liver enzymes (U/L) age, sex: demographics used several groups
code: col_map <- list(   TG = "TG",   HDL_c = "HDL_c",   LDL_c = "LDL_c",   TC = "TC",   BMI = "BMI",   G0 = "G0",   I0 = "I0",   ALT = "ALT",   AST = "AST",   age = "age",   sex = "sex" )

title: Health marker aggregators
text: Run metabolic_markers() small subset, focusing glycemic, lipid, liver groups (insulin panel excluded keep inputs minimal). Show new columns. Interpretation: outputs include lipid ratios, TyG-based metrics, liver-derived markers. Rows retained na_action = "keep" propagates missing inputs NA outputs.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: all_health_markers() can auto-infer columns use col_map. request multi-panel set skip insulin minimal inputs. Verbose mode reports inferred/user mappings groups computed skipped. Skipped groups leave input untouched.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: Include insulin indices alongside small panel. Missing OGTT/adipose/tracer inputs skipped safely; fasting indices compute G0/I0 present. verbose summary note insulin sub-panels ran (fasting) skipped due missing OGTT/adipose/tracer inputs.

title: Health marker aggregators
text: na_action controls row retention. Demonstrate tiny slice containing missing lipid value. keep propagates missing inputs NA markers; omit drops rows required-field NAs selected groups.
code: demo <- sim_small[1:6, c("TG", "HDL_c", "BMI", "ALT", "AST")] demo$TG[2] <- NA  mm_keep <- metabolic_markers(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   which = c("glycemic", "lipid", "liver"),   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE )  mm_omit <- metabolic_markers(   data = demo,   col_map = col_map,   which = c("glycemic", "lipid", "liver"),   na_action = "omit",   verbose = FALSE )  list(   keep_rows = nrow(mm_keep),   omit_rows = nrow(mm_omit) ) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 6 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 6

title: Health marker aggregators
text: Required inputs chosen groups must present/mapped; missing required columns abort skip depending underlying helper. na_action chooses whether missing inputs lead retained rows (keep), dropped rows (omit), immediate errors (error). normalize mode affect insulin outputs; ignored groups use . all_health_markers() infers columns col_map missing/NULL; inference fails required keys found. Verbose mode summarizes mappings plus computed/skipped groups can audit run.

"LDL_c", BMI = "BMI") all_health_markers(   df_v, col_v,   which = c("lipid"),   include_insulin = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE ) #> all_health_markers(): preparing inputs #> all_health_markers(): mapping title: Hormone marker ratios
text: Compute nine hormone ratios built-QA: FAI, LH_FSH, E2_P, T3_T4, ARR, Ins_Glu, GH_IGF1, PRL_T, CAR_slope. Inputs coerced numeric, non-finite become NA, optional extreme screening can warn, cap, blank, error --range labs.

title: Hormone marker ratios
text: complete hormone panel (mapped via col_map) need quick ratio features. want explicit NA handling optional extreme-value screening avoid crashes bad labs. need outputs preserve row counts unless explicitly omit missing rows.

title: Hormone marker ratios
text: keys required: total testosterone, SHBG, LH, FSH, estradiol, progesterone, free T3, free T4, aldosterone, renin, insulin, glucagon, GH, IGF1, prolactin, cortisol_0, cortisol_30. col_map: map required key column names; missing mappings columns abort. Units: keep units consistent within ratio (e.g., cortisol nmol/L; aldosterone/renin matching units). na_action: keep/ignore retain rows NA outputs; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts.

title: Hormone marker ratios
text: Use small synthetic tibble (covers required inputs) chunk runs anywhere. Replace horm_demo lab data.

title: Hormone marker ratios
text: Map every required key column names; mandatory computation. total_testosterone, SHBG LH, FSH estradiol, progesterone free_T3, free_T4 aldosterone, renin insulin, glucagon GH, IGF1 prolactin cortisol_0, cortisol_30 (nmol/L; slope uses 30-minute delta / 30)
code: col_map <- list(   total_testosterone = "tt",   SHBG = "shbg",   LH = "lh",   FSH = "fsh",   estradiol = "e2",   progesterone = "p4",   free_T3 = "ft3",   free_T4 = "ft4",   aldosterone = "aldo",   renin = "renin",   insulin = "ins",   glucagon = "gluc",   GH = "gh",   IGF1 = "igf1",   prolactin = "prl",   cortisol_0 = "cort0",   cortisol_30 = "cort30" )

title: Hormone marker ratios
text: Compute ratios preview outputs. Non-finite values set NA; rows retained na_action = "keep"/"ignore".

title: Hormone marker ratios
text: Show difference keeping rows (propagating NA) omitting rows required inputs missing.
code: horm_demo_na <- horm_demo horm_demo_na$ft3[2] <- NA  keep_res <- hormone_markers(   data = horm_demo_na,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep" )  omit_res <- hormone_markers(   data = horm_demo_na,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "omit" )  list(keep_rows = nrow(keep_res), omit_rows = nrow(omit_res)) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 4 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 3

title: Hormone marker ratios
text: Extreme labs produce extreme ratios. Pre-filter implausible values calling.
code: horm_demo_ext <- horm_demo horm_demo_ext$aldo[1] <- 2000  # intentionally extreme # Pre-filter or cap before calling horm_demo_ext$aldo[horm_demo_ext$aldo > 1000] <- 1000 head(hormone_markers(horm_demo_ext, col_map = col_map, na_action = "keep")[, c("ARR", "FAI")]) #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>     ARR   FAI #>   <dbl> <dbl> #> 1  83.3  16.7 #> 2  12    17.5 #> 3  12.7  16.1 #> 4  14.4  17.7

title: Hormone marker ratios
text: required inputs must mapped present; missing mappings/columns abort. na_action: keep/ignore retains rows NA outputs; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error stops missingness. Outputs numeric ratios; divisions zero/non-finite converted NA.
