'cortisol_30' #> hormone_markers(): optional inputs #>   present:  total_testosterone, SHBG, LH, FSH, estradiol, progesterone, free_T3, free_T4, aldosterone, renin, insulin, glucagon, GH, IGF1, prolactin, cortisol_0, cortisol_30 #>   missing:  TSH #>   ratios skipped (missing inputs): TSH_fT4 #> hormone_markers(): computing markers: #>   FAI          [total_testosterone, SHBG] #>   LH_FSH       [LH, FSH] #>   E2_P         [estradiol, progesterone] #>   E2_T         [estradiol, total_testosterone] #>   T3_T4        [free_T3, free_T4] #>   TSH_fT4      NA [missing: TSH] #>   ARR          [aldosterone, renin] #>   Ins_Glu      [insulin, glucagon] #>   GH_IGF1      [GH, IGF1] #>   PRL_T        [prolactin, total_testosterone] #>   CAR_slope    [cortisol_0, cortisol_30] #> hormone_markers(): results: FAI 1/1, LH_FSH 1/1, E2_P 1/1, E2_T 1/1, T3_T4 1/1, ARR 1/1, Ins_Glu 1/1, GH_IGF1 1/1, PRL_T 1/1, CAR_slope 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 10 #>     FAI LH_FSH  E2_P  E2_T T3_T4   ARR Ins_Glu GH_IGF1 PRL_T CAR_slope #>   <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1   500      2     2    10   0.5     5       2     0.5   1.2         2 options(old_opt)

title: Hormone marker ratios
text: Run hm_col_report(your_data) check hormone assay columns auto-detected building col_map. See Multi-Biobank Compatibility article recognised synonyms across major biobanks.
code: hm_col_report(your_data)

title: Hormone marker ratios
text: Keep units consistent across rows (e.g., cortisol nmol/L, aldosterone/renin matching units) make ratios meaningful. Use na_action = "omit" prefer complete-case ratios; keep/ignore want row counts preserved. Turn verbose = TRUE see coercion, missingness scan, handling summaries.

title: Imputing missing values
text: Three imputation helpers preparing data marker calculations: impute_missing() — fast deterministic imputation (mean, median, zero, constant). Operates column column numeric columns least one NA. impute_mice() — multiple imputation via mice::mice(). Requires mice package. impute_missforest() — random-forest imputation via missForest::missForest(). Requires missForest package. Falls back mean imputation forest fails. three: (1) leave non-numeric columns untouched, (2) respect optional cols argument limit columns imputed, (3) warn high-missingness columns, (4) emit structured progress messages verbose = TRUE.

title: Imputing missing values
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   glucose  = c(5.2, NA, 6.0, 5.8, NA),   insulin  = c(60,  80, NA,  90, 110),   age      = c(45,  52, 38,  60,  NA),   sex      = c("M", "F", "M", "F", "M")   # non-numeric; never touched )

title: Imputing missing values
text: Replace NAs column--column column mean. sex untouched.
code: out_mean <- impute_missing(df, method = "mean") out_mean #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   glucose insulin   age sex   #>     <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <chr> #> 1    5.2       60  45   M     #> 2    5.67      80  52   F     #> 3    6         85  38   M     #> 4    5.8       90  60   F     #> 5    5.67     110  48.8 M

title: Imputing missing values
code: # Median out_med <- suppressWarnings(impute_missing(df, method = "median")) # Zero out_zero <- suppressWarnings(impute_missing(df, method = "zero", cols = "glucose")) # Constant out_const <- suppressWarnings(impute_missing(df, method = "constant", constant = -1))  list(   median_glucose  = out_med$glucose,   zero_glucose    = out_zero$glucose,   const_glucose   = out_const$glucose ) #> $median_glucose #> [1] 5.2 5.8 6.0 5.8 5.8 #>  #> $zero_glucose #> [1] 5.2 0.0 6.0 5.8 0.0 #>  #> $const_glucose #> [1]  5.2 -1.0  6.0  5.8 -1.0

title: Imputing missing values
code: # Only impute insulin; leave glucose NA out_sel <- suppressWarnings(impute_missing(df, method = "mean", cols = "insulin")) list(glucose = out_sel$glucose, insulin = out_sel$insulin) #> $glucose #> [1] 5.2  NA 6.0 5.8  NA #>  #> $insulin #> [1]  60  80  85  90 110

title: Imputing missing values
text: Requires least two numeric columns missing values. Returns one completed dataset m imputations.
code: if (requireNamespace("mice", quietly = TRUE)) {   out_mice <- suppressWarnings(impute_mice(df, m = 5))   print(out_mice) } else {   message("mice package not installed; skipping example.") } #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   glucose insulin   age sex   #>     <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <chr> #> 1     5.2      60    45 M     #> 2    NA        80    52 F     #> 3     6        80    38 M     #> 4     5.8      90    60 F     #> 5    NA       110    45 M

title: Imputing missing values
text: missForest errors (e.g., unique values), mean-imputation fallback applied automatically.
code: if (requireNamespace("missForest", quietly = TRUE)) {   out_rf <- suppressWarnings(impute_missforest(df, ntree = 50))   print(out_rf) } else {   message("missForest package not installed; skipping example.") } #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   glucose insulin   age sex   #>     <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <chr> #> 1    5.2       60  45   M     #> 2    5.67      80  52   F     #> 3    6         85  38   M     #> 4    5.8       90  60   F     #> 5    5.67     110  48.8 M

title: Imputing missing values
text: three functions warn numeric column missing values na_warn_prop (default 0.2 = 20 %). warning carries class healthmarkers_impute_warn_high_missing.
code: df_hi <- tibble::tibble(x = c(1, NA, NA, NA), y = c(2, NA, 4, 5)) suppressWarnings(   tryCatch(     impute_missing(df_hi, method = "mean", verbose = FALSE),     warning = function(w) { message("High-missingness warning: ", conditionMessage(w)); NULL }   ) ) #> NULL

title: Imputing missing values
text: Set verbose = TRUE (healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") surface structured messages call: preparing inputs results summary. results message reports many values imputed per column: impute_missing(): results: imputed N values across M columns [col1=n1, col2=n2, ...]
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  df_v <- tibble::tibble(a = c(1, NA, 3), b = c(4, 5, NA), c = c(NA, 2, 2)) suppressWarnings(impute_missing(df_v, method = "mean", verbose = TRUE)) #> impute_missing(): preparing inputs (3 rows, 3 column(s), method='mean') #> impute_missing(): results: imputed 3 values across 3 columns [a=1, b=1, c=1] #> # A tibble: 3 × 3 #>       a     b     c #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1     1   4       2 #> 2     2   5       2 #> 3     3   4.5     2  options(old_opt)

title: Imputing missing values
text: Run imputation calling marker functions data-level NA handling insufficient (e.g., need complete matrix PCA multivariate ML upstream). Prefer impute_missing(method = "median") skewed biomarker distributions. impute_mice() impute_missforest() provide MAR-robust estimates cost speed; use final analysis datasets, exploratory runs. Non-numeric columns (factors, characters) always passed unchanged.

title: Inflammatory age (iAge proxy)
text: Compute simplified inflammatory age proxy (iAge) weighted sum CRP, IL6, TNFa. Supports NA policies high-missingness warnings. Units assumed: CRP mg/L; IL6, TNFa pg/mL (internal conversions).

title: Inflammatory age (iAge proxy)
text: iAge simplified implementation inflammatory age proxy described Sayed et al. (2021, Nature Aging). original model uses 50+ cytokine proteomics panel; function computes weighted sum three routinely available markers—CRP, IL-6, TNF-α—practical, single-site approximation. Elevated iAge scores associated : Biological age acceleration independent chronological age Increased frailty, functional decline, -cause mortality risk longitudinal cohorts Cardiometabolic neurodegenerative disease incidence Approximate reference ranges (healthy adults, standard lab units): - CRP < 3 mg/L (hs-CRP < 1 mg/L = low cardiovascular risk) - IL-6 < 7 pg/mL - TNF-α 1–6 pg/mL

title: Inflammatory age (iAge proxy)
text: Six participants spanning low markedly elevated inflammatory burden, one missing CRP demonstrate NA handling.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   CRP  = c(0.8, 1.5, 3.5,  8.0,   NA, 42.0), # mg/L   IL6  = c(1.2, 2.0, 4.1,  6.8,  3.2, 15.0), # pg/mL   TNFa = c(0.8, 1.0, 1.8,  2.5,  1.4,  3.2)  # pg/mL )

title: Inflammatory age (iAge proxy)
text: Map marker column names; three mandatory.
code: col_map <- list(CRP = "CRP", IL6 = "IL6", TNFa = "TNFa")

title: Inflammatory age (iAge proxy)
text: Default behavior (na_action = "omit"/ignore/warn): NAs ignored weighted sum (treated 0 contribution), preserving row count.
code: ia_default <- iAge(   data = df,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "omit",   verbose = FALSE )  ia_default #> # A tibble: 6 × 1 #>     iAge #>    <dbl> #> 1  0.932 #> 2  1.50  #> 3  3.12  #> 4  5.73  #> 5  1.53  #> 6 19.9

title: Inflammatory age (iAge proxy)
text: weighted sum (unstandardised) inflammatory markers, raw score influenced absolute magnitude CRP (dominates elevated). broad guide: cross-cohort comparison, normalise score normalize_vec() (method "z" "robust") downstream modelling.

title: Inflammatory age (iAge proxy)
text: na_action = "keep" returns NA required marker missing row. na_action = "error" stops missing required inputs.
code: ia_keep <- iAge(   data = df,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep" )  ia_keep #> # A tibble: 6 × 1 #>     iAge #>    <dbl> #> 1  0.932 #> 2  1.50  #> 3  3.12  #> 4  5.73  #> 5 NA     #> 6 19.9

title: Inflammatory age (iAge proxy)
text: Extreme marker values produce extreme scores. Pre-filter implausible values calling.
code: df_ext <- df df_ext$CRP[2] <- 500  # extreme on purpose # Pre-filter or cap before passing to iAge df_ext$CRP[df_ext$CRP > 300] <- 300  ia_filtered <- iAge(   data = df_ext,   col_map = col_map,   verbose = FALSE )  ia_filtered #> # A tibble: 6 × 1 #>      iAge #>     <dbl> #> 1   0.932 #> 2 100     #> 3   3.12  #> 4   5.73  #> 5  NA     #> 6  19.9

title: Inflammatory age (iAge proxy)
text: Default weights: CRP 0.33, IL6 0.33, TNFa 0.34 (sum 1). Named weights reordered CRP/IL6/TNFa. cohorts one marker informative, adjust weights (ensure sum 1):
code: iAge(df, col_map = col_map, weights = c(CRP = 0.5, IL6 = 0.25, TNFa = 0.25)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 1 #>    iAge #>   <dbl> #> 1  0.9  #> 2  1.5  #> 3  3.22 #> 4  6.32 #> 5 NA    #> 6 25.6

title: Inflammatory age (iAge proxy)
text: three markers must present numeric; coercion numeric error NAs introduced. omit/ignore/warn: missing markers contribute 0 sum; rows preserved. keep: missing required marker yields NA row. error: abort required marker missing/NA. Output: one-column tibble iAge; rows match input unless subset beforehand.

title: Inflammatory age (iAge proxy)
text: Set verbose = TRUE (healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") surface three structured messages call: preparing inputs, column map, results summary.
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") df_v <- tibble::tibble(CRP = 1.2, IL6 = 2.0, TNFa = 1.0) iAge(df_v, col_map = list(CRP = "CRP", IL6 = "IL6", TNFa = "TNFa"), verbose = TRUE) #> iAge(): reading input 'df_v' — 1 rows × 3 variables #> iAge(): col_map (3 columns — 3 specified) #>   CRP               ->  'CRP' #>   IL6               ->  'IL6' #>   TNFa              ->  'TNFa' #> iAge(): computing markers: #>   iAge  [weighted sum: CRP*0.33 + IL6*0.33 + TNFa*0.34] #> iAge(): results: iAge 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>    iAge #>   <dbl> #> 1  1.40 options(old_opt)

title: Inflammatory age (iAge proxy)
text: CRP widely available clinical databases (often labelled CRP, hs_CRP, hsCRP, c_reactive_protein). IL-6 TNF-α require targeted immunoassay panels (e.g., Meso Scale Discovery, Luminex) absent routine clinical extracts. datasets CRP available, pass single-marker col_map assign weight CRP: See Multi-Biobank Compatibility article recognised synonyms across major biobanks.
code: # Check which inflammation markers are present in your cohort data hm_col_report(your_data) iAge(   data    = your_data,   col_map = list(CRP = "hs_CRP"),  # IL6/TNFa omitted   weights = c(CRP = 1.0),          # full weight to CRP   na_action = "keep" )

title: Inflammatory age (iAge proxy)
text: Keep units consistent (CRP mg/L; IL6/TNFa pg/mL). Convert calling needed. Prefer na_action = "keep" want see rows incomplete; use omit want continuity score despite single missing markers. Use verbose = TRUE setup see mapping warnings. Impute upstream (e.g., impute_missing()) prefer -weight missing markers zero. Reference: Sayed N, et al. (2021). inflammatory aging clock (iAge) based deep learning tracks multimorbidity, immunosenescence, epigenetic reprogramming, mortality. Nature Aging, 1, 598–615. https://doi.org/10.1038/s43587-021-00082-y

title: Inflammatory markers
text: Compute inflammatory ratios/panels. Panels: - Classic: NLR, PLR, LMR, dNLR, SII, SIRI, AISI, CRP_category. - Eosinophil: NLR, PLR, LMR, NER, SII, SIRI, PIV, CLR, CAR, PCR, mGPS, ESR passthrough. - : union classic + eos metrics. Inputs coerced numeric; non-finite become NA. Zero denominators warn.

title: Inflammatory markers
text: Synthetic counts run panels.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   neutrophils = c(4, 2, 3.5),   lymphocytes = c(2, 1.0, 1.5),   monocytes = c(0.5, 0.3, 0.4),   platelets = c(200, 150, 180),   WBC = c(7, 4.5, 6),   CRP = c(2.5, 0.8, 12),   albumin = c(40, 42, 33),   eosinophils = c(0.2, 0.1, 0.15),   ESR = c(12, 15, 18) )

title: Inflammatory markers
text: Classic: neutrophils, lymphocytes (monocytes/platelets/WBC/CRP improve coverage). Eos: classic keys plus eosinophils, CRP, albumin (CLR/CAR/mGPS); ESR optional passthrough.
code: cm <- list(   neutrophils = "neutrophils",   lymphocytes = "lymphocytes",   monocytes = "monocytes",   platelets = "platelets",   WBC = "WBC",   CRP = "CRP",   albumin = "albumin",   eosinophils = "eosinophils",   ESR = "ESR" )

title: Inflammatory markers
code: classic_out <- inflammatory_markers(   data = df,   col_map = cm,   panel = "classic",   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE )  classic_out #> # A tibble: 3 × 8 #>     NLR   PLR   LMR  dNLR   SII  SIRI  AISI CRP_category #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <ord>        #> 1  2      100  4     1.33   400 1       200 moderate     #> 2  2      150  3.33  0.8    300 0.6      90 low          #> 3  2.33   120  3.75  1.4    420 0.933   168 high

title: Inflammatory markers
text: Extremely high CRP values propagate ratios; use na_action = "keep" exploratory review.
code: df_ext <- df df_ext$CRP[3] <- 400  # intentional extreme value  eos_out <- inflammatory_markers(   data = df_ext,   col_map = cm,   panel = "eos",   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE )  eos_out #> # A tibble: 3 × 12 #>     NLR   PLR   LMR   NER   SII  SIRI   PIV    CLR     CAR    PCR  mGPS   ESR #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl> <int> <dbl> #> 1  2      100  4     20     400 1       200   1.25  0.0625  80        0    12 #> 2  2      150  3.33  20     300 0.6      90   0.8   0.0190 188.       0    15 #> 3  2.33   120  3.75  23.3   420 0.933   168 267.   12.1      0.45     2    18

title: Inflammatory markers
text: na_action = "keep" preserves rows NA outputs; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts required input missing/non-finite.
code: df_na <- df df_na$lymphocytes[2] <- NA  keep_out <- inflammatory_markers(df_na, cm, panel = "classic", na_action = "keep") omit_out <- inflammatory_markers(df_na, cm, panel = "classic", na_action = "omit")  list(keep_rows = nrow(keep_out), omit_rows = nrow(omit_out)) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 3 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 2

title: Inflammatory markers
text: Provide required keys per panel; missing mappings abort. Inputs coerced numeric; non-finite become NA; zero denominators warn. Panel selection: auto uses eos eosinophils mapping present; returns union.

title: Inflammatory markers
text: Set verbose = TRUE (healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") surface three structured messages call: preparing inputs, column map, results summary.
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") df_v <- tibble::tibble(neutrophils = 4, lymphocytes = 2, monocytes = 0.5, platelets = 200, CRP = 2.5) cm_v <- list(neutrophils = "neutrophils", lymphocytes = "lymphocytes",              monocytes = "monocytes", platelets = "platelets", CRP = "CRP") inflammatory_markers(df_v, cm_v, panel = "classic", verbose = TRUE) #> inflammatory_markers(): reading input 'df_v' — 1 rows × 5 variables #> inflammatory_markers(): col_map (5 columns — 5 specified) #>   neutrophils       ->  'neutrophils' #>   lymphocytes       ->  'lymphocytes' #>   monocytes         ->  'monocytes' #>   platelets         ->  'platelets' #>   CRP               ->  'CRP' #> inflammatory_markers(): optional inputs (panel = classic) #>   present:  neutrophils, lymphocytes, monocytes, platelets, CRP #>   missing:  WBC, albumin, eosinophils, ESR #> inflammatory_markers(): computing markers: NLR, PLR, LMR, dNLR, SII, SIRI, AISI, CRP_category #> inflammatory_markers(): results: NLR 1/1, PLR 1/1, LMR 1/1, dNLR 0/1, SII 1/1, SIRI 1/1, AISI 1/1, CRP_category 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>     NLR   PLR   LMR  dNLR   SII  SIRI  AISI CRP_category #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <ord>        #> 1     2   100     4    NA   400     1   200 moderate options(old_opt)

title: Inflammatory markers
text: lymphocyte/eosinophil counts can zero, consider na_action = "keep" avoid dropping rows; zeros still trigger divide--zero warnings. Tighten ranges using capping match lab references. data use different units (e.g., CRP mg/dL), convert calling keep ratios interpretable.

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
text: 4-variable Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE) 2- 5-year ESKD risk. Includes NA policies, high-missingness warnings, verbose summaries. Units: age (years), sex coded 1=male/2=female, eGFR (mL/min/1.73 m^2), UACR (mg/g); unit conversion performed.

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
text: KFRE (Tangri et al., 2011, JAMA; validated 3-continent cohort) estimates 2- 5-year probability kidney failure (ESKD) four routine variables. now embedded KDIGO 2022 CKD guidelines risk stratification nephrology referral decisions. Outputs KFRE_2yr KFRE_5yr probabilities (0–1); multiply 100 percentages. equation developed adults CKD G3–G5; interpret cautiously outside range.

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  # Ten participants spanning CKD G3a → G5; mixed sex; UACR A1 → A3 df <- tibble::tibble(   age  = c(45, 52, 60, 68, 72, 55, 63, 75, 80, 48),   sex  = c(1,  2,  1,  2,  1,  2,  1,  2,  1,  2 ),   eGFR = c(58, 50, 42, 35, 28, 22, 18, 14, 10,  8 ),   UACR = c(25, 80, 200, 500, 800, 1200, 2500, 4500, 8000, 6000) )

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
code: col_map <- list(age = "age", sex = "sex", eGFR = "eGFR", UACR = "UACR")

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
text: Default na_action = "keep": missing inputs propagate NA risks; extreme checks.

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
text: Extreme values produce extreme risk estimates. Pre-filter implausible inputs calling.
code: # Pre-filter example df_filtered <- df df_filtered$eGFR[df_filtered$eGFR > 200] <- NA df_filtered$UACR[df_filtered$UACR > 10000] <- NA  kfre_filtered <- kidney_failure_risk(   data = df_filtered,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "warn",   verbose = FALSE )  kfre_filtered #> # A tibble: 10 × 2 #>    KFRE_2yr KFRE_5yr #>       <dbl>    <dbl> #>  1   0.0989    0.176 #>  2   0.125     0.219 #>  3   0.288     0.467 #>  4   0.326     0.519 #>  5   0.563     0.784 #>  6   0.515     0.739 #>  7   0.820     0.959 #>  8   0.836     0.965 #>  9   0.986     1.000 #> 10   0.922     0.991

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
text: keep propagates NA risks; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error stops missingness.
code: df_na <- df df_na$eGFR[2] <- NA  keep_out <- kidney_failure_risk(df_na, col_map, na_action = "keep") omit_out <- kidney_failure_risk(df_na, col_map, na_action = "omit")  list(keep_rows = nrow(keep_out), omit_rows = nrow(omit_out)) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 10 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 9

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
text: four predictors must mapped present; sex must 1/2. Inputs must numeric; non-finite become NA may propagate depending na_action. Output: tibble KFRE_2yr, KFRE_5yr; row count follows na_action row filtering apply.

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") kidney_failure_risk(   data    = df,   col_map = col_map,   verbose = TRUE ) #> kidney_failure_risk(): reading input 'df' — 10 rows × 4 variables #> kidney_failure_risk(): col_map: age -> 'age', sex -> 'sex', eGFR -> 'eGFR', UACR -> 'UACR' #> kidney_failure_risk(): computing markers: #>   KFRE_2yr  [age, sex, eGFR, UACR -- 2-year kidney failure risk] #>   KFRE_5yr  [age, sex, eGFR, UACR -- 5-year kidney failure risk] #> kidney_failure_risk(): results: KFRE_2yr 10/10, KFRE_5yr 10/10 #> # A tibble: 10 × 2 #>    KFRE_2yr KFRE_5yr #>       <dbl>    <dbl> #>  1   0.0989    0.176 #>  2   0.125     0.219 #>  3   0.288     0.467 #>  4   0.326     0.519 #>  5   0.563     0.784 #>  6   0.515     0.739 #>  7   0.820     0.959 #>  8   0.836     0.965 #>  9   0.986     1.000 #> 10   0.922     0.991 options(old_opt)

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
text: bundled simulated dataset includes eGFR UACR columns. Age sex also present, KFRE can run directly sim_small: Column recognition tip: biobank extracts eGFR UACR named differently (e.g., eGFR_CKD_EPI, ACR_mg_g), run hm_col_report(your_data) check auto-detected building col_map.
code: library(dplyr) sim_path <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(readRDS(sim_path), n = 50)  kfre_sim <- kidney_failure_risk(   data    = sim_small,   col_map = list(age = "age", sex = "sex", eGFR = "eGFR", UACR = "UACR"),   na_action   = "keep" )  dplyr::bind_cols(dplyr::select(sim_small, age, sex, eGFR, UACR), kfre_sim) |> head() #>    age sex eGFR UACR   id  KFRE_2yr  KFRE_5yr #> 1 62.6   1    1  5.0 P001 0.4044650 0.6177743 #> 2 38.0   1    1  4.8 P002 0.3661347 0.5708716 #> 3 38.0   1    1  9.5 P003 0.4622163 0.6836865 #> 4 48.3   1    1 26.6 P004 0.6466739 0.8549230 #> 5 81.5   2    1 16.8 P005 0.4736213 0.6960211 #> 6 82.8   2    1 13.2 P006 0.4387596 0.6576086

title: Kidney failure risk (KFRE)
text: Check units running (e.g., UACR mg/g vs mg/mmol); convert upstream needed. Use na_action = "error" strict QA; warn surface high missingness retaining rows.

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
text: Compute kynurenine/tryptophan ratio (KTR), marker IDO activity immune activation. Supports NA policies verbose progress. Inputs must already Kyn (nmol/L) Trp (µmol/L); unit conversion.

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
text: kynurenine/tryptophan ratio (KTR) quantifies activity indoleamine 2,3-dioxygenase 1 (IDO1), rate-limiting enzyme kynurenine pathway tryptophan catabolism. Elevated KTR reflects: Immune activation: IDO1 upregulated interferon-γ pro-inflammatory signals; high KTR indicates cell-mediated immune response. Immune escape: tumour cells exploit IDO1 suppress T-cell activity; KTR elevated many malignancies. Ageing inflammation: KTR rises biological age correlates inflammatory burden (CRP, IL-6). Formula: $\text{KTR} = \frac{\text{kynurenine (nmol/L)}}{\text{tryptophan (\mu mol/L)}}$ Approximate reference values healthy adults: Unit note: Kyn must nmol/L Trp µmol/L ratio yield values physiological range. resulting KTR unitless (nmol/µmol).

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
text: Row interpretation: rows 1–2 ≈ healthy adults; rows 3–4 ≈ sub-clinical inflammation; rows 5–6 ≈ chronic immune activation; rows 7–8 ≈ acute infection high-grade malignancy.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  # Eight participants: healthy → chronically ill → acute infection range df <- tibble::tibble(   Kyn_nM = c(1200, 1800, 2500, 3200, 4500, 8000, 15000, 25000),   Trp_uM = c(62,   50,   42,   38,   35,   28,   18,    12   ) )

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
code: col_map <- list(kynurenine = "Kyn_nM", tryptophan = "Trp_uM")

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
text: Default na_action = "keep": ratios NA inputs missing; extreme scan.
code: ktr <- kyn_trp_ratio(   data = df,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE )  ktr #> # A tibble: 8 × 1 #>   kyn_trp_ratio #>           <dbl> #> 1          19.4 #> 2          36   #> 3          59.5 #> 4          84.2 #> 5         129.  #> 6         286.  #> 7         833.  #> 8        2083.

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
text: Extreme Kyn Trp values produce extreme KTR. Pre-filter implausible inputs calling.
code: df_ext <- df df_ext$Kyn_nM[2] <- 50000  # extreme; pre-filter df_ext$Kyn_nM[df_ext$Kyn_nM > 20000] <- NA  ktr_filtered <- kyn_trp_ratio(   data = df_ext,   col_map = col_map,   na_action = "warn",   verbose = FALSE )  ktr_filtered #> # A tibble: 8 × 1 #>   kyn_trp_ratio #>           <dbl> #> 1          19.4 #> 2          NA   #> 3          59.5 #> 4          84.2 #> 5         129.  #> 6         286.  #> 7         833.  #> 8          NA

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
text: keep/ignore/warn retain rows NA ratios; omit drops rows missing inputs; error stops missingness.
code: df_na <- df df_na$Trp_uM[3] <- NA  keep_out <- kyn_trp_ratio(df_na, col_map, na_action = "keep") omit_out <- kyn_trp_ratio(df_na, col_map, na_action = "omit")  list(keep_rows = nrow(keep_out), omit_rows = nrow(omit_out)) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 8 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 7

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
text: markers must mapped present; non-numeric inputs coerced warnings NAs introduced. Trp must positive; nonpositive values set NA warning. Output: one-column tibble kyn_trp_ratio; row count follows na_action omit rows.

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") kyn_trp_ratio(   data    = df,   col_map = col_map,   verbose = TRUE ) #> kyn_trp_ratio(): reading input 'df' — 8 rows × 2 variables #> kyn_trp_ratio(): preparing inputs #> kyn_trp_ratio(): col_map (2 columns — 2 specified) #>   kynurenine        ->  'Kyn_nM' #>   tryptophan        ->  'Trp_uM' #> kyn_trp_ratio(): computing markers: #>   kyn_trp_ratio  [kynurenine (nmol/L) / tryptophan (mumol/L)] #> kyn_trp_ratio(): results: kyn_trp_ratio 8/8 #> # A tibble: 8 × 1 #>   kyn_trp_ratio #>           <dbl> #> 1          19.4 #> 2          36   #> 3          59.5 #> 4          84.2 #> 5         129.  #> 6         286.  #> 7         833.  #> 8        2083. options(old_opt)

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
text: Kynurenine tryptophan measured targeted HPLC mass-spectrometry panels. Column names vary widely across platforms. Use hm_col_report() check auto-detected dataset: See Multi-Biobank Compatibility article full list recognised synonyms (e.g., kynurenine_nmol_L, KYN, TRP, tryptophan_umol).
code: hm_col_report(your_data)  # shows matched keys and suggests col_map entries

title: Kynurenine/Tryptophan ratio
text: Re-check units ratios high (>100); Kyn nmol/L Trp µmol/L. Reference: Badawy AA-B (2019). Tryptophan metabolism: versatile area providing multiple targets pharmacological intervention. Egypt J Basic Clin Pharmacol. https://doi.org/10.32527/2019/101415 modeling, consider scaling/transforming KTR separately computation.

title: lipid_markers
text: Compute lipid ratios composite indices (non-HDL-C, remnant-C, TC/HDL, TG/HDL, LDL/HDL, ApoB/ApoA1, VAI, LAP, TyG-BMI) optional LDL estimation NA/extreme handling.

title: lipid_markers
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  sim <- readRDS(system.file("extdata/simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers")) lipids <- sim |>   dplyr::slice_head(n = 30) |>   dplyr::mutate(glucose = G0) |>   dplyr::select(TC, HDL_c, TG, LDL_c, ApoB, ApoA1, waist, BMI, glucose)

title: lipid_markers
text: Required: TC, HDL_c, TG (mmol/L). Optional present/mapped: LDL_c (mmol/L), ApoB, ApoA1, waist (cm), BMI (kg/m^2), glucose (mmol/L; needed TyG-BMI). Use col_map rename; numeric-like inputs coerced non-finite set NA policies.

title: lipid_markers
code: lm_out <- lipid_markers(   data = lipids,   col_map = list(     TC = "TC", HDL_c = "HDL_c", TG = "TG", LDL_c = "LDL_c",     ApoB = "ApoB", ApoA1 = "ApoA1", waist = "waist", BMI = "BMI", glucose = "glucose"   ),   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE ) lm_out |> dplyr::slice_head(n = 5) #> # A tibble: 5 × 11 #>   non_HDL_c remnant_c ratio_TC_HDL ratio_TG_HDL ratio_LDL_HDL ApoB_ApoA1 VAI_Men #>       <dbl>     <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>         <dbl>      <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1      5.14     1.43          4.95        2.42           2.85      0.690   3.04  #> 2      4.58     0.33          4.75        0.590          3.48      0.412   0.783 #> 3      5.7      0.49          5.52        0.849          4.13      0.992   0.991 #> 4      2.97     0.420         3.02        0.626          1.73      1.08    0.815 #> 5      3.12     0.75          2.96        1.03           1.49      0.674   1.10  #> # ℹ 4 more variables: VAI_Women <dbl>, LAP_Men <dbl>, LAP_Women <dbl>, #> #   TyG_BMI <dbl>

title: lipid_markers
text: data frame uses different column names, supply col_map redirect key differs. Keys omit auto-inferred common synonym patterns (e.g. total_chol, total_cholesterol, tc resolve TC). weight height overridden; TC, HDL_c, TG, LDL_c, waist, glucose matched built-synonym dictionary.
code: # Columns use non-standard names for weight and height df_mapped <- data.frame(   TC = 5.0, HDL_c = 1.3, TG = 1.5, LDL_c = 3.0,   body_weight = 80,    # kg   body_height = 175,   # cm   waist = 92,   glucose = 5.8 ) lm_mapped <- lipid_markers(   df_mapped,   col_map = list(weight = "body_weight", height = "body_height"),   verbose = FALSE ) # BMI was derived from body_weight / body_height; VAI and TyG-BMI are now available lm_mapped[, c("VAI_Men", "TyG_BMI")] #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   VAI_Men TyG_BMI #>     <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1    1.52    231.

title: lipid_markers
text: na_action: keep/ignore retain rows missing inputs (derived outputs become NA); omit drops ; error aborts; warn retains warnings. na_warn_prop flags high missingness warning. LDL_c absent, LDL estimated via Friedewald (TC - HDL - TG/5) TG available.

title: lipid_markers
text: Outputs: non_HDL_c, remnant_c, ratio_TC_HDL, ratio_TG_HDL, ratio_LDL_HDL, ApoB_ApoA1 (needs ApoB & ApoA1), VAI_Men/Women (needs waist & BMI), LAP_Men/Women (needs waist), TyG_BMI (needs glucose & BMI). Units follow inputs; TyG-BMI converts TG/glucose mg/dL internally.

title: lipid_markers
text: Provide waist BMI enable VAI/LAP; supply glucose BMI enable TyG-BMI. Set na_action = "omit" need complete-case derived lipid indices.

title: lipid_markers
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") lipid_markers(   data    = dplyr::slice_head(lipids, n = 5),   col_map = list(TC = "TC", HDL_c = "HDL_c", TG = "TG",                  LDL_c = "LDL_c", ApoB = "ApoB", ApoA1 = "ApoA1",                  waist = "waist", BMI = "BMI"),   verbose = TRUE ) #> lipid_markers(): reading input 'data' — 5 rows × 9 variables #> lipid_markers(): col_map (9 columns — 8 specified, 1 inferred from data) #>   TC                ->  'TC' #>   HDL_c             ->  'HDL_c' #>   TG                ->  'TG' #>   LDL_c             ->  'LDL_c' #>   ApoB              ->  'ApoB' #>   ApoA1             ->  'ApoA1' #>   waist             ->  'waist' #>   BMI               ->  'BMI' #>   glucose           ->  'glucose'    (inferred) #> lipid_markers(): optional inputs #>   present:  ApoB, ApoA1, waist, BMI, glucose #> lipid_markers(): range note (informational, values not altered): #>   TG: 5 value(s) outside plausible range #> lipid_markers(): computing markers: #>   non_HDL_c     [TC, HDL_c] #>   remnant_c     [TC, HDL_c, LDL_c] #>   ratio_TC_HDL  [TC, HDL_c] #>   ratio_TG_HDL  [TG, HDL_c] #>   ratio_LDL_HDL [LDL_c, HDL_c] #>   ApoB_ApoA1    [ApoB, ApoA1] #>   VAI_Men/Women [waist, BMI, TG, HDL_c] #>   LAP_Men/Women [waist, TG] #>   TyG_BMI       [TG, glucose, BMI] #> lipid_markers(): results: non_HDL_c 5/5, remnant_c 5/5, ratio_TC_HDL 5/5, ratio_TG_HDL 5/5, ratio_LDL_HDL 5/5, ApoB_ApoA1 5/5, VAI_Men 5/5, VAI_Women 5/5, LAP_Men 5/5, LAP_Women 5/5, TyG_BMI 5/5 #> # A tibble: 5 × 11 #>   non_HDL_c remnant_c ratio_TC_HDL ratio_TG_HDL ratio_LDL_HDL ApoB_ApoA1 VAI_Men #>       <dbl>     <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>         <dbl>      <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1      5.14     1.43          4.95        2.42           2.85      0.690   3.04  #> 2      4.58     0.33          4.75        0.590          3.48      0.412   0.783 #> 3      5.7      0.49          5.52        0.849          4.13      0.992   0.991 #> 4      2.97     0.420         3.02        0.626          1.73      1.08    0.815 #> 5      3.12     0.75          2.96        1.03           1.49      0.674   1.10  #> # ℹ 4 more variables: VAI_Women <dbl>, LAP_Men <dbl>, LAP_Women <dbl>, #> #   TyG_BMI <dbl> options(old_opt)

title: Liver Fat Surrogates: HSI and NAFLD-LFS
text: liver_fat_markers() computes two non-invasive surrogate indexes hepatic steatosis: HSI (Hepatic Steatosis Index): 8 × (ALT/AST) + BMI + 2 (female) + 2 (diabetes) NAFLD-LFS (NAFLD Liver Fat Score): requires metabolic syndrome status fasting insulin indexes returned tibble columns HSI NAFLD_LFS.

title: Liver Fat Surrogates: HSI and NAFLD-LFS
text: ALT, AST, BMI strictly required. Without sex/diabetes female diabetes terms default 0.
code: df <- data.frame(ALT = c(30, 45, 60), AST = c(20, 30, 25), BMI = c(24, 28, 33))  liver_fat_markers(   df,   col_map = list(ALT = "ALT", AST = "AST", BMI = "BMI") ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>     HSI NAFLD_LFS #>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  36          NA #> 2  40          NA #> 3  52.2        NA

title: Liver Fat Surrogates: HSI and NAFLD-LFS
text: Providing sex, diabetes, insulin enables full NAFLD-LFS formula.
code: df2 <- data.frame(   ALT      = c(30, 45, 60),   AST      = c(20, 30, 25),   BMI      = c(24, 28, 33),   sex      = c("female", "male", "female"),   diabetes = c(FALSE, TRUE, FALSE),   insulin  = c(10, 22, 15) )  liver_fat_markers(   df2,   col_map = list(     ALT      = "ALT",     AST      = "AST",     BMI      = "BMI",     sex      = "sex",     diabetes = "diabetes",     insulin  = "insulin"   ) ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>     HSI NAFLD_LFS #>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  38          NA #> 2  42          NA #> 3  54.2        NA

title: Liver Fat Surrogates: HSI and NAFLD-LFS
text: MetS available directly, function derives via NCEP-ATP III criteria waist, TG, HDL_c, blood pressure column, glucose provided.
code: df3 <- data.frame(   ALT = 55, AST = 30, BMI = 31,   sex = "female", diabetes = FALSE, insulin = 18,   waist = 92, TG = 1.9, HDL_c = 1.1, bp_sys = 135, glucose = 6.0 )  liver_fat_markers(   df3,   col_map = list(     ALT = "ALT", AST = "AST", BMI = "BMI",     sex = "sex", diabetes = "diabetes", insulin = "insulin",     waist = "waist", TG = "TG", HDL_c = "HDL_c",     bp_sys = "bp_sys", glucose = "glucose"   ) ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>     HSI NAFLD_LFS #>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  47.7      1.68

title: Liver Fat Surrogates: HSI and NAFLD-LFS
text: Use na_action control rows missing required values treated.
code: df_na <- data.frame(ALT = c(30, NA, 50), AST = c(20, 25, 30), BMI = c(25, 28, 31))  # omit: return only complete rows liver_fat_markers(   df_na,   col_map  = list(ALT = "ALT", AST = "AST", BMI = "BMI"),   na_action = "omit" ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>     HSI NAFLD_LFS #>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  37          NA #> 2  44.3        NA  # keep: return all rows, NA for incomplete ones liver_fat_markers(   df_na,   col_map  = list(ALT = "ALT", AST = "AST", BMI = "BMI"),   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>     HSI NAFLD_LFS #>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  37          NA #> 2  NA          NA #> 3  44.3        NA

title: Liver Fat Surrogates: HSI and NAFLD-LFS
text: Enable step--step messages trace exactly columns mapped many rows computed successfully.
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")  df_v <- data.frame(ALT = c(30, 45), AST = c(20, 30), BMI = c(24, 28),                    sex = c("male", "female"), diabetes = c(FALSE, TRUE),                    insulin = c(10, 22))  liver_fat_markers(   df_v,   col_map = list(     ALT = "ALT", AST = "AST", BMI = "BMI",     sex = "sex", diabetes = "diabetes", insulin = "insulin"   ),   verbose = TRUE ) #> liver_fat_markers(): reading input 'df_v' — 2 rows × 6 variables #> liver_fat_markers(): col_map (7 columns — 6 specified, 1 inferred from data) #>   ALT               ->  'ALT' #>   AST               ->  'AST' #>   BMI               ->  'BMI' #>   sex               ->  'sex' #>   diabetes          ->  'diabetes' #>   insulin           ->  'insulin' #>   I0                ->  'insulin'    (inferred) #> liver_fat_markers(): computing markers: #>   HSI        [8*(ALT/AST) + BMI + sex + diabetes] #>   NAFLD_LFS  [MetS/insulin/diabetes required; NA if unavailable] #> liver_fat_markers(): results: HSI 2/2, NAFLD_LFS 0/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>     HSI NAFLD_LFS #>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1    36        NA #> 2    44        NA  options(old_opt)

title: Liver Fat Surrogates: HSI and NAFLD-LFS
text: columns carry site-specific names, specify mapping explicitly:
code: df_ns <- data.frame(   alt_IU_L     = c(28, 42, 65, 38),   ast_IU_L     = c(18, 28, 35, 22),   bmi_kgm2     = c(23.5, 29.0, 34.2, 27.8),   gender       = c("female", "male", "female", "male"),   t2dm_flag    = c(FALSE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE),   insulin_uIUmL = c(9, 18, 28, 12) )  liver_fat_markers(   df_ns,   col_map = list(     ALT      = "alt_IU_L",     AST      = "ast_IU_L",     BMI      = "bmi_kgm2",     sex      = "gender",     diabetes = "t2dm_flag",     insulin  = "insulin_uIUmL"   ) ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>     HSI NAFLD_LFS #>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  37.9        NA #> 2  41          NA #> 3  53.1        NA #> 4  41.6        NA

title: Liver Fat Surrogates: HSI and NAFLD-LFS
text: Extreme ALT AST values produce extreme index scores. Pre-filter implausible inputs calling.
code: df_ext <- data.frame(   ALT = c(30, 650, 60),  # 650 is implausibly extreme; pre-filter   AST = c(20,  30, 25),   BMI = c(24,  28, 33) )  # Pre-filter before calling df_ext$ALT[df_ext$ALT > 500] <- NA  liver_fat_markers(   df_ext,   col_map = list(ALT = "ALT", AST = "AST", BMI = "BMI") ) #> liver_fat_markers(): reading input 'df_ext' — 3 rows × 3 variables #> liver_fat_markers(): col_map (3 columns — 3 specified) #>   ALT               ->  'ALT' #>   AST               ->  'AST' #>   BMI               ->  'BMI' #> liver_fat_markers(): computing markers: #>   HSI        [8*(ALT/AST) + BMI + sex + diabetes] #>   NAFLD_LFS  [MetS/insulin/diabetes required; NA if unavailable] #> liver_fat_markers(): results: HSI 2/3, NAFLD_LFS 0/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>     HSI NAFLD_LFS #>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  36          NA #> 2  NA          NA #> 3  52.2        NA

title: Liver Fat Surrogates: HSI and NAFLD-LFS
text: bundled simulated dataset ALT, AST, BMI, sex columns, enabling HSI (partial NAFLD-LFS) computation column renaming: Column recognition tip: Run hm_col_report(your_data) check liver enzyme metabolic columns auto-detected building col_map. See Multi-Biobank Compatibility article recognised synonyms across major biobanks (e.g., alanine_aminotransferase, ALT_UL, ALAT).
code: sim_path  <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(readRDS(sim_path), n = 50)  lf_sim <- liver_fat_markers(   sim_small,   col_map   = list(ALT = "ALT", AST = "AST", BMI = "BMI", sex = "sex"),   na_action = "keep" )  dplyr::bind_cols(dplyr::select(sim_small, ALT, AST, BMI), lf_sim) |> head() #>   ALT AST  BMI   id      HSI NAFLD_LFS #> 1  86  25 32.0 P001 59.52000  8.066744 #> 2  80  27 29.7 P002 55.40370  2.147750 #> 3  93  49 27.0 P003 44.18367  7.814731 #> 4  43  73 26.2 P004 32.91233  6.474186 #> 5  95  18 24.1 P005 68.32222 10.746895 #> 6  64  25 36.6 P006 59.08000  4.837813

title: Liver Markers
text: Compute liver-related indices (FLI, NFS, APRI, FIB-4, BARD, ALBI, MELD-XI) configurable handling missing data extreme values.

title: Liver Markers
text: routine liver labs anthropometry (BMI, waist, TG, GGT, AST, ALT, platelets, albumin, bilirubin, creatinine, diabetes flag) want standard steatosis/fibrosis scores. need built-NA policy controls, numeric coercion, optional extreme-value scanning/capping. want MELD-XI, ALBI, common non-invasive fibrosis indices one call screening modeling.

title: Liver Markers
text: data: data.frame/tibble required inputs. col_map: named list mapping required keys columns (units assumed, conversion): BMI (kg/m^2), waist (cm), TG (mg/dL), GGT (U/L), age (years), AST (U/L), ALT (U/L), platelets (10^9/L), albumin (g/L), diabetes (0/1 logical), bilirubin (mg/dL), creatinine (mg/dL). na_action: keep (default) propagates NA; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts; ignore treated keep; warn keeps issues high-missingness warnings via na_warn_prop. Numeric coercion: mapped columns (except diabetes) coerced numeric; non-finite values become NA policies. diabetes coerced integer 0/1 warning values outside {0,1,TRUE,FALSE}.

title: Liver Markers
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  liver <- tibble::tibble(   BMI           = c(24, 30, 33),   waist         = c(80, 100, 110),   triglycerides = c(150, 260, 90),   # mg/dL   GGT           = c(30, 55, 18),   age           = c(45, 58, 67),   AST           = c(25, 60, 42),   ALT           = c(20, 45, 28),   platelets     = c(250, 190, 210),  # 10^9/L   albumin       = c(42, 38, 40),     # g/L   diabetes      = c(FALSE, TRUE, 1),   bilirubin     = c(1.0, 0.9, 1.3),  # mg/dL   creatinine    = c(0.9, 1.1, 1.0)   # mg/dL )  liver_markers(   data = liver,   col_map = list(     BMI = "BMI", waist = "waist", TG = "triglycerides", GGT = "GGT", age = "age",     AST = "AST", ALT = "ALT", platelets = "platelets", albumin = "albumin", diabetes = "diabetes",     bilirubin = "bilirubin", creatinine = "creatinine"   ),   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>     FLI   NFS  APRI  FIB4  BARD  ALBI MELD_XI #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <int> <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1  27.9 -27.5 0.25   1.01     1 -2.76    8.20 #> 2  87.0 -21.8 0.789  2.73     3 -2.45   10.0  #> 3  73.8 -22.6 0.5    2.53     3 -2.52   10.8

title: Liver Markers
text: data use different column names, supply col_map entries keys differ. remaining keys matched automatically via built-synonym dictionary (case-insensitive, common abbreviations: bili → bilirubin, creat → creatinine, platelet_count → platelets, etc.). GGT, AST, ALT, PLT (→ platelets), serum_albumin (→ albumin), bili (→ bilirubin), creat (→ creatinine) matched automatically; five renamed keys required explicit col_map entries.
code: df_ns <- tibble::tibble(   bmi_kgm2      = 27,   waist_cm      = 92,   trig_mgdl     = 180,   # mg/dL   ggt           = 40,   age_years     = 52,   AST           = 32,   ALT           = 28,   PLT           = 220,   # platelets, 10^9/L   serum_albumin = 40,    # g/L   dm            = 1L,   bili          = 0.8,   # mg/dL   creat         = 1.0    # mg/dL )  liver_markers(   data    = df_ns,   col_map = list(     BMI        = "bmi_kgm2",     waist      = "waist_cm",     TG         = "trig_mgdl",     age        = "age_years",     diabetes   = "dm"   ),   verbose = FALSE ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>     FLI   NFS  APRI  FIB4  BARD  ALBI MELD_XI #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <int> <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1  61.8 -24.2 0.364  1.43     2 -2.66    8.30

title: Liver Markers
text: Missing values: keep/ignore propagate NA; omit drops rows missing required inputs; warn keeps logs high-missingness column; error aborts missing required inputs. Non-finite values become NA policy applied.

title: Liver Markers
text: Returns tibble : FLI, NFS, APRI, FIB4, BARD, ALBI, MELD_XI. Bilirubin converted umol/L internally ALBI; inputs use provided units. Zero nonpositive platelets, TG, GGT, bilirubin, creatinine, negative ALT trigger warnings yield NA affected indices.

title: Liver Markers
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") liver_markers(   data = liver,   col_map = list(     BMI = "BMI", waist = "waist", TG = "triglycerides", GGT = "GGT", age = "age",     AST = "AST", ALT = "ALT", platelets = "platelets", albumin = "albumin", diabetes = "diabetes",     bilirubin = "bilirubin", creatinine = "creatinine"   ),   verbose = TRUE ) #> liver_markers(): reading input 'liver' — 3 rows × 12 variables #> liver_markers(): col_map (12 columns — 12 specified) #>   BMI               ->  'BMI' #>   waist             ->  'waist' #>   TG                ->  'triglycerides' #>   GGT               ->  'GGT' #>   age               ->  'age' #>   AST               ->  'AST' #>   ALT               ->  'ALT' #>   platelets         ->  'platelets' #>   albumin           ->  'albumin' #>   diabetes          ->  'diabetes' #>   bilirubin         ->  'bilirubin' #>   creatinine        ->  'creatinine' #> liver_markers(): optional inputs #>   present:  BMI, waist, TG, GGT, age, AST, ALT, platelets, albumin, diabetes, bilirubin, creatinine #> liver_markers(): computing markers: #>   FLI        [BMI, waist, TG, GGT] #>   NFS        [age, BMI, diabetes, AST, ALT, platelets, albumin] #>   APRI       [AST, platelets] #>   FIB4       [age, AST, platelets, ALT] #>   BARD       [BMI, AST, ALT, diabetes] #>   ALBI       [bilirubin, albumin] #>   MELD_XI    [bilirubin, creatinine] #> liver_markers(): results: FLI 3/3, NFS 3/3, APRI 3/3, FIB4 3/3, BARD 3/3, ALBI 3/3, MELD_XI 3/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>     FLI   NFS  APRI  FIB4  BARD  ALBI MELD_XI #>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <int> <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1  27.9 -27.5 0.25   1.01     1 -2.76    8.20 #> 2  87.0 -21.8 0.789  2.73     3 -2.45   10.0  #> 3  73.8 -22.6 0.5    2.53     3 -2.52   10.8 options(old_opt)

title: Liver Markers
text: Run hm_col_report(your_data) check analyte columns auto-detected building col_map. See Multi-Biobank Compatibility article recognised synonyms across major biobanks.
code: hm_col_report(your_data)

title: Liver Markers
text: Confirm triglycerides mg/dL (mmol/L) albumin g/L. modeling-ready outputs, prefer na_action = "omit"; use keep warn inspecting data quality.

title: Metabolic Risk Features
text: Create pediatric-friendly binary risk flags (dyslipidemia, insulin resistance, hyperglycemia, hypertension) routine labs BP z-scores configurable NA extreme handling.

title: Metabolic Risk Features
text: lipid panel, glucose/HbA1c, BP z-scores, age, z-scored HOMA-IR want quick binary risk features. need NA policies, high-missingness warnings, optional extreme-value screening/capping built . working pediatrics/adolescents triglyceride thresholds differ age BP already standardized.

title: Metabolic Risk Features
text: data: data.frame/tibble required numeric inputs. col_map: named list mapping required keys (defaults names): chol_total, chol_ldl, chol_hdl, triglycerides, age_year, z_HOMA, glucose, HbA1c, bp_sys_z, bp_dia_z. Units assumed: lipids mmol/L; glucose mmol/L; HbA1c mmol/mol (IFCC); BP inputs already z-scores; age years; z_HOMA z-scored HOMA-IR. na_action: keep (default) propagates NA; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts; ignore acts like keep; warn acts like keep plus high-missingness warnings via na_warn_prop (default 0.2).

title: Metabolic Risk Features
text: Two required inputs derived, raw lab values:

title: Metabolic Risk Features
text: Compute HOMA-IR fasting glucose (mmol/L) fasting insulin (µIU/mL), standardise z-score relative healthy reference population: Published paediatric reference values: Keskin et al. (2005, Pediatr Diabetes); adults, 90th-percentile cutpoint (z ≈ 1.28) commonly applied.
code: # Step 1: compute HOMA-IR data$HOMA_IR <- (data$fasting_glucose_mmol * data$fasting_insulin_uIU) / 22.5  # Step 2: standardise within cohort (or use external reference parameters) data$z_HOMA  <- normalize_vec(data$HOMA_IR, method = "z")

title: Metabolic Risk Features
text: BP z-scores must derived using sex- age-specific normative tables (AAP 2017 Fourth Report paediatric reference). calc_sds() function can apply stratified reference parameters ; alternatively childsds package (CRAN) provides ready--use paediatric normative tables.
code: # Manual z-score: (observed − reference_mean) / reference_sd data$bp_sys_z <- (data$sbp - ref_sbp_mean) / ref_sbp_sd

title: Metabolic Risk Features
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  # Six children/adolescents spanning multiple metabolic risk combinations peds <- tibble::tibble(   chol_total    = c(5.4, 4.8, 3.9, 6.2, 5.1, 4.5),   chol_ldl      = c(3.6, 2.9, 2.4, 4.1, 3.2, 2.8),   chol_hdl      = c(0.9, 1.2, 1.4, 0.8, 1.0, 1.3),   triglycerides = c(1.6, 1.0, 0.8, 2.0, 1.3, 0.9),   age_year      = c(15,  9,  12,  17,  11,   8 ),   z_HOMA        = c(1.5, 0.8, 0.2, 2.1, 1.0, -0.3),   glucose       = c(5.8, 5.1, 4.9, 6.5, 5.4, 4.8),   HbA1c         = c(40,  35,  32,  46,  38,  30 ),   bp_sys_z      = c(1.2, 1.8, 0.3, 2.1, 0.8, -0.2),   bp_dia_z      = c(0.5, 1.7, 0.2, 1.9, 0.6,  0.1) )  metabolic_risk_features(   data = peds,   col_map = list(     chol_total = "chol_total", chol_ldl = "chol_ldl", chol_hdl = "chol_hdl", triglycerides = "triglycerides",     age_year = "age_year", z_HOMA = "z_HOMA", glucose = "glucose", HbA1c = "HbA1c",     bp_sys_z = "bp_sys_z", bp_dia_z = "bp_dia_z"   ),   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   dyslipidemia insulin_resistance hyperglycemia hypertension #>   <fct>        <fct>              <fct>         <fct>        #> 1 1            1                  1             0            #> 2 0            0                  0             1            #> 3 0            0                  0             0            #> 4 1            1                  1             1            #> 5 0            0                  0             0            #> 6 0            0                  0             0

title: Metabolic Risk Features
text: Extreme inputs produce extreme incorrect flags. Pre-filter implausible values calling.
code: extreme <- peds extreme$triglycerides[1] <- 40  # extreme; pre-filter extreme$bp_sys_z[2] <- 9        # out of expected range; pre-filter extreme$triglycerides[extreme$triglycerides > 30] <- NA extreme$bp_sys_z[abs(extreme$bp_sys_z) > 5] <- NA  metabolic_risk_features(   data = extreme,   col_map = list(     chol_total = "chol_total", chol_ldl = "chol_ldl", chol_hdl = "chol_hdl", triglycerides = "triglycerides",     age_year = "age_year", z_HOMA = "z_HOMA", glucose = "glucose", HbA1c = "HbA1c",     bp_sys_z = "bp_sys_z", bp_dia_z = "bp_dia_z"   ),   na_action = "warn",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   dyslipidemia insulin_resistance hyperglycemia hypertension #>   <fct>        <fct>              <fct>         <fct>        #> 1 1            1                  1             0            #> 2 0            0                  0             1            #> 3 0            0                  0             0            #> 4 1            1                  1             1            #> 5 0            0                  0             0            #> 6 0            0                  0             0

title: Metabolic Risk Features
code: missing <- peds missing$glucose[2] <- NA  metabolic_risk_features(   data = missing,   col_map = list(chol_total = "chol_total", chol_ldl = "chol_ldl", chol_hdl = "chol_hdl", triglycerides = "triglycerides",                  age_year = "age_year", z_HOMA = "z_HOMA", glucose = "glucose", HbA1c = "HbA1c",                  bp_sys_z = "bp_sys_z", bp_dia_z = "bp_dia_z"),   na_action = "omit" ) #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   dyslipidemia insulin_resistance hyperglycemia hypertension #>   <fct>        <fct>              <fct>         <fct>        #> 1 1            1                  1             0            #> 2 0            0                  0             0            #> 3 1            1                  1             1            #> 4 0            0                  0             0            #> 5 0            0                  0             0  metabolic_risk_features(   data = missing,   col_map = list(chol_total = "chol_total", chol_ldl = "chol_ldl", chol_hdl = "chol_hdl", triglycerides = "triglycerides",                  age_year = "age_year", z_HOMA = "z_HOMA", glucose = "glucose", HbA1c = "HbA1c",                  bp_sys_z = "bp_sys_z", bp_dia_z = "bp_dia_z"),   na_action = "warn" ) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   dyslipidemia insulin_resistance hyperglycemia hypertension #>   <fct>        <fct>              <fct>         <fct>        #> 1 1            1                  1             0            #> 2 0            0                  NA            1            #> 3 0            0                  0             0            #> 4 1            1                  1             1            #> 5 0            0                  0             0            #> 6 0            0                  0             0

title: Metabolic Risk Features
code: ext_cap <- peds ext_cap$triglycerides[1] <- 40   # extreme high ext_cap$bp_sys_z[2] <- 9         # extreme high ext_cap$HbA1c[2] <- NA           # missing HbA1c to trigger drop  out_cap <- metabolic_risk_features(   data = ext_cap,   col_map = list(     chol_total = "chol_total", chol_ldl = "chol_ldl", chol_hdl = "chol_hdl", triglycerides = "triglycerides",     age_year = "age_year", z_HOMA = "z_HOMA", glucose = "glucose", HbA1c = "HbA1c",     bp_sys_z = "bp_sys_z", bp_dia_z = "bp_dia_z"   ),   na_action = "omit",   verbose = TRUE )  list(rows_returned = nrow(out_cap), flags = out_cap) #> $rows_returned #> [1] 5 #>  #> $flags #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   dyslipidemia insulin_resistance hyperglycemia hypertension #>   <fct>        <fct>              <fct>         <fct>        #> 1 1            1                  1             0            #> 2 0            0                  0             0            #> 3 1            1                  1             1            #> 4 0            0                  0             0            #> 5 0            0                  0             0

title: Metabolic Risk Features
text: Missingness: keep/ignore propagate NA; omit drops rows missing required inputs; warn logs high-missingness; error aborts missing required inputs. Outputs: tibble factor columns dyslipidemia, insulin_resistance, hyperglycemia, hypertension (levels 0/1). Age-based TG cutoffs: >1.1 mmol/L (0–9 y) >1.5 mmol/L (10–19 y) dyslipidemia; BP z > 1.64 hypertension; z_HOMA > 1.28 insulin resistance; glucose 5.6–6.9 HbA1c 39–47 hyperglycemia.

title: Metabolic Risk Features
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") metabolic_risk_features(   data = peds,   col_map = list(     chol_total = "chol_total", chol_ldl = "chol_ldl", chol_hdl = "chol_hdl",     triglycerides = "triglycerides", age_year = "age_year", z_HOMA = "z_HOMA",     glucose = "glucose", HbA1c = "HbA1c", bp_sys_z = "bp_sys_z", bp_dia_z = "bp_dia_z"   ),   verbose = TRUE ) #> metabolic_risk_features(): reading input 'peds' — 6 rows × 10 variables #> metabolic_risk_features(): col_map (10 columns — 10 specified) #>   chol_total        ->  'chol_total' #>   chol_ldl          ->  'chol_ldl' #>   chol_hdl          ->  'chol_hdl' #>   triglycerides     ->  'triglycerides' #>   age_year          ->  'age_year' #>   z_HOMA            ->  'z_HOMA' #>   glucose           ->  'glucose' #>   HbA1c             ->  'HbA1c' #>   bp_sys_z          ->  'bp_sys_z' #>   bp_dia_z          ->  'bp_dia_z' #> metabolic_risk_features(): computing markers: #>   dyslipidemia       [chol_total, chol_ldl, chol_hdl, triglycerides, age_year] #>   insulin_resistance [z_HOMA] #>   hyperglycemia      [glucose, HbA1c] #>   hypertension       [bp_sys_z, bp_dia_z] #> metabolic_risk_features(): results: dyslipidemia 6/6, insulin_resistance 6/6, hyperglycemia 6/6, hypertension 6/6 #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   dyslipidemia insulin_resistance hyperglycemia hypertension #>   <fct>        <fct>              <fct>         <fct>        #> 1 1            1                  1             0            #> 2 0            0                  0             1            #> 3 0            0                  0             0            #> 4 1            1                  1             1            #> 5 0            0                  0             0            #> 6 0            0                  0             0 options(old_opt)

title: Metabolic Risk Features
text: Keep lipids/glucose mmol/L HbA1c mmol/mol; BP inputs must already z-scores. Use na_action = "omit" analysis-ready flags; keep/warn QA inspect missingness. Watch HbA1c reported % (values <=14 suggest percent) lipids/glucose mg/dL (appear ~×38.7 ×18 higher); standardize units calling. BP inputs must already z-scores; large magnitude (|z| > 5) usually indicates scaling issues.

title: MetSS
text: Compute Metabolic Syndrome Severity Score (MetSSS) using sex- race-specific coefficients, configurable NA/extreme handling plausibility diagnostics.

title: MetSS
text: waist, blood pressure, fasting lipids/glucose, sex, race want continuous MetS severity score. need built-NA policies, high-missingness warnings, optional extreme-value scanning/capping. okay single parameter key (race/sex) applied rows—matching original implementation.

title: MetSS
text: data: data.frame/tibble waist (cm), bp_sys/bp_dia (mmHg), TG/HDL_c/glucose (mmol/L), sex (1=male, 2=female), race {NHW, NHB, HW, HA} accepted synonyms. params: named list keyed RACE_SEX (e.g., NHW_M), intercept component vectors (waist, TG, HDL, glucose, MAP) containing mean, sd, coef. na_action: keep (default) propagates NA; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts; ignore/warn act like keep (warn also adds high-missingness warnings via na_warn_prop). diagnostics: TRUE (default), warns implausible ranges invalid sex/race encodings. verbose: log progress summary.

title: MetSS
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  mets <- tibble::tibble(   waist = c(95, 108),   bp_sys = c(120, 138),   bp_dia = c(80, 86),   TG = c(1.5, 2.1),   HDL_c = c(1.2, 0.9),   glucose = c(5.5, 6.2),   sex = c(1, 1),   race = c("NHW", "NHW") )  metss(   data = mets,   params = list(     NHW_M = list(       intercept = -2.344,       waist   = c(mean = 94.0, sd = 12.4, coef = 0.846),       TG      = c(mean = 1.5,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.701),       HDL     = c(mean = 1.1,  sd = 0.3,  coef = -0.663),       glucose = c(mean = 5.3,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.658),       MAP     = c(mean = 97,   sd = 11,   coef = 0.466)     )   ),   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>   MetSSS #>    <dbl> #> 1  -2.43 #> 2   1.01

title: MetSS
text: Extreme inputs produce extreme MetSSS scores. diagnostics = TRUE option warn implausible ranges; pre-filter calling.
code: extreme <- mets extreme$TG[2] <- 25       # extreme; pre-filter extreme$bp_sys[1] <- 400  # out of range; pre-filter extreme$TG[extreme$TG > 20] <- NA extreme$bp_sys[extreme$bp_sys > 300] <- NA  metss(   data = extreme,   params = list(     NHW_M = list(       intercept = -2.344,       waist   = c(mean = 94.0, sd = 12.4, coef = 0.846),       TG      = c(mean = 1.5,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.701),       HDL     = c(mean = 1.1,  sd = 0.3,  coef = -0.663),       glucose = c(mean = 5.3,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.658),       MAP     = c(mean = 97,   sd = 11,   coef = 0.466)     )   ),   na_action = "warn",   diagnostics = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>   MetSSS #>    <dbl> #> 1     NA #> 2     NA

title: MetSS
code: missing <- mets missing$HDL_c[1] <- NA  metss(   data = missing,   params = list(     NHW_M = list(       intercept = -2.344,       waist   = c(mean = 94.0, sd = 12.4, coef = 0.846),       TG      = c(mean = 1.5,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.701),       HDL     = c(mean = 1.1,  sd = 0.3,  coef = -0.663),       glucose = c(mean = 5.3,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.658),       MAP     = c(mean = 97,   sd = 11,   coef = 0.466)     )   ),   na_action = "omit" ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   MetSSS #>    <dbl> #> 1   1.01  metss(   data = missing,   params = list(     NHW_M = list(       intercept = -2.344,       waist   = c(mean = 94.0, sd = 12.4, coef = 0.846),       TG      = c(mean = 1.5,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.701),       HDL     = c(mean = 1.1,  sd = 0.3,  coef = -0.663),       glucose = c(mean = 5.3,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.658),       MAP     = c(mean = 97,   sd = 11,   coef = 0.466)     )   ),   na_action = "warn" ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>   MetSSS #>    <dbl> #> 1  NA    #> 2   1.01

title: MetSS
text: Returns tibble MetSSS using single race/sex key derived first row (warns multiple keys present). na_action = "omit" drops rows missing required inputs; otherwise missing inputs propagate NA MetSSS. diagnostics warns independently implausible ranges.

title: MetSS
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") metss(   data = mets,   params = list(     NHW_M = list(       intercept = -2.344,       waist   = c(mean = 94.0, sd = 12.4, coef = 0.846),       TG      = c(mean = 1.5,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.701),       HDL     = c(mean = 1.1,  sd = 0.3,  coef = -0.663),       glucose = c(mean = 5.3,  sd = 0.6,  coef = 0.658),       MAP     = c(mean = 97,   sd = 11,   coef = 0.466)     )   ),   verbose = TRUE ) #> metss(): reading input 'mets' — 2 rows × 8 variables #> metss(): col_map (8 columns — 8 inferred from data) #>   waist             ->  'waist'    (inferred) #>   bp_sys            ->  'bp_sys'    (inferred) #>   bp_dia            ->  'bp_dia'    (inferred) #>   TG                ->  'TG'    (inferred) #>   HDL_c             ->  'HDL_c'    (inferred) #>   glucose           ->  'glucose'    (inferred) #>   sex               ->  'sex'    (inferred) #>   race              ->  'race'    (inferred) #> metss(): computing markers: #>   MetSSS  [factor-loading z-score, sex/race-specific params] #> metss(): results: MetSSS 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>   MetSSS #>    <dbl> #> 1  -2.43 #> 2   1.01 options(old_opt)

title: MetSS
text: Confirm sex encoded 1/2 race matches params key; first-row key used. Keep units consistent (waist cm, BP mmHg, lipids/glucose mmol/L); unit conversion occurs . Leave diagnostics = TRUE QA catch implausible ranges; silence needed tests.

what: Supplying params for all race/sex groups
title: MetSS
text: function requires params list keyed RACE_SEX (e.g., "NHW_M"). Published coefficients eight groups available : Gurka MJ, Vishnu , Santen RJ, DeBoer MD (2016). Adjusting metabolic syndrome components use menopausal transition. J Clin Endocrinol Metab, 101(8), 3104–3113. structure shows full eight-group params object. Population means standard deviations derived NHANES; regression coefficients weight contribution standardised component: Note: NHW_M coefficients match original Gurka et al. values shown Quick start example. remaining seven groups use approximate values consistent NHANES population distributions; verify original paper publishing results. Pass full list dataset includes multiple race/sex groups:

title: MetSS
text: bundled simulated dataset waist, TG, HDL_c, glucose, bp_sys, bp_dia, sex, race columns. Compute MAP internally run MetSSS directly sim_small:
code: library(dplyr) sim_path  <- system.file("extdata", "simulated_hm_data.rds", package = "HealthMarkers") sim_small <- dplyr::slice_head(readRDS(sim_path), n = 50)  # metss expects MAP; derive it if not already present: sim_small <- dplyr::mutate(   sim_small,   MAP = (bp_sys + 2 * bp_dia) / 3 )  metss(   data   = sim_small,   params = all_params,    # defined above   na_action = "keep" )

title: Multi-Biobank Compatibility: Automatic Column Recognition
text: HealthMarkers designed work data wide range international cohort studies biobanks without manual column renaming. internal synonym dictionary covers naming conventions 15+ major studies. article explains matching works, biobanks supported, diagnose fix cases column recognised.

title: Multi-Biobank Compatibility: Automatic Column Recognition
text: Every HealthMarkers function looks column names five-layer matching pipeline computation: first four layers active default. Layer 5 opt-useful catching typos column names.

what: All of Us / OMOP CDM (LOINC codes)
title: Multi-Biobank Compatibility: Automatic Column Recognition
text: EHR-derived data modelled OMOP Common Data Model, concept codes LOINC_XXXX_X format recognised major analytes (hyphen LOINC code replaced underscore):

title: Multi-Biobank Compatibility: Automatic Column Recognition
text: running computation new dataset, call hm_col_report() see exactly columns matched : output looks like: FOUND keys, either: 1. Add column data (compute raw inputs), 2. Provide explicit col_map entry, 3. Ignore — functions produce NA marker requires key.
code: library(HealthMarkers)  # Load your biobank extract (example: HUNT data with Norwegian column names) hunt_data <- read.csv("hunt_extract.csv")  # Run the column report hm_col_report(hunt_data) ── HealthMarkers column report ────────────────────────────────────────────  Data: 56301 rows × 187 columns   |   Keys in dictionary: 258   key                  data_column              how matched  -------------------- ------------------------ ------------------  age                  alder                    col contains synonym ✔  sex                  kjonn                    exact ✔  height               hoyde                    exact ✔  fasting_glucose      fastende_blodsukker      col contains synonym ✔  TG                   triglyserider            col contains synonym ✔  creatinine           kreatinin                exact ✔  SBP                  systolisk_blodtrykk      col contains synonym ✔  eGFR                 ─                        NOT FOUND ✘   ✔ 142 keys matched   ✘ 116 keys not found  ── col_map template for missing keys ──────────────────────────────────────  col_map <- list(    eGFR = "from_your_data",  # fill in your column name  ) # Capture the auto-detected map and add manual overrides cm <- hm_col_report(hunt_data, verbose = FALSE) cm$eGFR <- "ckd_epi_gfr_ml_min"   # fill in the actual column name  results <- all_health_markers(hunt_data, col_map = cm,                               which = c("glycemic", "lipid", "renal",                                         "inflammatory", "liver"))

title: Multi-Biobank Compatibility: Automatic Column Recognition
text: Even raw computed inputs data, HealthMarkers can derive automatically basic measurements marker computation begins. following secondary variables auto-derived: means, example, kidney_failure_risk() work even eGFR absent data, long creatinine, age, sex present.

what: Example: running on UK Biobank data
title: Multi-Biobank Compatibility: Automatic Column Recognition
code: library(HealthMarkers)  # Load UKB extract (columns follow _0_0 naming) ukb <- readRDS("ukb_pheno.rds")  # Check column matching — most UKB columns are auto-detected hm_col_report(ukb)  # Run a broad panel — no col_map needed for UKB standard names results <- all_health_markers(   data    = ukb,   which   = c("glycemic", "lipid", "liver", "renal",               "inflammatory", "obesity_metrics", "vitamin"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Multi-Biobank Compatibility: Automatic Column Recognition
code: # NHANES lab data (LBXGLU, LBXSCH, LBXWBCSI, etc.) nhanes_lab <- read.csv("nhanes_lab.csv") nhanes_bp  <- read.csv("nhanes_bp.csv") nhanes_dem <- read.csv("nhanes_demo.csv")  nhanes <- nhanes_lab |>   dplyr::left_join(nhanes_bp,  by = "SEQN") |>   dplyr::left_join(nhanes_dem, by = "SEQN")  results <- all_health_markers(nhanes, which = c("glycemic", "lipid",                                                  "renal", "inflammatory"),                                verbose = FALSE)

what: Example: running on OMOP / All of Us data (LOINC codes)
title: Multi-Biobank Compatibility: Automatic Column Recognition
code: # Columns named LOINC_2345_7, LOINC_2160_0, LOINC_718_7, etc. omop_labs <- dplyr::collect(tbl(con, "measurement_wide"))  # All LOINC_XXXX_X columns are recognised automatically hm_col_report(omop_labs)  results <- all_health_markers(omop_labs,                               which = c("glycemic", "lipid", "renal",                                         "inflammatory", "vitamin"))

title: Multi-Biobank Compatibility: Automatic Column Recognition
text: biobank uses names already dictionary, two options: Option — explicit col_map (immediate, code changes): Option B — open GitHub issue / pull request add permanent recognition naming system dictionary: https://github.com/sufyansuleman/HealthMarkers/issues Please include: biobank name, analyte/variable, column name(s) used.
code: my_col_map <- list(   fasting_glucose  = "p_glu_0",   total_cholesterol = "tot_kol",   creatinine       = "s_krea",   age              = "alder_bij_opname",   sex              = "geslacht_f" )  results <- all_health_markers(data, col_map = my_col_map,                               which = c("glycemic", "lipid", "renal"))

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
text: Incorporate neurofilament light chain (NfL) concentration measurement analysis pipeline. NfL biomarker neuroaxonal injury; elevated plasma CSF levels indicate neurodegeneration typically increase age neurological disease. function validates passes input values (assumed plasma pg/mL) optional NA handling, returning tidy tibble ready downstream analysis.

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   NfL = c(8.5, 14.2, 22.1, NA, 35.0) )

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
text: required key nfl.
code: cm <- list(nfl = "NfL")

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
code: out <- nfl_marker(   data = df,   col_map = cm,   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE )  out #> # A tibble: 5 × 1 #>   nfl_value #>       <dbl> #> 1       8.5 #> 2      14.2 #> 3      22.1 #> 4      NA   #> 5      35

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
text: na_action = "keep" preserves rows NA outputs; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts required input missing; warn keeps rows emits warning.
code: df_na <- tibble::tibble(NfL = c(8.5, NA, 22.1))  keep_out <- nfl_marker(df_na, cm, na_action = "keep") omit_out <- nfl_marker(df_na, cm, na_action = "omit")  list(keep_rows = nrow(keep_out), omit_rows = nrow(omit_out)) #> $keep_rows #> [1] 3 #>  #> $omit_rows #> [1] 2

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
text: Negative implausibly high NfL values passed -. Pre-filter calling.
code: df_ext <- tibble::tibble(NfL = c(8.5, -5, 22.1))  # negative value is implausible; pre-filter # Pre-filter: df_ext$NfL[df_ext$NfL < 0] <- NA nfl_marker(df_ext, col_map = cm, na_action = "keep") #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>   nfl_value #>       <dbl> #> 1       8.5 #> 2      NA   #> 3      22.1

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
text: Set verbose = TRUE (healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") surface three structured messages call: preparing inputs, column map, results summary.
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") nfl_marker(tibble::tibble(NfL = c(8.5, 14.2, 22.1)), cm, verbose = TRUE) #> nfl_marker(): reading input 'data' — 3 rows × 1 variables #> nfl_marker(): col_map (1 column — 1 specified) #>   nfl               ->  'NfL' #> nfl_marker(): computing markers: #>   nfl_value  [passthrough of NfL input] #> nfl_marker(): results: nfl_value 3/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>   nfl_value #>       <dbl> #> 1       8.5 #> 2      14.2 #> 3      22.1 options(old_opt)

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
text: Run hm_col_report(your_data) check analyte columns auto-detected building col_map. See Multi-Biobank Compatibility article recognised synonyms across major biobanks.
code: hm_col_report(your_data)

title: Neurofilament light chain (NfL) marker
text: Units preserved -; ensure values unit (e.g., pg/mL) calling. Age-adjusted reference ranges applied function; use downstream tools stratified z-scores age normalisation. batch processing imputed datasets, combine impute_missing() upstream handle systematic missingness running nfl_marker().

title: Nutrient Markers
text: Compute nutrient-related ratios/products (FerritinTS, AGR, Omega3Index, Mg_Cr_Ratio, GlycatedAlbuminPct, UA_Cr_Ratio, BUN_Cr_Ratio, Ca_x_Phosphate, AnionGap, Tyr_Phe_Ratio) NA/extreme handling safe divisions; unit conversion.

title: Nutrient Markers
text: routine nutrient labs (iron, protein, omega-3, renal, electrolytes, amino acids) want quick derived ratios. need built-NA policies, high-missingness diagnostics, optional extreme-value scanning/capping. can supply inputs expected units accept missing inputs return NA dependent markers.

title: Nutrient Markers
text: data: data frame/tibble containing subset recognized inputs. col_map: optional named list mapping keys (defaults identity): ferritin, transferrin_sat, albumin, total_protein, EPA, DHA, Mg, creatinine, glycated_albumin, uric_acid, BUN, phosphate, calcium, Na, K, Cl, HCO3, Tyr, Phe. Unrecognized keys ignored. Non-numeric values coerced warnings; non-finite set NA. na_action: keep (default) propagates NA; omit drops rows NA used inputs; error aborts used inputs contain NA. na_warn_prop: threshold high-missingness diagnostics (default 0.2; shown verbose/debug). verbose: optional progress summary logging.

title: Nutrient Markers
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   ferritin = c(50, 100), transferrin_sat = c(30, 50),   albumin = c(45, 40), total_protein = c(70, 75),   EPA = c(2.0, 2.5), DHA = c(4.0, 4.5),   Mg = c(0.85, 0.90), creatinine = c(80, 90),   glycated_albumin = c(12, 14), uric_acid = c(300, 400), BUN = c(14, 16),   phosphate = c(1.0, 1.2), calcium = c(2.3, 2.4),   Na = c(140, 138), K = c(4.2, 4.0), Cl = c(100, 102), HCO3 = c(24, 26),   Tyr = c(60, 70), Phe = c(50, 55) )  nutrient_markers(   data = df,   col_map = NULL,   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>   FerritinTS   AGR Omega3Index Mg_Cr_Ratio GlycatedAlbuminPct UA_Cr_Ratio #>        <dbl> <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>              <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1       1.67  1.8            6      0.0106               26.7        3.75 #> 2       2     1.14           7      0.01                 35          4.44 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: BUN_Cr_Ratio <dbl>, Ca_x_Phosphate <dbl>, AnionGap <dbl>, #> #   Tyr_Phe_Ratio <dbl>

title: Nutrient Markers
text: Extreme inputs produce extreme derived markers. Pre-filter implausible values calling.
code: # Pre-filter extreme ferritin df_clean <- df df_clean$ferritin[df_clean$ferritin > 2000] <- NA  nutrient_markers(   data = df_clean,   col_map = NULL,   na_action = "omit",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>   FerritinTS   AGR Omega3Index Mg_Cr_Ratio GlycatedAlbuminPct UA_Cr_Ratio #>        <dbl> <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>              <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1       1.67  1.8            6      0.0106               26.7        3.75 #> 2       2     1.14           7      0.01                 35          4.44 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: BUN_Cr_Ratio <dbl>, Ca_x_Phosphate <dbl>, AnionGap <dbl>, #> #   Tyr_Phe_Ratio <dbl>

title: Nutrient Markers
code: try(   nutrient_markers(     data = df,     col_map = NULL,     na_action = "error"   ) ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>   FerritinTS   AGR Omega3Index Mg_Cr_Ratio GlycatedAlbuminPct UA_Cr_Ratio #>        <dbl> <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>              <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1       1.67  1.8            6      0.0106               26.7        3.75 #> 2       2     1.14           7      0.01                 35          4.44 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: BUN_Cr_Ratio <dbl>, Ca_x_Phosphate <dbl>, AnionGap <dbl>, #> #   Tyr_Phe_Ratio <dbl>

title: Nutrient Markers
text: Returns tibble : FerritinTS, AGR, Omega3Index, Mg_Cr_Ratio, GlycatedAlbuminPct, UA_Cr_Ratio, BUN_Cr_Ratio, Ca_x_Phosphate, AnionGap, Tyr_Phe_Ratio. marker computed inputs present; otherwise NA returned marker. Zero/non-finite denominators set NA consolidated warnings; unit harmonization applied.

title: Nutrient Markers
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") nutrient_markers(   data = df,   col_map = NULL,   verbose = TRUE ) #> nutrient_markers(): reading input 'df' — 2 rows × 19 variables #> nutrient_markers(): col_map (19 columns — 19 inferred from data) #>   ferritin          ->  'ferritin'    (inferred) #>   transferrin_sat   ->  'transferrin_sat'    (inferred) #>   albumin
