title: Nutrient Markers
text: Run hm_col_report(your_data) check analyte columns auto-detected building col_map. See Multi-Biobank Compatibility article recognised synonyms across major biobanks.
code: hm_col_report(your_data)

title: Nutrient Markers
text: Provide inputs ; missing inputs yield NA dependent markers. Omega3Index expects EPA/DHA percentages; AGR uses globulin = total_protein - albumin. Use na_action = "omit" prefer row-complete outputs; use keep QA inspect missingness.

title: Obesity Indices
text: Compute anthropometric adiposity/shape indices (BMI + category, WHR/WHtR, AVI, BAI, ABSI, BRI, Conicity Index, waist/BMI weight/height ratios, optional WHRadjBMI RFM) unit conversion, NA/extreme handling, safe-division guards.

title: Obesity Indices
text: weight/height/waist/hip (optionally sex) want comprehensive set body composition proxies one call. need built-unit conversion (kg/lb, cm/m) NA policy controls. want optional BMI-adjusted WHR Relative Fat Mass sex coding available.

title: Obesity Indices
text: data: data frame/tibble numeric columns weight, height, waist, hip; sex needed include_RFM = TRUE (coded 0=male, 1=female). Column arguments unquoted: weight, height, waist, hip, optional sex. Units: weight kg (lb weight_unit = "lb"), height m (cm height_unit = "cm"), waist/hip cm. weight_unit / height_unit: convert kg/m internally. adjust_WHR: add BMI-adjusted WHR residuals. include_RFM: compute Relative Fat Mass (requires sex). na_action: keep (default) propagates NA; omit drops rows missing required inputs; error aborts required inputs contain NA. na_warn_prop: threshold high-missingness debug messages (default 0.2). verbose: optional progress summary logging.

title: Obesity Indices
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   wt    = c(70, 80),   # kg   ht    = c(175, 165), # cm   waist = c(80, 90),   # cm   hip   = c(100, 95),  # cm   sex   = c(0, 1) )  obesity_indices(   data = df,   weight = wt,   height = ht,   waist = waist,   hip = hip,   sex = sex,   weight_unit = "kg",   height_unit = "cm",   adjust_WHR = TRUE,   include_RFM = TRUE,   na_action = "keep",   verbose = FALSE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 15 #>   weight_kg height_m   BMI BMI_cat         WHR WHRadjBMI waist_to_height_ratio #>       <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <chr>         <dbl>     <dbl>                 <dbl> #> 1        70     1.75  22.9 Normal weight 0.8           0                  45.7 #> 2        80     1.65  29.4 Overweight    0.947         0                  54.5 #> # ℹ 8 more variables: waist_to_BMI_ratio <dbl>, weight_to_height_ratio <dbl>, #> #   AVI <dbl>, BAI <dbl>, ABSI <dbl>, BRI <dbl>, CI <dbl>, RFM <dbl>

title: Obesity Indices
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") obesity_indices(   data = df,   weight = wt,   height = ht,   waist = waist,   hip = hip,   sex = sex,   weight_unit = "kg",   height_unit = "cm",   adjust_WHR = FALSE,   include_RFM = TRUE,   na_action = "omit",   verbose = TRUE ) #> obesity_indices(): reading input 'df' — 2 rows × 5 variables #> obesity_indices(): col_map: weight -> 'wt', height -> 'ht', waist -> 'waist', hip -> 'hip', sex -> 'sex' #> obesity_indices(): computing markers: #>   BMI, BMI_cat, WHR, WHtR, AVI, BAI, ABSI, BRI, CI, RFM #> obesity_indices(): omitting 0 rows with NA in required inputs #> obesity_indices(): results: weight_kg 2/2, height_m 2/2, BMI 2/2, BMI_cat 2/2, WHR 2/2, waist_to_height_ratio 2/2, waist_to_BMI_ratio 2/2, weight_to_height_ratio 2/2, AVI 2/2, BAI 2/2, ABSI 2/2, BRI 2/2, CI 2/2, RFM 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 14 #>   weight_kg height_m   BMI BMI_cat         WHR waist_to_height_ratio #>       <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <chr>         <dbl>                 <dbl> #> 1        70     1.75  22.9 Normal weight 0.8                    45.7 #> 2        80     1.65  29.4 Overweight    0.947                  54.5 #> # ℹ 8 more variables: waist_to_BMI_ratio <dbl>, weight_to_height_ratio <dbl>, #> #   AVI <dbl>, BAI <dbl>, ABSI <dbl>, BRI <dbl>, CI <dbl>, RFM <dbl> options(old_opt)

title: Obesity Indices
code: try(   obesity_indices(     data = df,     weight = wt,     height = ht,     waist = waist,     hip = hip,     na_action = "error"   ) ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 13 #>   weight_kg height_m   BMI BMI_cat         WHR waist_to_height_ratio #>       <dbl>    <dbl> <dbl> <chr>         <dbl>                 <dbl> #> 1        70     1.75  22.9 Normal weight 0.8                    45.7 #> 2        80     1.65  29.4 Overweight    0.947                  54.5 #> # ℹ 7 more variables: waist_to_BMI_ratio <dbl>, weight_to_height_ratio <dbl>, #> #   AVI <dbl>, BAI <dbl>, ABSI <dbl>, BRI <dbl>, CI <dbl>

title: Obesity Indices
text: Adds: weight_kg, height_m, BMI, BMI_cat, WHR, waist_to_height_ratio, AVI, BAI, ABSI, BRI, CI, waist_to_BMI_ratio, weight_to_height_ratio, optional WHRadjBMI, optional RFM. Zero denominators yield NA warnings; WHRadjBMI requires variance WHR BMI; RFM requires sex coded 0/1.

title: Obesity Indices
text: Set weight/height units correctly; waist/hip assumed cm. Enable include_RFM = TRUE valid sex coding; leave FALSE otherwise. WHRadjBMI needs variation WHR BMI; otherwise returns NA warning. Use na_action = "omit" row-complete outputs; keep QA see missingness.

title: OGTT Insulin Sensitivity
text: Compute OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices (Matsuda, Cederholm, Gutt, Stumvoll variants, BIGTT, HIRI_inv, Belfiore, etc.) glucose/insulin 0/30/120 minutes plus weight, BMI, age, sex. Includes validation, missingness policies, optional normalization returned indices.

title: OGTT Insulin Sensitivity
text: OGTT glucose (mmol/L) insulin (pmol/L) 0, 30, 120 minutes, plus weight, BMI, age, sex, need multiple published insulin sensitivity indices. want consistent NA handling optional normalization modeling pipelines.

title: OGTT Insulin Sensitivity
text: Units: Inputs must already mmol/L (glucose) pmol/L (insulin); weight kg; BMI kg/m^2; age years; sex coded 1/2 (0/1 coerced). function internally converts glucose mg/dL (x18) insulin muU/mL (/6) specific formulas require .

title: OGTT Insulin Sensitivity
text: Validation: required columns must exist; non-numeric inputs coerced warnings; non-finite values become NA. Missingness: keep propagates NA; omit drops rows required NA; error aborts required inputs contain NA (computation). High-missing diagnostics: na_warn_prop controls debug/verbose messages missingness; enable verbose = TRUE see . Unit handling: inputs expected mmol/L (glucose) pmol/L (insulin); internally converted mg/dL (*18) muU/mL (/6) formulas need . Normalization (optional): applied via normalize_vec(); use “none” raw interpretable values. Sex coding: expects 1 = male, 2 = female; 0 treated female coercion; strings become NA.

title: OGTT Insulin Sensitivity
text: Returns tibble : Isi_120, Cederholm_index, Gutt_index, Avignon_Si0, Avignon_Si120, Avignon_Sim, Modified_stumvoll, Stumvoll_Demographics, Matsuda_AUC, Matsuda_ISI, BigttSi, Ifc_inv, HIRI_inv, Belfiore_isi_gly. NA indicates missing/invalid inputs, zero/negative arguments logs, values blanked normalization NA inputs.

title: OGTT Insulin Sensitivity
text: Output screening happens normalization; outputs normalized normalize != "none". High-missingness diagnostics printed verbose/debug logging . Zero/negative inputs log-based terms become NA design (safe log guard).

title: OGTT Insulin Sensitivity
text: Interpretation: Returns indices NA inputs missing non-finite.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   G0 = 5.5, I0 = 60,   G30 = 7.8, I30 = 90,   G120 = 6.2, I120 = 50,   weight = 70, bmi = 24, age = 30, sex = 1 )  ogtt_is(   data = df,   col_map = list(     G0 = "G0", I0 = "I0",     G30 = "G30", I30 = "I30",     G120 = "G120", I120 = "I120",     weight = "weight", bmi = "bmi",     age = "age", sex = "sex"   ),   normalize = "none",   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 14 #>   Isi_120 Cederholm_index Gutt_index Avignon_Si0 Avignon_Si120 Avignon_Sim #>     <dbl>           <dbl>      <dbl>       <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1    10.8            1.10       2.67        9.62          10.2        9.93 #> # ℹ 8 more variables: Modified_stumvoll <dbl>, Stumvoll_Demographics <dbl>, #> #   Matsuda_AUC <dbl>, Matsuda_ISI <dbl>, BigttSi <dbl>, Ifc_inv <dbl>, #> #   HIRI_inv <dbl>, Belfiore_isi_gly <dbl>

title: OGTT Insulin Sensitivity
text: Interpretation: Outputs z-scaled; verbose logs summarize column-map resolution result counts.
code: ogtt_is(   data = df,   col_map = list(     G0 = "G0", I0 = "I0",     G30 = "G30", I30 = "I30",     G120 = "G120", I120 = "I120",     weight = "weight", bmi = "bmi",     age = "age", sex = "sex"   ),   normalize = "z",   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 14 #>   Isi_120 Cederholm_index Gutt_index Avignon_Si0 Avignon_Si120 Avignon_Sim #>     <dbl>           <dbl>      <dbl>       <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1       0               0          0           0             0           0 #> # ℹ 8 more variables: Modified_stumvoll <dbl>, Stumvoll_Demographics <dbl>, #> #   Matsuda_AUC <dbl>, Matsuda_ISI <dbl>, BigttSi <dbl>, Ifc_inv <dbl>, #> #   HIRI_inv <dbl>, Belfiore_isi_gly <dbl>

title: OGTT Insulin Sensitivity
text: Missing YAML: ensure header stays top; Markdown break knitting. Missing columns: every required col_map key must exist data mapped. Units: inputs must mmol/L (glucose) pmol/L (insulin); internal conversions applied formulas. Sex coding: expects 1/2; 0/1 coerced; strings become NA propagate. NA outputs: usually missing required inputs, zero/negative values passed logs, NA produced normalization NA inputs.

title: OGTT Insulin Sensitivity

text: Input validation: Non-numeric columns coerced; warnings issued NAs introduced. Non-finite values become NA. na_action = "keep": Output NA rows missing GSH GSSG. na_action = "omit": Rows missing GSH GSSG dropped. na_action = "error": Function aborts required value missing. Division zero: GSSG zero non-finite, ratio set NA warning. Verbose output: Shows counts omitted/kept rows NA ratios troubleshooting. title: Psychometric markers
text: Compute common self-report mental health scales (PHQ-9/8, GAD-7, K6/K10, GHQ-12, -5, ISI, MDQ, ASRS), key-driven scales (BIS, SPQ), cognitive composites, diagnosis/medication flags one call via psych_markers(). NA handling (keep/omit/error) rowwise mean-imputation built , per-scale missingness thresholds.

title: Psychometric markers
text: Map canonical item IDs dataset columns. Keys BIS/SPQ stay user-supplied (item text package).
code: col_map <- list(   phq9 = list(items = setNames(sprintf("phq9_%02d", 1:9), sprintf("phq9_%02d", 1:9))),   gad7 = list(items = setNames(sprintf("gad7_%02d", 1:7), sprintf("gad7_%02d", 1:7))),   k6   = list(items = setNames(sprintf("k6_%02d", 1:6), sprintf("k6_%02d", 1:6))),   k10  = list(items = setNames(sprintf("k10_%02d", 1:10), sprintf("k10_%02d", 1:10))),   ghq12 = list(items = setNames(sprintf("ghq12_%02d", 1:12), sprintf("ghq12_%02d", 1:12))),   who5 = list(items = setNames(sprintf("who5_%02d", 1:5), sprintf("who5_%02d", 1:5))),   isi  = list(items = setNames(sprintf("isi_%02d", 1:7), sprintf("isi_%02d", 1:7))),   mdq  = list(items = setNames(sprintf("mdq_%02d", 1:13), sprintf("mdq_%02d", 1:13)), cluster = "mdq_cluster", impairment = "mdq_impair"),   asrs = list(items = setNames(sprintf("asrs_%02d", 1:18), sprintf("asrs_%02d", 1:18))),   bis  = list(items = setNames(sprintf("bis_%02d", 1:12), sprintf("bis_%02d", 1:12))),   spq  = list(items = setNames(sprintf("spq_%02d", 1:4), sprintf("spq_%02d", 1:4))),   cognitive = list(tasks = list(rt = "task_rt", memory = "task_mem", attention = "task_att")),   dx_flags = list(dx = list(mdd = "dx_mdd", anxiety = "dx_anxiety", adhd = "dx_adhd", bipolar = "dx_bipolar", scz = "dx_scz", sud = "dx_sud")),   med_flags = list(med = list(ssri = "med_ssri", snri = "med_snri", antipsychotic = "med_antipsychotic", mood_stabilizer = "med_mood_stabilizer", anxiolytic = "med_anxiolytic")) )  bis_key <- list(   name = "BIS_demo",   items = sprintf("bis_%02d", 1:12),   min_val = 1, max_val = 4,   reverse = c("bis_02","bis_03"),   subscales = list(attention = c("bis_01","bis_05"), motor = c("bis_02","bis_06")) )  spq_key <- list(   name = "SPQ_demo",   items = sprintf("spq_%02d", 1:4),   min_val = 0, max_val = 1 )

title: Psychometric markers
text: Select scales want via . Rowwise mean imputation optional; keep defaults missingness tolerance 20% per scale.
code: out <- psych_markers(   data = psych_df,   col_map = col_map,   which = c("phq9","gad7","k6","k10","ghq12_binary","who5","isi","mdq","asrs","bis","spq","cognitive","dx_flags","med_flags"),   na_action = "keep",   missing_prop_max = 0.2,   impute = "mean",   bis_key = bis_key,   spq_key = spq_key,   cognitive_method = "z_mean" )  new_cols <- setdiff(names(out), names(psych_df)) dplyr::select(out, dplyr::all_of(new_cols)) #> # A tibble: 1 × 33 #>   PHQ9_total PHQ9_severity GAD7_total GAD7_severity K6_total K6_case K10_total #>        <dbl> <fct>              <dbl> <fct>            <dbl> <lgl>       <dbl> #> 1         11 moderate               4 minimal              9 FALSE          10 #> # ℹ 26 more variables: GHQ12_total_binary <dbl>, GHQ12_case_binary <lgl>, #> #   WHO5_raw <dbl>, WHO5_percent <dbl>, WHO5_low_wellbeing <lgl>, #> #   ISI_total <dbl>, ISI_severity <fct>, MDQ_symptom_count <dbl>, #> #   MDQ_clustering <lgl>, MDQ_impairment <lgl>, MDQ_positive_screen <lgl>, #> #   ASRS_total <dbl>, ASRS_partA_count <dbl>, ASRS_partA_positive <lgl>, #> #   BIS_total <dbl>, BIS_attention <dbl>, BIS_motor <dbl>, SPQ_total <dbl>, #> #   cog_z_mean <dbl>, dx_any_psych <lgl>, dx_internalizing <lgl>, …

title: Psychometric markers
text: na_action: keep (default), omit (drops rows NA new outputs), error. missing_prop_max: rows higher missing proportion per scale set NA scale. impute = "mean" applies rowwise mean-imputation scoring; use "none" skip. BIS/SPQ require user-supplied keys; item text ships package. Cognitive composite supports method = "z_mean" "pca1" (complete cases PCA loadings).

title: Pulmonary Markers
text: Compute spirometry-based pulmonary markers using GLI/GLIgl/NHANES3 reference equations rspiro: predicted normals, z-scores, percent-predicted, LLN FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC ratio. Handles missing-data policies, basic height unit detection, verbose diagnostics.

title: Pulmonary Markers
text: need standardized pulmonary markers (predicted, z, percent-predicted, LLN) FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC using established reference equations. dataset age/sex/height/ethnicity plus measured FEV1 FVC (liters). want controlled handling missingness clear warnings implausible inputs (e.g., unmapped sex/ethnicity, zero/negative heights).

title: Pulmonary Markers
text: Required columns (names must match exactly): age (years), sex (“male”/“female” 1/2 0/1), height (cm m; auto-detected), ethnicity (text), fev1 (L), fvc (L). col_map needed; use names. Units: FEV1/FVC liters. Height auto-detected cm value > 3; otherwise treated meters. unit conversions performed.

title: Pulmonary Markers
text: Validation & coercion: required columns checked; age/height/fev1/fvc coerced numeric warnings NAs introduced. Missingness: keep = propagate NA; omit = drop rows required NA; error = abort required NA. Height handling: height > 3 treated cm divided 100; non-positive heights warn yield NA outputs. Sex/Ethnicity mapping: case-insensitive mapping; unmapped values warn become NA (sex) /Mixed (ethnicity code 5). Division zero/non-finite: ratios set NA denominator zero non-finite. Dependency: requires rspiro; aborts missing chosen equation unsupported.

title: Pulmonary Markers
text: Tibble (rows follow na_action) : - fev1_pred, fev1_z, fev1_pctpred, fev1_LLN - fvc_pred, fvc_z, fvc_pctpred, fvc_LLN - fev1_fvc_ratio, fev1_fvc_pred, fev1_fvc_z, fev1_fvc_pctpred, fev1_fvc_LLN (LLN currently NA placeholder) Values numeric; NA inputs missing/invalid division--zero occurred.

title: Pulmonary Markers
text: Interpretation: Mixed height units handled; missingness (none ) propagate. Outputs include predicted, z, percent-predicted, ratios.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   age = c(45, 62, 30),   sex = c("male", "female", "female"),   height = c(178, 162, 1.70),  # mixed cm/m accepted (auto-detect)   ethnicity = c("Caucasian", "African-American", "Other"),   fev1 = c(2.8, 1.4, 3.0),   fvc  = c(3.5, 2.1, 3.8) )  pulmo_markers(   data = df,   equation = "GLI",   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 13 #>   fev1_pred    fev1_z fev1_pctpred fev1_LLN fvc_pred   fvc_z fvc_pctpred fvc_LLN #>       <dbl>     <dbl>        <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1  4.09         -2.42         68.5 3.22     5.14     -2.51e0        68.0 4.06e+0 #> 2  2.09         -1.95         67.0 1.51     2.64     -1.24e0        79.6 1.93e+0 #> 3  0.000188 512779.      1592155.  0.000151 0.000115  5.42e4   3303233.  9.31e-5 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: fev1_fvc_ratio <dbl>, fev1_fvc_pred <dbl>, #> #   fev1_fvc_z <dbl>, fev1_fvc_pctpred <dbl>, fev1_fvc_LLN <dbl>

title: Renal Markers
text: Compute renal function, excretion, injury markers: CKD-EPI 2009 creatinine eGFR (race factor retained), optional cystatin C combined eGFR, BUN/Cr ratio, fractional excretion urea (FE_Urea), plus pass-urine injury markers (NGAL, KIM1, NAG, Beta2Micro, IL18, L_FABP). Includes mapping validation, missingness policies, optional extreme screening/capping, zero-denominator warnings. unit conversion performed.

title: Renal Markers
text: need eGFR (creatinine ± cystatin C) FE_Urea renal function AKI assessment. data includes required labs (creatinine, age, sex, race, BUN) optionally cystatin C urine assays. want clear handling missingness, extreme-value screening, transparent NA propagation.

title: Renal Markers
text: Required columns (col_map): creatinine (mg/dL), age (years), sex (1/0 “male”/“female”), race (aliases white/black/), BUN (mg/dL). Optional columns: cystatin_C (mg/L), urea_serum (mg/dL), creatinine_urine (mg/dL), urea_urine (mg/dL), NGAL, KIM1, NAG, beta2_micro (outputs Beta2Micro), IL18, L_FABP. Units: conversion. Serum creatinine/BUN/urea mg/dL; cystatin C mg/L; urine creatinine/urea mg/dL. FE_Urea requires urea_serum + creatinine_urine + urea_urine.

title: Renal Markers
text: Validation & coercion: required columns must exist; numeric coercion applied warnings NAs introduced. Missingness: keep = propagate NA; omit = drop rows required NA; error = abort required NA. High-missingness diagnostics: na_warn_prop governs debug warnings appear (verbose/debug). Zero denominators: divisions NA consolidated warning (e.g., BUN/Cr, FE_Urea components). Race factor: CKD-EPI 2009 race coefficient retained (race-free applied ). Injury markers: NGAL, KIM1, NAG, beta2_micro, IL18, L_FABP passed .numeric without transformation range checks.

title: Renal Markers
text: Sex mapping: accepts 1/0, 1/2, male/female strings; unmapped values become NA warning. Race mapping: common aliases map “black” “white”; everything else becomes “” (NA). Zero denominators: consolidated warning lists ratios hit zero (e.g., BUN_Cr_ratio, FE_Urea components) many. NA diagnostics: high-missingness messages emitted debug/verbose levels; set verbose = TRUE QC see . Empty result: na_action = "omit" removes rows, returns zero-row tibble expected columns.

title: Renal Markers
text: Returns tibble (rows follow na_action). Columns: eGFR_cr, eGFR_cys (cystatin_C provided), eGFR_combined (Cys provided), BUN_Cr_ratio, FE_Urea (needs urea_serum + creatinine_urine + urea_urine), NGAL, KIM1, NAG, Beta2Micro, IL18, L_FABP (pass-mapped). NA inputs missing/invalid, denominators zero, optional inputs absent.

code: df2 <- tibble::tibble(   Cr = c(0.9, 2.2, 18),   Age = c(55, 68, 50),   Sex = c("male", "female", "male"),   Race = c("white", "other", "black"),   BUN = c(16, 40, 210),   Cys = c(1.0, 1.4, 0.6),   UreaS = c(28, 35, 18),   CrU = c(120, 95, 80),   UreaU = c(480, 520, 300),   NGAL = c(20, 35, 15) )  renal_markers(   data = df2,   col_map = list(     creatinine = "Cr",     age = "Age",     sex = "Sex",     race = "Race",     BUN = "BUN",     cystatin_C = "Cys",     urea_serum = "UreaS",     creatinine_urine = "CrU",     urea_urine = "UreaU",     NGAL = "NGAL"   ),   na_action = "omit",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 11 #>   eGFR_cr eGFR_cys eGFR_combined BUN_Cr_ratio FE_Urea  NGAL  KIM1   NAG #>     <dbl>    <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1   95.8      79.3          87.4         17.8    12.9    20    NA    NA #> 2   21.9      44.9          32.4         18.2    34.4    35    NA    NA #> 3    3.07    126.           22.4         11.7   375      15    NA    NA #> # ℹ 3 more variables: Beta2Micro <dbl>, IL18 <dbl>, L_FABP <dbl>

title: Renal Markers
text: Missing columns: ensure required col_map entries exist mapped columns data. Units: conversion; convert upstream (mg/dL creatinine/BUN/urea, mg/L cystatin C). Race factor: CKD-EPI 2009 race coefficient retained; interpret accordingly. Zero denominators: check zeros creatinine urea_serum/urea_urine; warnings list counts. NA outputs: often missing required inputs, unmapped sex/race, missing cystatin_C expecting combined eGFR.

Set verbose = TRUE QC see omission counts, extreme handling, zero-denominator diagnostics. FE_Urea requires urea_serum, creatinine_urine, urea_urine; otherwise NA.

title: Renal Markers
text: Check: BUN_Cr_ratio = BUN / creatinine; FE_Urea = 100 * (urea_urine/urea_serum) / (creatinine_urine/creatinine) finite. eGFR_cr declines higher creatinine older age; race factor increases eGFR Black race (CKD-EPI 2009).

title: Saliva Markers
text: Compute saliva-based stress glycemic markers: safe-log waking cortisol, cortisol awakening response AUC (trapezoidal 3 samples), safe-log salivary alpha-amylase, pass-salivary glucose. Includes mapping validation, missingness policies, optional extreme screening/capping inputs, safe handling non-positive values logs.

title: Saliva Markers
text: three salivary cortisol measures around wake (e.g., 0/30/60 minutes) want CAR AUC log cortisol. also collect salivary alpha-amylase (stress marker) /salivary glucose need cleaned, log-safe outputs. want explicit NA policies, optional extreme truncation, flexible sampling times AUC.

title: Saliva Markers
text: Required columns (col_map): cort1, cort2, cort3 (cortisol nmol/L), amylase (U/mL), glucose (mg/dL). Mapped columns must exist. Units: conversion performed. Supply cortisol nmol/L, alpha-amylase U/mL, salivary glucose mg/dL.

title: Saliva Markers
text: Validation & coercion: required columns must exist; non-numeric coerced warnings NAs introduced; non-finite become NA. Missingness: keep propagates NA; omit drops rows required NA; error aborts required inputs contain NA. High-missing diagnostics: controlled na_warn_prop; shown verbose (debug) enabled. Safe logs: cortisol amylase log-transformed setting non-positive NA. CAR AUC: trapezoidal three cortisol values using times; non-finite cortisol triplet yields NA row. Times: must numeric length 3, non-decreasing; customize protocol. unit conversion: ensure assays consistent units calling.

title: Saliva Markers
text: NA diagnostics appear verbose/debug logging; set verbose = TRUE QC. Empty result: na_action = "omit" drops rows, returns zero-row tibble expected columns.

title: Saliva Markers
text: Returns tibble (rows follow na_action) : log_cortisol_wake, CAR_AUC, log_amylase, saliva_glucose. NA indicates missing/invalid inputs, non-positive values log, NA cortisol CAR_AUC, values dropped via na_action.

what: Worked example 1: Standard times, keep NAs
title: Saliva Markers
text: Interpretation: CAR_AUC log cortisol/amylase compute inputs present positive; third row yields NA log_cortisol_wake CAR_AUC due missing cort1.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   saliva_cort1 = c(12.5, 9.0, NA),   saliva_cort2 = c(18.0, 11.0, 7.5),   saliva_cort3 = c(16.2, 10.5, 6.8),   saliva_amylase = c(85, 120, 95),   saliva_glucose = c(4.2, 5.0, 4.8) )  saliva_markers(   data = df,   col_map = list(     cort1 = "saliva_cort1",     cort2 = "saliva_cort2",     cort3 = "saliva_cort3",     amylase = "saliva_amylase",     glucose = "saliva_glucose"   ),   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   log_cortisol_wake CAR_AUC log_amylase saliva_glucose #>               <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1              2.53    970.        4.44            4.2 #> 2              2.20    622.        4.79            5   #> 3             NA        NA         4.55            4.8

what: Worked example 2: Custom times, drop incomplete
title: Saliva Markers
text: Interpretation: Rows required NAs dropped; CAR_AUC uses custom times (0,20,50).
code: df2 <- tibble::tibble(   c1 = c(14, 0, 2200),    # zero and extreme cortisol; pre-filter   c2 = c(19, 12, 2100),   c3 = c(17, 11, 2050),   amy = c(90, 70, 60000),  # extreme amylase; pre-filter   glu = c(4.5, 5.1, 7.0) )  # Pre-filter implausible values before calling df2$c1[df2$c1 > 2000] <- NA df2$amy[df2$amy > 50000] <- NA  saliva_markers(   data = df2,   col_map = list(cort1 = "c1", cort2 = "c2", cort3 = "c3", amylase = "amy", glucose = "glu"),   times = c(0, 20, 50),   na_action = "omit",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>   log_cortisol_wake CAR_AUC log_amylase saliva_glucose #>               <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1              2.64     870        4.50            4.5 #> 2             NA        465        4.25            5.1

title: Saliva Markers
text: Missing columns: ensure every required col_map key mapped existing column. Non-positive cortisol/amylase: become NA logging; expect NA outputs values 0 negative. Times validation: must numeric length 3 non-decreasing; otherwise function aborts. NA outputs: typically due missing cortisol triplets, non-positive cortisol/amylase, aggressive na_action = "omit".

title: Saliva Markers
text: CAR_AUC trapezoidal: ∑=12ci+ci+12×(ti+1−ti)\sum_{=1}^{2} \frac{c_i + c_{+1}}{2} \times (t_{+1} - t_i) using three cortisol values times gaps. Safe logs: log(x)\log(x) taken x>0x > 0 finite; otherwise NA.

title: SARC-F Score
text: Compute 5-item SARC-F sarcopenia screening score (Strength, Assistance walking, Rise chair, Climb stairs, Falls). Items expected 0–2 scale; total ranges 0–10. Flags sarc_f_high_risk total ≥ 4. Includes mapping validation, NA policies, optional extreme screening/capping, non-numeric coercion safeguards.

title: SARC-F Score
text: SARC-F questionnaire responses need total score plus high-risk flag. want controlled handling missing miscoded responses (0–2 expected) optional capping. need outputs suitable downstream modeling screening summaries.

title: SARC-F Score
text: Required columns (col_map): strength, walking, chair, stairs, falls (expected 0/1/2). Mapped columns must exist. Units: None. Inputs already coded 0–2; transformation beyond coercion/capping/NA handling.

title: SARC-F Score
text: Validation & coercion: mapped columns must exist; non-numeric inputs coerced numeric warnings NAs introduced; non-finite values become NA. Missingness: keep/ignore/warn propagate NA total; omit drops rows required NA; error aborts missing. --range check: warning issued values fall outside 0–2. Scoring: sarc_f_score sums five items; sarc_f_high_risk TRUE score ≥ 4, FALSE otherwise, NA score NA. Empty result: na_action = "omit" drops rows, returns zero-row tibble expected columns.

title: SARC-F Score
text: Returns tibble sarc_f_score (0–10, NA) sarc_f_high_risk (TRUE/FALSE/NA). NA arises missing inputs, NA introduced coercion, NA set extreme handling, dropped rows omit.

title: SARC-F Score
text: Interpretation: Rows complete items return scores; row NA item yields sarc_f_score = NA sarc_f_high_risk = NA.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   Strength = c(0, 1, 2, NA),   Walking  = c(0, 0, 2, 1),   Chair    = c(0, 1, 2, 1),   Stairs   = c(0, 1, 2, 1),   Falls    = c(0, 0, 1, 0) )  sarc_f_score(   data = df,   col_map = list(     strength = "Strength",     walking  = "Walking",     chair    = "Chair",     stairs   = "Stairs",     falls    = "Falls"   ),   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   sarc_f_score sarc_f_high_risk #>          <dbl> <lgl>            #> 1            0 FALSE            #> 2            3 FALSE            #> 3            9 TRUE             #> 4           NA NA

title: SARC-F Score
text: Interpretation: --range values set NA summing; scores NA items return NA.
code: df2 <- tibble::tibble(   str = c(0, 3, -1),   walk = c(1, 2, 1),   chair = c(0, 2, 1),   stairs = c(0, 2, 2),   falls = c(0, 0, 1) )  # Pre-recode out-of-range values before calling df2 <- df2 %>% dplyr::mutate(dplyr::across(everything(), ~ ifelse(. < 0 | . > 2, NA_real_, .)))  sarc_f_score(   data = df2,   col_map = list(strength = "str", walking = "walk", chair = "chair", stairs = "stairs", falls = "falls"),   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   sarc_f_score sarc_f_high_risk #>          <dbl> <lgl>            #> 1            1 FALSE            #> 2           NA NA               #> 3           NA NA

title: SARC-F Score
text: Missing columns: ensure every required col_map key mapped existing column. Non-numeric inputs: coerced warnings; resulting NAs propagate unless omit used. --range values: warning emitted; recode --range items NA calling. NA scores: usually due missing items coercion NA.

title: SARC-F Score
text: Enable verbose output inspect column mapping, row counts, result summaries QC:
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") sarc_f_score(   data.frame(Strength = 1, Walking = 1, Chair = 1, Stairs = 0, Falls = 0),   col_map = list(strength="Strength", walking="Walking", chair="Chair", stairs="Stairs", falls="Falls"),   verbose = TRUE ) #> sarc_f_score(): reading input 'data' — 1 rows × 5 variables #> sarc_f_score(): col_map (5 columns — 5 specified) #>   strength          ->  'Strength' #>   walking           ->  'Walking' #>   chair             ->  'Chair' #>   stairs            ->  'Stairs' #>   falls             ->  'Falls' #> sarc_f_score(): computing markers: #>   sarc_f_score      [0-10 sum] #>   sarc_f_high_risk  [score >= 4] #> sarc_f_score(): results: sarc_f_score 1/1, sarc_f_high_risk 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   sarc_f_score sarc_f_high_risk #>          <dbl> <lgl>            #> 1            3 FALSE options(old_opt)

title: SARC-F Score
text: Recode --range items NA calling data may noisy. Choose na_action = "omit" need complete-case scores modeling; use keep/warn exploratory summaries partial data expected. Treat sarc_f_high_risk screening flag (score ≥ 4); follow clinical assessment appropriate.

title: SARC-F Score
text: Score simple sum five items; risk flag score >= 4 score finite. unit conversion weighting applied; inputs already reflect standard SARC-F coding.

title: Spirometry Markers
text: Compute spirometry interpretive markers: pre/post FEV1/FVC ratios, fixed-ratio obstruction flag (0.70), optional GLI percent-predicted/z/LLN/GOLD demographics rspiro available (falls back simple refs ), bronchodilator response deltas (%). Includes mapping validation, NA policies, optional extreme screening/capping volumes, safe handling zero/negative values ratios > 1 warnings.

title: Spirometry Markers
text: spirometry volumes (FEV1, FVC) liters optionally post-bronchodilator values. want obstruction flags fixed ratio , possible, LLN/GOLD using GLI references. need controlled NA handling, optional outlier checks/capping, post-bronchodilator response calculations.

title: Spirometry Markers
text: Units: Inputs must liters. unit conversion performed. GLI inputs (optional): age (years), height (cm), sex (male/female 1/0/2), ethnicity (GLI-compatible strings). Requires rspiro; otherwise debug fallback reference used GLI fields stay NA.

title: Spirometry Markers
text: Validation & coercion: required columns must exist; non-numeric volumes coerced warnings; non-finite become NA. Missingness: keep/ignore/warn propagate NA; omit drops rows required NA; error aborts required NA. Domain warnings: zero FVC -> ratio NA warning; negative volumes warn; ratios > 1 warn check units. GLI references: used age/height/sex/ethnicity provided rspiro available; else simple fallback used (debug) GLI outputs remain NA. Ethnicity mapped GLI categories; sex mapped first letter/1/0/2. GOLD grade: computed obstruction_lln TRUE; uses fev1 percent-predicted thresholds. Bronchodilator response: percent change (post - pre) / pre * 100; requires pre post columns.

title: Spirometry Markers
text: GLI mapping: sex strings starting m/1 -> Male; f/0/2 -> Female; ethnicity regex maps GLI categories (Caucasian, African American, North East Asian, South East Asian, ). Fallback reference (rspiro unavailable): rough height/age equations testability; clinical—aimed avoid -NA outputs tests. Empty result: na_action = "omit" drops rows, returns zero-row tibble expected columns.

title: Spirometry Markers
text: Columns: ratio_pre, ratio_post, copd_flag_fixed, obstruction_lln, fev1_pp, fvc_pp, fev1_z, fvc_z, ratio_z, gold_grade, bdr_fev1, bdr_fvc. NA arises required inputs missing, ratios undefined (e.g., zero FVC), GLI inputs absent/invalid, post values missing deltas.

title: Spirometry Markers
text: Interpretation: Returns pre FEV1/FVC ratios fixed-ratio flags; NA propagates inputs missing.
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   FEV1 = c(2.8, 1.6, NA),   FVC  = c(3.6, 2.1, 3.0) )  spirometry_markers(   data = df,   col_map = list(fev1 = "FEV1", fvc = "FVC"),   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 12 #>   ratio_pre ratio_post copd_flag_fixed obstruction_lln fev1_pp fvc_pp fev1_z #>       <dbl>      <dbl> <lgl>           <lgl>             <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1     0.778         NA FALSE           NA                   NA     NA     NA #> 2     0.762         NA FALSE           NA                   NA     NA     NA #> 3    NA             NA NA              NA                   NA     NA     NA #> # ℹ 5 more variables: fvc_z <dbl>, ratio_z <dbl>, gold_grade <chr>, #> #   bdr_fev1 <dbl>, bdr_fvc <dbl>

what: Worked example 2: With post-BD and GLI inputs
title: Spirometry Markers
text: Interpretation: Implausible volumes set NA calling; GLI fields computed rspiro available, otherwise fall back remain NA. Bronchodilator deltas percent changes pre→post.
code: df2 <- tibble::tibble(   fev1_pre = c(2.1, 0.9, 7.5),   fvc_pre  = c(3.0, 1.8, 11.0),   fev1_post = c(2.4, 1.1, 7.2),   fvc_post  = c(3.2, 1.9, 10.5),   age = c(55, 72, 45),   height = c(170, 160, 182),   sex = c("male", "female", "male"),   ethnicity = c("Caucasian", "African American", "Other") )  # Pre-filter implausible volumes before calling df2$fev1_pre[df2$fev1_pre > 8] <- NA df2$fvc_pre[df2$fvc_pre > 10] <- NA  cm2 <- list(   fev1 = "fev1_pre",   fvc = "fvc_pre",   fev1_post = "fev1_post",   fvc_post = "fvc_post",   age = "age",   height = "height",   sex = "sex",   ethnicity = "ethnicity" )  spirometry_markers(   data = df2,   col_map = cm2,   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 12 #>   ratio_pre ratio_post copd_flag_fixed obstruction_lln fev1_pp fvc_pp fev1_z #>       <dbl>      <dbl> <lgl>           <lgl>             <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1       0.7      0.75  FALSE           FALSE              40.8   42.1  -5.92 #> 2       0.5      0.579 TRUE            TRUE               23.7   31.2  -7.63 #> 3      NA        0.686 TRUE            TRUE              126.    NA     2.65 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: fvc_z <dbl>, ratio_z <dbl>, gold_grade <chr>, #> #   bdr_fev1 <dbl>, bdr_fvc <dbl>

title: Spirometry Markers

Missingness: keep propagates NA; omit drops rows required NA; error aborts required inputs contain NA. High-missing diagnostics: controlled na_warn_prop; shown verbose (debug) enabled. Safe division: ratios use helper sets NA zero/invalid denominators emits consolidated warning. Sweat rate: (weight_before−weight_after)/duration/body_surface_area(\text{weight_before} - \text{weight_after}) / \text{duration} / \text{body_surface_area} (1 kg ≈ 1 L water). Empty result: na_action = "omit" drops rows, returns zero-row tibble expected columns.

title: Sweat Markers
text: Returns tibble (rows follow na_action) : sweat_chloride, Na_K_ratio, sweat_lactate, sweat_rate. NA arises missing/invalid inputs, zero/invalid denominators, dropped rows omit.

what: Worked example 1: Standard usage, keep NAs
title: Sweat Markers
text: Interpretation: Returns outputs; NA propagates inputs missing denominators zero (e.g., sweat_K = 0).
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   sweat_chloride = c(45, 60, NA),   sweat_Na = c(55, 70, 60),   sweat_K = c(5, 4, 0),   sweat_lactate = c(4.8, 5.2, 6.0),   weight_before = c(70.0, 80.0, 75.0),   weight_after = c(69.5, 79.2, 74.8),   duration = c(1.0, 1.5, 0.5),   body_surface_area = c(1.9, 2.1, 2.0) )  sweat_markers(   data = df,   col_map = list(     sweat_chloride = "sweat_chloride",     sweat_Na = "sweat_Na",     sweat_K = "sweat_K",     sweat_lactate = "sweat_lactate",     weight_before = "weight_before",     weight_after = "weight_after",     duration = "duration",     body_surface_area = "body_surface_area"   ),   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 4 #>   sweat_chloride Na_K_ratio sweat_lactate sweat_rate #>            <dbl>      <dbl>         <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1             45       11             4.8      0.263 #> 2             60       17.5           5.2      0.254 #> 3             NA       NA             6        0.200

what: Worked example 2: Pre-filter extremes, drop incomplete

title: Tracer DXA Insulin Sensitivity
text: Compute tracer/DXA-based insulin sensitivity indices: LIRI_inv, Lipo_inv, ATIRI_inv (adipose-mode), , OGTT lipid data present, I_AUC, FFA_AUC, tracer_palmitate_SI, tracer_glycerol_SI. Includes mapping validation, NA/extreme handling, safe division/logs, built-unit conversions insulin, TG, HDL-c.

title: Tracer DXA Insulin Sensitivity
text: tracer infusion rates, DXA fat mass, optionally OGTT/lipid data want validated insulin sensitivity indices. want explicit NA/extreme handling, safe log/division, automatic mode selection based available columns.

title: Tracer DXA Insulin Sensitivity
text: Adipose-required keys: I0, rate_glycerol, rate_palmitate, fat_mass, weight, HDL_c, bmi. Full mode additional required keys: G0, G30, G120, I30, I120, TG, FFA. Units: - Glucose: mmol/L (conversion except formulas needing mg/dL) - Insulin: pmol/L (internally /6 microU/mL) - TG: mmol/L (×88.57 mg/dL) - HDL-c: mmol/L (×38.67 mg/dL) - Tracer rates: micromol/min - Fat mass, weight: kg; BMI: kg/m²

title: Tracer DXA Insulin Sensitivity
text: Validation & coercion: mapped columns must exist; non-numeric inputs coerced warnings NAs introduced; non-finite become NA. Missingness: keep propagates NA; omit drops rows required NA; error aborts required inputs contain NA. High-missing diagnostics: controlled na_warn_prop; shown verbose (debug) enabled. Safe division/log: ratios/logs use helpers set NA zero/invalid denominators non-positive log arguments, consolidated warnings. Mode selection: OGTT/lipid keys present, full mode used; otherwise, adipose-mode. Empty result: na_action = "omit" drops rows, returns zero-row tibble expected columns.

title: Tracer DXA Insulin Sensitivity
text: Safe logs: positive, finite values logged; others become NA. Consolidated zero-denominator warnings emitted divisions.

title: Tracer DXA Insulin Sensitivity

title: Urine Markers
text: Compute urine-renal tubular markers: UACR, KDIGO albuminuria stage (A1–A3), microalbuminuria flag, UPCR (protein provided), urine Na/K ratio (electrolytes provided), creatinine-normalized tubular markers (NGAL, KIM1, NAG, Beta2Micro, A1Micro, IL18, L_FABP). Includes mapping validation, NA policies, optional extreme screening/capping, safe division consolidated zero-denominator warnings. unit conversion applied.

title: Urine Markers
text: urine albumin creatinine (mg/L, mg/dL) want UACR albuminuria staging. also urine protein, Na/K, tubular markers need creatinine-normalized ratios. want robust NA/extreme handling safe divisions avoid Inf/NaN.

title: Urine Markers
text: Required columns: urine_albumin (mg/L), urine_creatinine (mg/dL). Optional columns: urine_protein (mg/L), urine_Na (mmol/L), urine_K (mmol/L), NGAL, KIM1, NAG, beta2_micro (Beta2Micro output), a1_micro (A1Micro output), IL18, L_FABP. Units: conversion. Ensure albumin/protein mg/L, creatinine mg/dL, Na/K mmol/L; tubular marker units consistent across markers.

title: Urine Markers
text: Validation & coercion: required columns must exist; numeric coercion warnings NAs introduced; non-finite set NA. Missingness: keep propagates NA; omit drops rows required NA; error aborts required inputs contain NA. High-missing diagnostics: controlled na_warn_prop; shown verbose (debug) enabled. Safe division: zero/invalid denominators set outputs NA consolidated warning. Albuminuria staging: A1 < 30 mg/g, A2 30–300 mg/g, A3 > 300 mg/g; microalbuminuria flag micro 30–300 mg/g, else normal. Empty result: na_action = "omit" drops rows, returns zero-row tibble expected columns.

title: Urine Markers
text: UACR, albuminuria_stage (A1/A2/A3), microalbuminuria (normal/micro), UPCR (urine_protein present), U_Na_K_ratio (Na & K present), NGAL_per_gCr, KIM1_per_gCr, NAG_per_gCr, Beta2Micro_per_gCr, A1Micro_per_gCr, IL18_per_gCr, L_FABP_per_gCr. NA arises missing/invalid inputs, zero denominators, dropped rows omit.

what: Worked example 1: Standard inputs, keep NAs
title: Urine Markers
text: Interpretation: Computes UACR, staging, micro flag, UPCR, U_Na_K_ratio, NGAL_per_gCr inputs exist; NA propagates missing zero denominators (e.g., urine_K = 0).
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   urine_albumin = c(25, 450, NA),      # mg/L   urine_creatinine = c(1.0, 0.8, 0.5), # mg/dL   urine_protein = c(120, NA, 90),      # mg/L   urine_Na = c(100, 80, 90),   urine_K = c(40, 30, 0),   NGAL = c(15, 40, 20) )  urine_markers(   data = df,   na_action = "keep",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 3 × 12 #>     UACR albuminuria_stage microalbuminuria  UPCR U_Na_K_ratio NGAL_per_gCr #>    <dbl> <fct>             <fct>            <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1  25000 A3                normal           12000         2.5          1500 #> 2 562500 A3                normal              NA         2.67         5000 #> 3     NA NA                normal           18000        NA            4000 #> # ℹ 6 more variables: KIM1_per_gCr <dbl>, NAG_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   Beta2Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, A1Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, IL18_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   L_FABP_per_gCr <dbl>

A3                normal           6667.         2.57         833. #> # ℹ 6 more variables: KIM1_per_gCr <dbl>, NAG_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   Beta2Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, A1Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, IL18_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   L_FABP_per_gCr <dbl>

title: Urine Markers
text: UACR = (urine_albumin / urine_creatinine) * 1000 (mg/g); UPCR analogous protein present. U_Na_K_ratio = urine_Na / urine_K. Tubular markers per g creatinine: marker / (urine_creatinine * 0.01) denominator g/L. Divisions safe: non-finite/zero denominators yield NA consolidated warning.

title: Vitamin D Status
text: vitamin_d_status() classifies serum 25(OH)D (assumed ng/mL) Deficient (<20), Insufficient (20-29), Sufficient (>=30). validates mappings, enforces missing-data policy, can scan/cap implausible values.

title: Vitamin D Status
text: Standardize vitamin D categorization dashboards, cohorts, QC summaries. Guard ETL steps: abort omit rows missing/implausible 25(OH)D downstream models. Spot potential unit mix-ups: nmol/L inputs often appear ~2.5x higher ng/mL.

title: Vitamin D Status
text: data: data frame/tibble containing 25(OH)D column. col_map: named list mapping either vitd vitamin_d column name. column must exist coercible numeric; coercion introduces NAs emits warning.

title: Vitamin D Status
text: na_action = c("keep","omit","error","ignore","warn") (default “keep”): policy missing 25(OH)D. warn/ignore behave like keep; omit drops rows; error aborts. verbose = FALSE: emit progress messages.

title: Vitamin D Status
text: Missing 25(OH)D follows na_action; omit shrinks output; error aborts. Non-numeric inputs coerced numeric; NAs introduced trigger warning. Negative values high medians (suggesting nmol/L) warn values appear implausible.

title: Vitamin D Status
text: Tibble ordered factor vitamin_d_status (Deficient < Insufficient < Sufficient). Rows drop na_action = "omit"; otherwise output aligns input row count.

title: Vitamin D Status
text: Quick categorization Pre-filter classify
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(VitD = c(12, 25, 36, NA))  vitamin_d_status(   data = df,   col_map = list(vitd = "VitD"),   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 4 × 1 #>   vitamin_d_status #>   <ord>            #> 1 Deficient        #> 2 Insufficient     #> 3 Sufficient       #> 4 NA df2 <- tibble::tibble(d = c(15, 280, 8, 65, NA))  # 280 is implausible; pre-filter  # Pre-filter before calling df2$d[df2$d > 250] <- NA  vitamin_d_status(   data = df2,   col_map = list(vitd = "d"),   na_action = "warn",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 5 × 1 #>   vitamin_d_status #>   <ord>            #> 1 Deficient        #> 2 NA               #> 3 Deficient        #> 4 Sufficient       #> 5 NA

title: Vitamin D Status
code: old_opt <- options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform") vitamin_d_status(   data = tibble::tibble(VitD = c(15, 32)),   col_map = list(vitamin_d = "VitD"),   verbose = TRUE ) #> vitamin_d_status(): reading input 'data' — 2 rows × 1 variables #> vitamin_d_status(): col_map (2 columns — 1 specified, 1 inferred from data) #>   vitamin_d         ->  'VitD' #>   vitd              ->  'VitD'    (inferred) #> vitamin_d_status(): computing markers: #>   vitamin_d_status [VitD] #> vitamin_d_status(): results: vitamin_d_status 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 1 #>   vitamin_d_status #>   <ord>            #> 1 Deficient        #> 2 Sufficient options(old_opt)

title: Vitamin D Status
text: medians ~150-250 without obvious outliers, verify units (nmol/L divided 2.5 get ng/mL). Set na_action = "omit" downstream steps handle NA statuses. Keep col_map named; missing empty mappings abort classed errors can assert tests.

title: Vitamin Markers
text: vitamin_markers() builds composite vitamin/endocrine ratios z-scores (VitD_Z, B12_Fol_Ratio, Ferr_TSat_R, Cort_DHEA_R, T_E2_Ratio, TSH_fT4_R, Retinol_Z, Toco_Lip_R, Mg_Zn_R, Cu_Zn_R) passes PIVKA_II, VitC, Homocysteine, MMA. validates mappings, enforces missing-data policy, can scan/cap extremes, uses safe division avoid inf/NaN.

title: Vitamin Markers
text: Create harmonized nutrient/endocrine summaries cohorts dashboards. QC pipelines missingness/extremes handled uniformly across many analytes. Downstream modeling expects standardized ratios/z-scores rather raw labs.

title: Vitamin Markers
text: keys required; defaults. col_map must name every key column: VitD, VitD_ref_mean, VitD_ref_sd B12, Folate Ferritin, TSat Cortisol, DHEAS Testosterone, Estradiol TSH, free_T4 Retinol, Retinol_ref_mean, Retinol_ref_sd Tocopherol, Total_lipids PIVKA_II, VitC, Homocysteine, MMA Magnesium, Zinc, Copper Columns must exist coercible numeric. Non-finite values become NA; coercion introduces NAs warns.

title: Vitamin Markers
text: na_action = c("keep","omit","error") (default “keep”): policy missing required inputs. omit drops rows; error aborts. na_warn_prop = 0.2: debug-level verbose enabled, flags columns >=20% missing. verbose = FALSE: progress summaries via hm_inform().

what: How inputs are validated and cleaned
title: Vitamin Markers
text: hm_validate_inputs() ensures data data frame keys mapped; missing maps abort scoped errors. mapped column coerced numeric; NA introduced warned; non-finite set NA. NA policy applied across required columns; omit output shrinks rows fully observed required inputs. Safe division sets zero denominators NA emits consolidated warning listing affected ratios.

title: Vitamin Markers
text: Tibble : - Ratios/z-scores: VitD_Z, B12_Fol_Ratio, Ferr_TSat_R, Cort_DHEA_R, T_E2_Ratio, TSH_fT4_R, Retinol_Z, Toco_Lip_R, Mg_Zn_R, Cu_Zn_R - Pass-: PIVKA_II, VitC, Homocysteine, MMA Output row count matches input unless na_action = "omit" removed rows.

title: Vitamin Markers
text: Basic run Pre-filter run
code: library(HealthMarkers) library(tibble)  df <- tibble::tibble(   VitD = c(50, 30), VitD_ref_mean = c(40, 40), VitD_ref_sd = c(5, 5),   B12 = c(300, 280), Folate = c(15, 12),   Ferritin = c(100, 80), TSat = c(0.25, 0.22),   Cortisol = c(200, 180), DHEAS = c(100, 90),   Testosterone = c(12, 10), Estradiol = c(120, 110),   TSH = c(2, 2.5), free_T4 = c(14, 13),   Retinol = c(0.8, 0.7), Retinol_ref_mean = c(0.9, 0.9), Retinol_ref_sd = c(0.2, 0.2),   Tocopherol = c(30, 28), Total_lipids = c(3, 2.8),   PIVKA_II = c(5, 6), VitC = c(60, 55), Homocysteine = c(10, 11), MMA = c(0.3, 0.35),   Magnesium = c(0.8, 0.78), Zinc = c(15, 14), Copper = c(15, 14) )  cm <- as.list(names(df)); names(cm) <- names(df)  vitamin_markers(   data = df,   col_map = cm,   na_action = "keep" ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 14 #>   VitD_Z B12_Fol_Ratio Ferr_TSat_R Cort_DHEA_R T_E2_Ratio TSH_fT4_R Retinol_Z #>    <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>      <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1      2          20          400            2     0.1        0.143      -0.5 #> 2     -2          23.3        364.           2     0.0909     0.192      -1   #> # ℹ 7 more variables: Toco_Lip_R <dbl>, PIVKA_II <dbl>, VitC <dbl>, #> #   Homocysteine <dbl>, MMA <dbl>, Mg_Zn_R <dbl>, Cu_Zn_R <dbl> df2 <- df # Force a few implausible values df2$TSat[2] <- 2.0 df2$Zinc[1] <- 2000  # Pre-filter implausible values before calling df2$TSat[df2$TSat > 1] <- NA df2$Zinc[df2$Zinc > 500] <- NA  vitamin_markers(   data = df2,   col_map = cm,   na_action = "omit",   verbose = TRUE ) #> # A tibble: 0 × 14 #> # ℹ 14 variables: VitD_Z <dbl>, B12_Fol_Ratio <dbl>, Ferr_TSat_R <dbl>, #> #   Cort_DHEA_R <dbl>, T_E2_Ratio <dbl>, TSH_fT4_R <dbl>, Retinol_Z <dbl>, #> #   Toco_Lip_R <dbl>, PIVKA_II <dbl>, VitC <dbl>, Homocysteine <dbl>, #> #   MMA <dbl>, Mg_Zn_R <dbl>, Cu_Zn_R <dbl>

title: Vitamin Markers
text: Incomplete unnamed col_map aborts; ensure every key mapped. denominators zero (e.g., TSat = 0), expect warning NA ratios; address upstream unexpected. Align units across inputs; unit conversion performed ratios/z-scores. Use na_action = "omit" downstream models handle propagated NAs.

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: HealthMarkers comprehensive R toolkit computing, standardising, summarising clinical research biomarkers routine laboratory phenotypic data. provides 50 specialist functions covering 290 biomarkers, includinginsulin sensitivity indices, cardiovascular risk scores, inflammatory aging clocks, frailty indices, psychiatric rating scales, alternate-biofluid panels, much . accessible unified dispatcher, all_health_markers(). Full documentation, function reference, vignettes available package website:https://sufyansuleman.github.io/HealthMarkers/ Covers wide range biomarkers. single all_health_markers() call returns glycaemic, lipid, liver, renal, pulmonary, inflammatory, hormonal, bone, psychiatric, nutritional markers one wide tibble. Variable naming flexibility. built-synonym dictionary covers naming conventions 15+ cohorts biobanks: UK Biobank, NHANES, HUNT, Tromsø, FinnGen, Estonian Biobank, LifeLines (Netherlands), Generation Scotland, Us (LOINC codes), Danish registers (NPU codes), . Column names systems recognised automatically without manual mapping. Safe defaults. NA handling, input validation, column-name inference, range-capping built . Failed marker groups skipped warning, never crashing pipeline. Reproducibility. Explicit col_map arguments map column names expected internal keys, silent renaming. References. Every function cites primary paper, derived methods, validated applications reviews original work modifications. Full bibliography inst/REFERENCES.bib. 46 vignettes worked clinical examples included.

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: Optional packages unlock additional marker groups: optional packages absent, dependent groups skipped safely; running verbose = TRUE shows groups computed skipped () summary message.
code: # From CRAN install.packages("HealthMarkers")  # Development version from GitHub remotes::install_github("sufyansuleman/HealthMarkers") install.packages(c("CVrisk", "rspiro", "PooledCohort", "QRISK3",                    "RiskScorescvd", "di", "mice", "missForest"))

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: Use want compute many marker groups one call receive everything back single wide tibble. argument accepts following group keys: Pass = "" run every group (groups requiring unavailable optional packages silently skipped).
code: library(HealthMarkers)  labs <- data.frame(   age  = 52,  sex  = "M",   G0   = 5.8, I0   = 14.2,   TC   = 5.6, LDL_c = 3.4, HDL_c = 1.1, TG = 2.1,   ALT  = 38,  AST  = 30,   BMI   = 30.1,   SBP  = 138, DBP  = 88,   WC    = 98,   eGFR = 74,  UACR = 18 )  results <- all_health_markers(   data    = labs,   which   = c("glycemic", "lipid", "liver", "renal", "kidney_kfre",               "mets", "obesity_metrics"),   verbose = TRUE )  # results is the original data + all computed columns as one wide tibble names(results) insulin_fasting     insulin_ogtt        insulin_adipose     insulin_tracer_dxa glycemic            lipid               atherogenic         cvd_aip cvd_risk            cvd_ldl_particles   cvd_ascvd           cvd_qrisk3 cvd_scorescvd       cvd_stroke          liver               liver_fat mets metabolic_risk      pulmo               spirometry          bode saliva              sweat               urine               renal kidney_kfre         ckd_stage           nutrient            vitamin vitamin_d_status    hormone             inflammatory        iAge bone                frax                allostatic_load     oxidative frailty_index       charlson            sarc_f              psych nfl                 inflammatory_age    calcium_corrected   kyn_trp adiposity_sds       adiposity_sds_strat obesity_metrics     alm_bmi

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: Use need fine-grained control, working specialist data (e.g. OGTT time-series, DXA outputs, spirometry), want inspect one marker family detail.

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: fasting glucose + insulin data available; OGTT data multiple time points; DXA body-composition data; tracer clamp data.
code: # Fasting indices (HOMA-IR, QUICKI, Bennett, FIRI, ...) # Needs: G0 (fasting glucose mmol/L), I0 (fasting insulin mU/L) fasting_is(data, col_map = list(G0 = "glucose", I0 = "insulin"))  # OGTT indices (Matsuda, Stumvoll, Gutt, Avignon, ...) # Needs: G0/G30/G60/G120 and I0/I30/I60/I120 (mmol/L and mU/L) ogtt_is(data, col_map = list(G0="G0", G30="G30", G60="G60", G120="G120",                               I0="I0", I30="I30", I60="I60", I120="I120"))  # Adipose-tissue indices (LIRI, SPISE, VAI, LAP, ...) # Needs: BMI, WC, TG, HDL_c adipo_is(data, col_map = list(BMI="BMI", WC="WC", TG="TG", HDL_c="HDL_c"))  # DXA / tracer-based indices # Needs: fat mass, lean mass, Ra (palmitate/glycerol rates) tracer_dxa_is(data, col_map = list(fat_mass="FM_kg", lean_mass="LM_kg"))  # All insulin indices at once (fasting + OGTT + adipose + DXA) all_insulin_indices(data, col_map = list(...), normalize = "none",                     mode = "both",  # "IS" = sensitivity only, "IR" = resistance only                     na_action = "keep")

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: primary prevention cohorts; assessing 10-year MACE risk; comparing risk algorithms side--side.
code: # ASCVD Pooled Cohort Equations (10-yr or 30-yr) # Needs: age, sex, race, total_chol, HDL_c, sbp, bp_treated, smoker, diabetes, bmi # Requires: PooledCohort package cvd_risk_ascvd(data, year = 10)  # QRISK3 (UK population) # Requires: QRISK3 package cvd_risk_qrisk3(data)  # SCORE2 / SCORE2-OP (European) # Requires: RiskScorescvd package cvd_risk_scorescvd(data)  # 10-year stroke risk (Pooled Cohort) # Requires: PooledCohort package cvd_risk_stroke(data)  # Atherogenic index of plasma (log TG/HDL) cvd_marker_aip(data, col_map = list(TG = "TG", HDL_c = "HDL_c"))  # LDL particle number from ApoB cvd_marker_ldl_particle_number(data, col_map = list(ApoB = "ApoB"))  # Run all CVD algorithms at once and pick one model cvd_risk(data, model = "ALL")   # or "ASCVD", "QRISK3", "Stroke", etc.

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: nephrology studies; CKD cohorts; monitoring progression.
code: # Kidney Failure Risk Equation (KFRE) 2-year and 5-year probability # Needs: age, sex, eGFR (CKD-EPI, mL/min/1.73m²), UACR (mg/g) kidney_failure_risk(data, col_map = list(age="age", sex="sex",                                           eGFR="eGFR", UACR="UACR"))  # eGFR, creatinine ratios, BUN/creatinine, FE-Urea, etc. renal_markers(data, col_map = list(creatinine="Creat", age="age", sex="sex"))  # KDIGO CKD staging (G1–G5 × A1–A3) ckd_stage(data, col_map = list(eGFR="eGFR", UACR="UACR"))  # Urine panel: protein/creatinine ratio, microalbumin, osmolality urine_markers(data, col_map = list(urine_creat="UCr", urine_protein="UPr"))

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: respiratory epidemiology; COPD staging; lung-function studies.
code: # Individual spirometry z-scores and % predicted (GLI 2012) # Needs: FEV1, FVC; optionally age, height, sex, ethnicity # Requires: rspiro package spirometry_markers(data, col_map = list(fev1="FEV1", fvc="FVC",                                          age="age", height="ht_cm", sex="sex"))  # Simpler pulmonary ratios (FEV1/FVC, FEF25-75, etc.) no extra packages needed pulmo_markers(data)  # BODE index for COPD prognosis # Needs: FEV1% predicted, 6-minute walk distance, mMRC dyspnoea score, BMI bode_index(data, col_map = list(fev1_pct="FEV1pct", sixmwd="Walk6m",                                   mmrc="mMRC", bmi="BMI"))

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: mental health research; epidemiological surveys standardised questionnaires.
code: # Score one or many scales from item columns # Supported: PHQ-9, GAD-7, K6, K10, GHQ-12, WHO-5, ISI, MDQ, #            ASRS, BIS-11, SPQ, cognitive composite # # col_map is a nested list keyed by instrument name. # Internal item keys use zero-padded names (phq9_01 ... phq9_09). psych_markers(   data,   col_map = list(     phq9 = list(items = list(phq9_01 = "Q1", phq9_02 = "Q2", ...)),     gad7 = list(items = list(gad7_01 = "G1", gad7_02 = "G2", ...))   ),   which   = c("phq9", "gad7", "k10")  # choose scales to score )  # If your columns are already named phq9_01 ... phq9_09 etc., no col_map needed: phq9_score(data) gad7_score(data) k10_score(data)

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: paediatric cohorts (SDS); obesity epidemiology; sarcopenia assessment.
code: # Common obesity and adiposity indices # Needs: height, weight, WC, hip circumference; optionally age, sex obesity_indices(data)  # SDS z-scores from user-supplied reference mean and SD calc_sds(x = data$BMI, mean_ref = 22.5, sd_ref = 3.8)  # Sex-stratified SDS for multiple adiposity variables simultaneously adiposity_sds_strat(data, col_map = list(sex = "sex"),                     var_cols = c("BMI","WC","WHR"),                     ref_male = list(BMI = c(mean=25, sd=4)),                     ref_female = list(BMI = c(mean=24, sd=3.8)))  # Appendicular lean mass / BMI index (sarcopenia screening) alm_bmi_index(data, col_map = list(alm="ALM_kg", bmi="BMI", sex="Sex"))

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: immunology studies; biological age estimation; chronic disease research.
code: # Blood count-derived inflammatory ratios # Needs: neutrophils, lymphocytes, monocytes, platelets inflammatory_markers(data, col_map = list(neut="NEUT", lymph="LYMPH",                                            mono="MONO", plt="PLT"))  # iAge inflammatory aging clock # Needs: a panel of inflammatory proteins (IL-6, CXCL9, etc.) iAge(data, col_map = list(IL6="IL6", CXCL9="CXCL9"))

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: use: stress research (saliva); cystic fibrosis / sweat testing; nephrology urine panels.
code: saliva_markers(data, col_map = list(cortisol_wake="C_wake",                                      cortisol_30="C_30min")) sweat_markers(data,  col_map = list(sweat_chloride="Cl_mmol")) urine_markers(data,  col_map = list(urine_creat="UCr", urine_na="UNa"))

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: Every function accepts col_map argument: named list mapping internal keys (function expects) actual column names ().
code: # Internal key = "G0", your column is called "fasting_glucose_mmol" fasting_is(   data    = my_data,   col_map = list(G0 = "fasting_glucose_mmol", I0 = "insulin_uU_mL") )

what: Multi-biobank automatic variable name OR column name recognition
title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: synonym dictionary recognises column names 15+ major cohorts biobanks box. table shows analyte named across systems: OMOP CDM / Us data, concept codes LOINC_XXXX_X format recognised major analytes. Nordic EHR / register data, Danish Norwegian NPU codes (NPU01994, NPU01567, etc.) matched directly. Generation Scotland-specific names (SBP_mean, DBP_mean, genetic_sex, ethnic_group) HUNT/Tromsø Norwegian-language terms also included.

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: Call hm_col_report() first see columns auto-detected need manual mapping: prints report like: Copy printed col_map template fill column names unmatched keys: capture auto-detected mappings directly merge: Pass col_map function: hm_col_report() accepts two optional flags:
code: library(HealthMarkers) hm_col_report(my_data) ── HealthMarkers column report ────────────────────────────────────────────  Data: 40314 rows × 299 columns   |   Keys in dictionary: 258   key                  data_column        how matched  -------------------- ------------------ ------------------  fasting_glucose      pglu0              exact  ✔  TG                   trig               exact  ✔  ALT                  alat               exact  ✔  albumin              alb                exact  ✔  vitaminD             vitd25             exact  ✔  eGFR                 ─                  NOT FOUND ✘   ✔ 187 keys matched   ✘ 71 keys not found  ── col_map template for missing keys ──────────────────────────────────────  col_map <- list(    eGFR  = "from_your_data",   # fill in your column name  ) my_col_map <- list(   eGFR = "GFR_ckdepi" ) # Returns a named list of all matched key → column pairs cm <- hm_col_report(my_data, verbose = FALSE)  # Add manual overrides for anything not matched cm$eGFR <- "GFR_ckdepi" all_health_markers(   data    = my_data,   which   = c("insulin_fasting", "glycemic", "lipid", "liver"),   col_map = cm ) hm_col_report(my_data, show_unmatched = TRUE)  # list every unmatched key hm_col_report(my_data, fuzzy = TRUE)           # add fuzzy matching as last resort

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: Missing values generally resolved passing data marker function. package provides three main helpers: impute_mice(): multiple imputation using mice package, suitable inference analyses preserving uncertainty important. impute_missforest(): random-forest imputation via missForest, useful prediction quality priority. impute_missing(): fast deterministic column-wise imputation numeric variables, good quick exploratory work simple pipelines. Use mean missing values roughly symmetric, median data skewed contain outliers. impute_missing() supports several methods beyond median, including mean, zero, constant.
code: # Multiple imputation (mice) recommended when you want to preserve inference uncertainty completed <- impute_mice(my_data, m = 5, seed = 42)  # Random-forest imputation (missForest) recommended for predictive filling of missing values completed <- impute_missforest(my_data)  # Simple deterministic imputation for fast exploratory analysis completed <- impute_missing(my_data, method = "median")

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: Set verbose = TRUE function see progress messages columns mapped, groups computed, skipped (reasons).: Enable globally entire session:
code: results <- all_health_markers(data = labs, which = c("lipid","liver"),                                verbose = TRUE) #> Column mapping summary: TC->TC (user), HDL_c->HDL_c (user) ... #> -> lipid #> -> liver #> all_health_markers(): summary - computed: lipid, liver | skipped/failed: none # levels: "none" (default), "inform" (progress only), "debug" (all internal steps) options(healthmarkers.verbose = "inform")

title: Toolkit for Clinical, Metabolic, and Cardiovascular Biomarker Calculations
text: HealthMarkers active development. indices implemented original, revised verified published manuscripts. notice error index, please open issue can corrected. insulin sensitivity resistance indices independently verified used following peer-reviewed publications: Suleman S, Ängquist L, Linneberg , Hansen T, Grarup N. Exploring genetic intersection obesity-associated genetic variants insulin sensitivity indices. Sci Rep. 2025;15:15761. PMID 40328835 Suleman S, Huang Y, Jensen RT, Poggi AI, Christensen SB, Fraulund MM, Anderson LV, Stinson SE, Fonvig CE, Pedersen O, Holm JC, Hansen T, Grarup N. Adult-based Genetic Risk Scores Insulin Resistance Associate Cardiometabolic Traits Children Adolescents. J Clin Endocrinol Metab. 2024;110(9):2645–2654. what: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices (QUICKI, VAI, LAP, TyG, TG/HDL, Belfiore) — adipo_is
title: Adipose insulin sensitivity indices (QUICKI, VAI, LAP, TyG, TG/HDL, Belfiore) — adipo_is
text: Computes adipose-related insulin sensitivity/resistance indices fasting inputs. Expected input units (converted internally): Glucose G0 mmol/L -> mg/dL (* 18) Insulin I0 pmol/L -> muU/mL (/ 6) TG mmol/L -> mg/dL (* 88.57) HDL mmol/L -> mg/dL (* 38.67) Reported indices (higher magnitude negative "_inv" values implies worse adipose IR): Revised_QUICKI = 1 / (log10(I0 (muU/mL)) + log10(G0 (mg/dL)) + log10(FFA (mmol/L))) VAI (sex-specific; inverted VAI_*_inv larger negative = worse) TG_HDL_C_inv = -(TG/HDL) mg/dL TyG_inv = -ln(TG (mg/dL) * G0 (mg/dL) / 2) LAP (sex-specific; inverted) McAuley_index = exp(2.63 - 0.28 ln(I0 (muU/mL)) - 0.31 ln(TG (mmol/L))) Adipo_inv = -(FFA * I0 (muU/mL)) Belfiore_inv_FFA = - 2 / (I0 (muU/mL) * FFA + 1)

what: Calculate standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds
title: Calculate standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds
text: Computes standard deviation (z) scores anthropometric variables relative single (non-sex-stratified) reference set means standard deviations. Includes input validation, optional raw-value extreme screening/capping, configurable handling extreme SDS values, NA row policies, optional concise summary output, optional verbose progress messages.

title: Calculate standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds
code: adiposity_sds(   data,   col_map = NULL,   ref,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   extreme_action = c("cap", "NA", "error", "warn", "ignore"),   sds_cap = 6,   diagnostics = FALSE,   warn_thresholds = list(na_prop = 0.05, extreme_prop = 0.01),   id_col = NULL,   return_summary = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE,   na_strategy = NULL,   extreme_strategy = NULL )

title: Calculate standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds
text: data data.frame tibble containing measurement columns. col_map Optional named list mapping reference variable names column names data. NULL, identity mapping assumed (names(ref) must data). ref Named list element numeric vector names mean sd, e.g. list(BMI = c(mean = 23, sd = 4)). na_action One c("keep","omit","error") row handling mapped variable missing. extreme_action One c("cap","NA","error","warn","ignore") SDS values exceeding sds_cap. sds_cap Positive numeric; absolute cap used extreme_action = "cap". diagnostics Logical; TRUE emit informational/warning messages (coercions, missingness, extremes). FALSE suppresses non-critical warnings. warn_thresholds Named list optional elements na_prop (default 0.05) extreme_prop (default 0.01) used proportion-based diagnostic warnings. id_col Optional column name used verbose summaries. return_summary Logical; TRUE return list elements data, summary, warnings instead just SDS tibble. verbose Logical; TRUE print progress completion summaries. na_strategy Soft-deprecated alias na_action (provided na_action missing, used). extreme_strategy Soft-deprecated alias extreme_action (provided extreme_action missing, used).

title: Calculate standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds
text: tibble one <var>_SDS column per reference variable, list return_summary = TRUE.

title: Calculate standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds
text: SDS computed : (observed - mean) / sd. Rows removed na_action = 'omit'. Raw-value extreme screening (enabled) applied SDS computation. Extreme SDS handling (cap / warn / error / ignore) controlled extreme_action. Legacy argument aliases (na_strategy, extreme_strategy) soft-deprecated still accepted.

title: Calculate standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds
text: Cole TJ, Green PJ (1992). “Smoothing Reference Centile Curves: LMS Method Penalized Likelihood.” Statistics Medicine, 11(10), 1305–1319. doi:10.1002/sim.4780111005 .

title: Calculate standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds
code: ref <- list(BMI = c(mean = 23, sd = 4), waist = c(mean = 80, sd = 12)) df <- data.frame(BMI = c(25, NA, 60, 18), waist = c(85, 70, 300, 55)) adiposity_sds(df, ref) #> adiposity_sds(): col_map (2 columns — 2 inferred from data) #>   BMI               ->  'BMI'    (inferred) #>   waist             ->  'waist'    (inferred) #> adiposity_sds(): computing markers: #>   BMI_SDS, waist_SDS #> adiposity_sds(): results: BMI_SDS 3/4, waist_SDS 4/4 #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   BMI_SDS waist_SDS #>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1    0.5      0.417 #> 2   NA       -0.833 #> 3    6        6     #> 4   -1.25    -2.08

title: Compute sex-stratified standardized scores (SDS) for adiposity measures — adiposity_sds_strat
text: data Data frame tibble variables sex column col_map Named list mapping: sex: column name sex values ("M","F","m","f", 1, 2) vars: optional named list mapping reference variable names -> data column names. omitted, expects reference variable names exist data. ref Named list elements "M" "F". named list numeric vectors c(mean=, sd=) keyed variable name, e.g.: list( M = list(BMI = c(mean=23, sd=3.5), waist = c(mean=85, sd=10)), F = list(BMI = c(mean=21, sd=3.0), waist = c(mean=75, sd=9)) ) na_action One : "keep"  - keep rows NA (propagates outputs) "omit"  - drop rows NA required variable "error" - abort required variable NA allow_partial TRUE, skip variables absent data (warning); FALSE error prefix Optional prefix output columns (default "") verbose Logical; TRUE emit progress via package logger; default logging controlled options(healthmarkers.verbose)

code: ref <- list(   M = list(BMI = c(mean=24.5, sd=3.8), waist = c(mean=88, sd=12)),   F = list(BMI = c(mean=22.1, sd=4.2), waist = c(mean=76, sd=11)) ) df <- data.frame(BMI=c(25.2,21.8,27.1), waist=c(85,72,95), sex=c("M","F","M")) adiposity_sds_strat(   df,   col_map = list(sex = "sex", vars = list(BMI = "BMI", waist = "waist")),   ref = ref ) #> adiposity_sds_strat(): reading input 'df' — 3 rows × 3 variables #> adiposity_sds_strat(): col_map (2 columns — 2 specified) #>   BMI               ->  'BMI' #>   waist             ->  'waist' #> adiposity_sds_strat(): computing markers: #>   BMI_SDS, waist_SDS #> adiposity_sds_strat(): results: BMI_SDS 3/3, waist_SDS 3/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   BMI_SDS waist_SDS #>     <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1  0.184     -0.25  #> 2 -0.0714    -0.364 #> 3  0.684      0.583

title: Allostatic Load Index — allostatic_load
text: Computes composite Allostatic Load (AL) score flagging biomarkers exceed user-specified high-risk thresholds (strict > multiple biomarkers; inclusive >= one biomarker). Aligned HM-CS v3: structured validation, diagnostic control, verbose reporting, optional summary output.

title: Allostatic Load Index — allostatic_load
code: allostatic_load(   data,   thresholds,   col_map = NULL,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   return_summary = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Allostatic Load Index — allostatic_load
text: data data.frame tibble numeric biomarker columns. thresholds named list scalar numeric cutoffs (names must match columns). col_map optional named list mapping keys thresholds column names data. na_action one c("keep","omit","error") ("keep" treats NA zero contribution). return_summary logical; TRUE returns list(data, summary, warnings). verbose logical; print progress messages via hm_inform() (also gated options(healthmarkers.verbose)).

title: Allostatic Load Index — allostatic_load
text: tibble AllostaticLoad list return_summary = TRUE.

title: Allostatic Load Index — allostatic_load
text: API pattern note: Unlike HealthMarkers functions follow standard (data, col_map, ...) signature, allostatic_load() uses (data, thresholds, col_map = NULL, ...) thresholds domain-specific required argument inferred column names alone. result, function directly dispatchable standard all_health_markers() registry; custom wrapper supplies thresholds explicitly required.

title: Allostatic Load Index — allostatic_load
text: McEwen BS, Stellar E (1993). “Stress Individual: Mechanisms Leading Disease.” Archives Internal Medicine, 153(18), 2093–2101. doi:10.1001/archinte.153.18.2093 . ; Seeman TE, Singer BH, Rowe JW, Horwitz RI, McEwen BS (1997). “Price Adaptation—Allostatic Load Health Consequences.” Archives Internal Medicine, 157(19), 2259–2268. doi:10.1001/archinte.1997.00440400111013 . ; Juster R, McEwen BS, Lupien SJ (2010). “Allostatic Load Biomarkers Chronic Stress Impact Health Cognition.” Neuroscience Biobehavioral Reviews, 35(1), 2–16. doi:10.1016/j.neubiorev.2009.10.002 . ; Wiley JF, Gruenewald TL, Karlamangla , Seeman TE (2016). “Modeling Multisystem Physiological Dysregulation.” Psychosomatic Medicine, 78(3), 290–301. doi:10.1097/PSY.0000000000000288 .

title: Allostatic Load Index — allostatic_load
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   SBP = c(118, 142, 130),   DBP = c(76, 92, 85),   CRP = c(1.2, 4.8, 2.1) ) thr <- list(SBP = 130, DBP = 85, CRP = 3) allostatic_load(df, thresholds = thr, na_action = "keep", verbose = FALSE) #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>   AllostaticLoad #>            <int> #> 1              0 #> 2              3 #> 3              0  # Single biomarker uses inclusive >= rule allostatic_load(df, thresholds = list(CRP = 3)) #> allostatic_load(): reading input 'df' — 3 rows × 3 variables #> allostatic_load(): col_map (1 column — 1 inferred from data) #>   CRP               ->  'CRP'    (inferred) #> allostatic_load(): computing markers: #>   AllostaticLoad [CRP] #> allostatic_load(): results: AllostaticLoad 3/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 1 #>   AllostaticLoad #>            <int> #> 1              0 #> 2              1 #> 3              0

title: Adult ADHD Self-Report Scale scoring — asrs_score

title: Compute atherogenic indices — atherogenic_indices
text: Calculates: AIP: Atherogenic Index Plasma = log10(TG / HDL_c) CRI_I: Castelli Risk Index = TC / HDL_c CRI_II: Castelli Risk Index II = LDL_c / HDL_c

title: Compute atherogenic indices — atherogenic_indices
code: atherogenic_indices(   data,   col_map = NULL,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   normalize = c("none", "log10"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Compute atherogenic indices — atherogenic_indices
text: data data.frame/tibble lipid columns. col_map named list mapping keys columns, e.g. list(TG="TG", HDL_c="HDL_c", TC="TC", LDL_c="LDL_c"). na_action one c("keep","omit","error"). normalize one c("none","log10"). Reserved; AIP always uses log10(TG/HDL_c). verbose Logical; TRUE (default), prints column mapping, list indices computed, per-column results summary.

title: Compute atherogenic indices — atherogenic_indices
text: tibble columns AIP, CRI_I, CRI_II. ID column detected data (e.g. id, IID, participant_id), prepended.

title: Compute atherogenic indices — atherogenic_indices
text: Behavior: Required keys: TG, HDL_c. Optional: TC, LDL_c. NA policy via na_action: "keep" (default), "omit" (drop rows NA used lipids), "error". Extreme screening via check_extreme extreme_action ("warn","cap","error","ignore","NA"). Default bounds (mg/dL) used screening: TG (0, 10000), HDL_c (0, 1000), LDL_c (0, 10000), TC (0, 10000). Note: indices unitless ratios; units cancel computations. Emits progress via hm_inform() verbose = TRUE package option enables logs.

title: Compute atherogenic indices — atherogenic_indices
text: Dobiášová M (2004). “Atherogenic Index Plasma (AIP) log(Triglycerides/HDL-Cholesterol): Theoretical Practical Implications.” Clinical Chemistry, 50(7), 1113–1115. doi:10.1373/clinchem.2004.033175 . ; Castelli WP, Doyle JT, Gordon T, et al. (1977). “High-Density Lipoprotein Cholesterol Lipids Coronary Heart Disease: Framingham Study.” American Journal Medicine, 62(5), 707–714. doi:10.1016/0002-9343(77)90874-9 .

title: Compute atherogenic indices — atherogenic_indices
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   TG = c(150, 200),   HDL_c = c(50, 40),   TC = c(200, 220),   LDL_c = c(120, 150) ) cm <- list(TG = "TG", HDL_c = "HDL_c", TC = "TC", LDL_c = "LDL_c") atherogenic_indices(df, col_map = cm) #> atherogenic_indices(): reading input 'df' — 2 rows × 4 variables #> atherogenic_indices(): col_map (4 columns — 4 specified) #>   TG                ->  'TG' #>   HDL_c             ->  'HDL_c' #>   TC                ->  'TC' #>   LDL_c             ->  'LDL_c' #> atherogenic_indices(): computing markers: #>   AIP        [log10(TG / HDL_c)] #>   CRI_I      [TC / HDL_c [if TC available]] #>   CRI_II     [LDL_c / HDL_c [if LDL_c available]] #> atherogenic_indices(): results: AIP 2/2, CRI_I 2/2, CRI_II 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>     AIP CRI_I CRI_II #>   <dbl> <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1 0.477   4     2.4  #> 2 0.699   5.5   3.75

Example minimal: list(fev1_pct="FEV1pct", sixmwd="Walk_m", mmrc="mMRC", bmi="BMI") Example derive: list(fev1="FEV1", fev1_pred="FEV1_pred", sixmwd="Walk_m", mmrc="mMRC", bmi="BMI") Example spirometry_markers: list(fev1_pp="fev1_pp", sixmwd="Walk_m", mmrc="mMRC", bmi="BMI") na_action one c("keep","omit","error","ignore","warn"). verbose logical; TRUE (default) emits messages.
