title: Normalize a numeric vector — normalize_vec
text: Utility used across HealthMarkers normalize numeric vectors several common schemes handling edge cases (constant vectors, -NA, non-finite values) robustly. NA positions preserved.

title: Normalize a numeric vector — normalize_vec
code: normalize_vec(   x,   method = c("none", "z", "inverse", "range", "robust"),   na_rm = TRUE,   feature_range = c(0, 1),   invnorm_denominator = c("n", "n+1", "blom"),   ties = c("average", "first", "last", "random", "max", "min"),   warn_constant = TRUE )

title: Normalize a numeric vector — normalize_vec
text: x numeric (numeric-coercible) vector. method One c("none","z","inverse","range","robust"). Default "none". na_rm Logical; remove NAs estimating statistics (mean, sd, etc.). Default TRUE. feature_range Numeric length-2 vector giving target range method = "range". Default c(0, 1). invnorm_denominator One c("n","n+1","blom") controlling denominator inverse-normal transform: "n": p = (r - 0.5) / n  (Rankit; default) "n+1": p = (r - 0.5) / (n + 1) "blom": p = (r - 3/8) / (n + 1/4)  (Blom, 1958) ties Ties method passed base::rank method = "inverse". One c("average","first","last","random","max","min"). Default "average". warn_constant Logical; TRUE, warn input constant zero vector (lower bound range) returned. Default TRUE.

title: Normalize a numeric vector — normalize_vec
text: numeric vector length x.

title: Normalize a numeric vector — normalize_vec
text: Methods: "none": return input -(coercion; fully backward compatible). "z": z-score (mean 0, sd 1). Constant vectors return zeros (non-NA entries). "range": min-max target interval (default \([0,1]\)). Constant vectors return lower bound (mapped zeros). "robust": median/MAD scaling. Constant vectors (MAD=0) return zeros. "inverse": rank-based inverse normal transform (normal scores).

title: Normalize a numeric vector — normalize_vec
text: Beasley TM, Erickson S, Allison DB (2009). Rank-based inverse normal transformations increasingly used, merited? Behav Genet, 39(2):214-227. Leys C, Ley C, Klein O, Bernard P, Licata L (2013). Detecting outliers: use standard deviation around mean, use median absolute deviation around median. Front Psychol, 4:241. doi:10.3389/fpsyg.2013.00241  Bland JM, Altman DG (1996). Standard deviations standard errors. BMJ, 313(7047):41-42.

what: Compute a Suite of Nutrient-Based Health Markers — nutrient_markers
title: Compute a Suite of Nutrient-Based Health Markers — nutrient_markers
text: Given data frame tibble routine biochemical labs, nutrient_markers() returns set widely used ratios, products, simple percentages summarize iron metabolism, protein status, omega-3 balance, renal excretion, mineral homeostasis, aromatic amino-acid patterns.

title: Compute a Suite of Nutrient-Based Health Markers — nutrient_markers
code: nutrient_markers(   data,   col_map = NULL,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Compute a Suite of Nutrient-Based Health Markers — nutrient_markers
text: data data frame tibble containing subject-level data. col_map Optional named list mapping variable keys (see Details) column names data. need supply keys ; markers missing inputs return NA. NULL, defaults identity mapping known keys. na_action One c("keep","omit","error") controlling missing-data policy across columns referenced col_map. "keep" (default): keep NA; outputs become NA inputs NA. "omit": drop rows NA used input column. "error": abort used input contains NA. na_warn_prop Numeric \([0,1]\); per-variable threshold high-missingness diagnostics used input columns. Default 0.2. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints stepwise messages final summary via hm_inform. Default FALSE.

title: Compute a Suite of Nutrient-Based Health Markers — nutrient_markers
text: tibble one row per input row columns: FerritinTS, AGR, Omega3Index, Mg_Cr_Ratio, GlycatedAlbuminPct, UA_Cr_Ratio, BUN_Cr_Ratio, Ca_x_Phosphate, AnionGap, Tyr_Phe_Ratio.

title: Compute a Suite of Nutrient-Based Health Markers — nutrient_markers
text: Recognized markers (returned columns): FerritinTS: Ferritin / Transferrin saturation AGR: Albumin / Globulin, Globulin = Total protein - Albumin Omega3Index: EPA + DHA (percentage points) Mg_Cr_Ratio: Magnesium / Creatinine GlycatedAlbuminPct: (Glycated albumin / Albumin) x 100 UA_Cr_Ratio: Uric acid / Creatinine BUN_Cr_Ratio: BUN / Creatinine Ca_x_Phosphate: Calcium x Phosphate AnionGap: (Na + K) - (Cl + HCO3) Tyr_Phe_Ratio: Tyrosine / Phenylalanine Recognized col_map keys expected units (automatic conversion): ferritin: Serum ferritin (ng/mL) transferrin_sat: Transferrin saturation (%) albumin: Serum albumin (g/L) total_protein: Total serum protein (g/L) EPA: Red-cell EPA % total fatty acids DHA: Red-cell DHA % total fatty acids Mg: Serum magnesium (mmol/L) creatinine: Serum creatinine (umol/L) glycated_albumin: Glycated albumin (g/L) uric_acid: Serum uric acid (umol/L) BUN: Blood urea nitrogen (mg/dL) phosphate: Serum phosphate (mmol/L) calcium: Serum calcium (mmol/L) Na: Serum sodium (mmol/L) K: Serum potassium (mmol/L) Cl: Serum chloride (mmol/L) HCO3: Serum bicarbonate (mmol/L) Tyr: Serum tyrosine (umol/L) Phe: Serum phenylalanine (umol/L) Default extreme_rules (inputs) broad intended unit/entry checks: ferritin (0, 2000), transferrin_sat (0, 100), albumin (10, 60), total_protein (40, 100), EPA (0, 20), DHA (0, 20), Mg (0.2, 3), creatinine (20, 2000), glycated_albumin (0, 60), uric_acid (50, 1000), BUN (1, 150), phosphate (0.1, 5), calcium (0.5, 4), Na (100, 200), K (2, 8), Cl (70, 130), HCO3 (5, 45), Tyr (10, 300), Phe (20, 300).

what: Compute anthropometric obesity & adiposity indices — obesity_indices
title: Compute anthropometric obesity & adiposity indices — obesity_indices
text: Calculates comprehensive set body shape adiposity indices: BMI BMI categories Waist--hip ratio (WHR) optional WHR adjusted BMI (WHRadjBMI) Waist--height ratio (WHtR) Abdominal Volume Index (AVI) Body Adiposity Index (BAI) Body Shape Index (ABSI) Body Roundness Index (BRI) Conicity Index (CI) (Optional) Relative Fat Mass (RFM)

title: Compute anthropometric obesity & adiposity indices — obesity_indices
code: obesity_indices(   data,   weight,   height,   waist,   hip,   sex = NULL,   weight_unit = c("kg", "lb"),   height_unit = c("cm", "m"),   adjust_WHR = FALSE,   include_RFM = FALSE,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Compute anthropometric obesity & adiposity indices — obesity_indices
text: data data.frame tibble containing input columns. weight Unquoted column name weight. height Unquoted column name height. waist Unquoted column name waist circumference. hip Unquoted column name hip circumference. sex (Optional) Unquoted column name sex, coded 0=male, 1=female; required include_RFM=TRUE. weight_unit One c("kg","lb"); "lb", converts weight kg *0.45359237. height_unit One c("cm","m"); "cm", converts height metres /100. adjust_WHR Logical; TRUE, adds column WHRadjBMI residuals WHR ~ BMI. include_RFM Logical; TRUE, computes Relative Fat Mass (requires sex column). na_action One c("keep","omit","error") handling NA required inputs. Default "keep". na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) trigger high-missingness warnings required inputs. Default 0.2. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints column mapping computing messages.

title: Compute anthropometric obesity & adiposity indices — obesity_indices
text: tibble computed indices (slim output): weight_kg, height_m (unit-normalised intermediates), BMI, BMI_cat, WHR, WHRadjBMI (adjust_WHR = TRUE), waist_to_height_ratio, waist_to_BMI_ratio, weight_to_height_ratio, AVI, BAI, ABSI, BRI, CI, RFM (include_RFM = TRUE).

title: Compute anthropometric obesity & adiposity indices — obesity_indices
text: Units assumed (automatic conversion beyond specified weight/height options): weight: kg (lb weight_unit = "lb") height: m (cm height_unit = "cm") waist, hip: cm sex: 0 = male, 1 = female (required include_RFM = TRUE)

what: Calculate OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices — ogtt_is
title: Calculate OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices — ogtt_is
text: Given glucose & insulin 0, 30, 120 min (plus weight, BMI, age, sex), computes: Isi_120 Cederholm_index Gutt_index Avignon_Si0 Avignon_Si120 Avignon_Sim Modified_stumvoll Stumvoll_Demographics Matsuda_AUC Matsuda_ISI BigttSi Ifc_inv HIRI_inv Belfiore_isi_gly

title: Calculate OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices — ogtt_is
code: ogtt_is(   data,   col_map = NULL,   normalize = "none",   verbose = TRUE,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2 )

title: Calculate OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices — ogtt_is
text: data data.frame tibble containing least columns mapped col_map. col_map Named list mapping: G0, G30, G120 -> glucose 0, 30, 120 min (mmol/L) I0, I30, I120 -> insulin 0, 30, 120 min (pmol/L) weight -> body weight (kg) bmi -> body-mass index (kg/m^2) age -> age (years) sex -> sex (1 = male, 2 = female) normalize One c("none","z","inverse","range","robust") used normalize_vec(). verbose Logical; TRUE (default), prints column mapping, list indices computed, per-column results summary. na_action One c("keep","omit","error") missing/non-finite required inputs. Default "keep". na_warn_prop Proportion (0-1) high-missingness diagnostics (debug). Default 0.2.

title: Calculate OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices — ogtt_is
text: tibble OGTT-based index columns listed . ID column detected data (e.g. id, IID, participant_id), prepended first output column.

title: Calculate OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices — ogtt_is
text: Units assumed: OGTT glucose mmol/L (internally converted mg/dL via *18 select indices) OGTT insulin pmol/L (internally converted muU/mL via /6 select indices) weight kg; BMI kg/m^2; age years; sex coded 1 = male, 2 = female Notes Conversions mirror existing implementation preserve outputs. formulas intentionally use unconverted inputs (prior code). Logs safe: log(x) becomes NA x <= 0 non-finite.

title: Calculate OGTT-based insulin sensitivity indices — ogtt_is
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   G0 = 5.5, I0 = 60,   G30 = 7.8, I30 = 90,   G120 = 6.2, I120 = 50,   weight = 70, bmi = 24, age = 30, sex = 1 ) ogtt_is(   df,   col_map = list(     G0 = "G0", I0 = "I0",     G30 = "G30", I30 = "I30",     G120 = "G120", I120 = "I120",     weight = "weight", bmi = "bmi",     age = "age", sex = "sex"   ),   normalize = "none",   verbose = TRUE ) #> ogtt_is(): reading input 'df' — 1 rows × 10 variables #> ogtt_is(): col_map (10 columns — 10 specified) #>   G0                ->  'G0' #>   I0                ->  'I0' #>   G30               ->  'G30' #>   I30               ->  'I30' #>   G120              ->  'G120' #>   I120              ->  'I120' #>   weight            ->  'weight' #>   bmi               ->  'bmi' #>   age               ->  'age' #>   sex               ->  'sex' #> ogtt_is(): computing markers: #>   Isi_120                [G120, I120] #>   Cederholm_index        [G0, G120, I0, I120, weight] #>   Gutt_index             [G0, G120, I0, I120, weight] #>   Avignon_Si0            [G0, I0, weight] #>   Avignon_Si120          [G120, I120, weight] #>   Avignon_Sim            [Si0, Si120] #>   Modified_stumvoll      [I0, I120, G120] #>   Stumvoll_Demog.        [bmi, I120, age] #>   Matsuda_AUC            [G0, I0, G_AUC, I_AUC] #>   Matsuda_ISI            [G0, I0, G_mean, I_mean] #>   BigttSi                [I0, I30, I120, G0, G30, G120, sex, bmi] #>   Ifc_inv                [I0, I120] #>   HIRI_inv               [G0, G30, I0, I30] #>   Belfiore_isi_gly       [I_AUC, G_AUC] #> ogtt_is(): results: Isi_120 1/1, Cederholm_index 1/1, Gutt_index 1/1, Avignon_Si0 1/1, Avignon_Si120 1/1, Avignon_Sim 1/1, Modified_stumvoll 1/1, Stumvoll_Demographics 1/1, Matsuda_AUC 1/1, Matsuda_ISI 1/1, BigttSi 1/1, Ifc_inv 1/1, HIRI_inv 1/1, Belfiore_isi_gly 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 14 #>   Isi_120 Cederholm_index Gutt_index Avignon_Si0 Avignon_Si120 Avignon_Sim #>     <dbl>           <dbl>      <dbl>       <dbl>         <dbl>       <dbl> #> 1    10.8            1.10       2.67        9.62          10.2        9.93 #> # ℹ 8 more variables: Modified_stumvoll <dbl>, Stumvoll_Demographics <dbl>, #> #   Matsuda_AUC <dbl>, Matsuda_ISI <dbl>, BigttSi <dbl>, Ifc_inv <dbl>, #> #   HIRI_inv <dbl>, Belfiore_isi_gly <dbl>

what: Plot FI vs age (convenience wrapper) — plot_frailty_age
title: Plot FI vs age (convenience wrapper) — plot_frailty_age
text: Calls frailty_index() visible = TRUE; see frailty_index() arguments, validation, references.

title: Plot FI vs age (convenience wrapper) — plot_frailty_age
code: plot_frailty_age(   data,   cols = NULL,   invert = NULL,   rescale = TRUE,   age = NULL,   rescale.custom = NULL,   rescale.avoid = NULL,   bins = 7,   na_action = c("ignore", "warn", "error", "keep", "omit"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   return = c("list", "data"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Plot FI vs age (convenience wrapper) — plot_frailty_age
text: data data.frame tibble health deficits (ideally binary/logical scaled \([0,1]\)). Non-binary numeric columns can rescaled di::di rescale = TRUE. cols Character vector deficit column names use. NULL (default), numeric columns used except age (supplied). invert Character vector column names whose values inverted di::di (e.g., higher values indicate better health). rescale Logical; TRUE, non-binary columns rescaled \([0,1]\) di::di. Default TRUE. age Optional name column holding age (used di plotting optional age-binned outputs; excluded auto-selected cols). rescale.custom Advanced argument passed di::di. See di::di documentation syntax. rescale.avoid Advanced argument passed di::di; see di::di documentation syntax. bins Integer; number age bins FI--age plots. Default 7. na_action One c("keep","omit","error","warn","ignore"). Controls handling missing values selected deficit columns. "keep" (backward-compatible alias "ignore") passes NAs di::di. "warn" emits warning keeps NAs (alias "keep" missingness warning). "omit" drops rows NA selected deficits computing. "error" stops NA detected. Default "ignore" (retained backward compatibility; equivalent "keep"). na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) high-missingness warning emitted (per column) na_action = "warn". Default 0.2. return One c("list","data"). "list" (default) returns original di::di result (backward compatible). "data" returns tibble one row per individual, columns: di (frailty index) plus selected deficit columns (post-capping applied). Age included present. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress completion summary.

title: Plot FI vs age (convenience wrapper) — plot_frailty_age
text: object returned frailty_index() (di::di object return="list").

title: Plot FI vs age (convenience wrapper) — plot_frailty_age
code: if (requireNamespace("di", quietly = TRUE)) {   df <- data.frame(age = c(70, 75, 80), d1 = c(0, 1, 1),     d2 = c(0.2, 0.8, 1.0), d3 = c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE))   plot_frailty_age(df, cols = c("d1", "d2", "d3"), age = "age") } #> frailty_index(): preparing inputs (3 rows, 3 deficits, age provided) #> frailty_index(): col_map: d1, d2, d3  #> frailty_index(): results: di range [0.333, 1.000] (3 rows, 3 deficits) #> $di #> [1] 0.3333333 0.5833333 1.0000000 #>  #> $columns #>   d1   d2 d3 #> 1  0 0.00  1 #> 2  1 0.75  0 #> 3  1 1.00  1 #>

what: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
title: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
text: Uses rspiro reference equations compute predicted normals, z-scores, percent predicted lower limits normal (LLN) FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC ratio.

title: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
code: pulmo_markers(   data,   col_map = NULL,   equation = c("GLI", "GLIgl", "NHANES3"),   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
text: data data.frame tibble columns: age       (numeric): years sex       (character numeric): "male"/"female" (case-insensitive) codes 1/2 0/1 height    (numeric): cm m (auto-detected) ethnicity (character): e.g. "Caucasian", "African-American", "NE Asian", "SE Asian", "/Mixed" fev1      (numeric): observed FEV1 L fvc       (numeric): observed FVC L col_map Optional named list mapping canonical keys (age, sex, height, ethnicity, fev1, fvc) actual column names data. NULL, column names inferred automatically. equation One c("GLI","GLIgl","NHANES3") (see rspiro details). GLIgl ignores ethnicity. na_action One c("keep","omit","error") handling missing values required inputs. Default "keep". na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) trigger high-missingness warnings required inputs. Default 0.2. verbose Logical; TRUE prints progress messages completion summary.

title: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
text: tibble columns: fev1_pred, fev1_z,   fev1_pctpred, fev1_LLN fvc_pred,  fvc_z,    fvc_pctpred,  fvc_LLN fev1_fvc_ratio, fev1_fvc_pred, fev1_fvc_z, fev1_fvc_pctpred, fev1_fvc_LLN

title: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
text: Inputs validated, missingness handled via na_action, heights auto-detected cm height > 3; otherwise interpreted metres (automatic unit conversion beyond heuristic, preserving prior behavior).

title: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
text: Quanjer PH, Stanojevic S, Cole TJ, Baur X, Hall GL, Culver BH, et al. (2012). “Multi-ethnic reference values spirometry 3–95-yr age range: global lung function 2012 equations.” European Respiratory Journal, 40, 1324–1343. doi:10.1183/09031936.00080312 .  Hankinson JL, Odencrantz JR, Fedan KB (1999). “Spirometric reference values sample general U.S. population.” American Journal Respiratory Critical Care Medicine, 159, 179–187. doi:10.1164/ajrccm.159.1.9712108 .  Bowerman SD, Quanjer PH, others (2023). “GLI-Global update.” European Respiratory Journal, 61, 2201632. DOI resolvable Crossref 2026-03-16.

title: Calculate pulmonary function markers (FEV1/FVC, z-scores, percent predicted, LLN, etc.) — pulmo_markers
code: if (requireNamespace("rspiro", quietly = TRUE)) {   df <- data.frame(     age = c(40, 55), sex = c("male", "female"),     height = c(175, 162), ethnicity = c("Caucasian", "Caucasian"),     fev1 = c(3.5, 2.4), fvc = c(4.4, 3.1)   )   pulmo_markers(df) } #> pulmo_markers(): reading input 'df' — 2 rows × 6 variables #> pulmo_markers(): preparing inputs [GLI] #> pulmo_markers(): col_map (6 columns — 6 inferred from data) #>   age               ->  'age'    (inferred) #>   sex               ->  'sex'    (inferred) #>   height            ->  'height'    (inferred) #>   ethnicity         ->  'ethnicity'    (inferred) #>   fev1              ->  'fev1'    (inferred) #>   fvc               ->  'fvc'    (inferred) #> pulmo_markers(): computing markers: #>   fev1_pred, fev1_z, fev1_pctpred, fev1_LLN [age, height, sex, ethnicity, fev1] #>   fvc_pred, fvc_z, fvc_pctpred, fvc_LLN [age, height, sex, ethnicity, fvc] #>   fev1_fvc_ratio, fev1_fvc_pred, fev1_fvc_z, fev1_fvc_pctpred, fev1_fvc_LLN [fev1, fvc] #> pulmo_markers(): converting height from cm to m #> pulmo_markers(): results: fev1_pred 2/2, fev1_z 2/2, fev1_pctpred 2/2, fev1_LLN 2/2, fvc_pred 2/2, fvc_z 2/2, fvc_pctpred 2/2, fvc_LLN 2/2, fev1_fvc_ratio 2/2, fev1_fvc_pred 2/2, fev1_fvc_z 2/2, fev1_fvc_pctpred 2/2, fev1_fvc_LLN 0/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 13 #>   fev1_pred fev1_z fev1_pctpred fev1_LLN fvc_pred  fvc_z fvc_pctpred fvc_LLN #>       <dbl>  <dbl>        <dbl>    <dbl>    <dbl>  <dbl>       <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1      4.08 -1.13          85.8     3.23     5.05 -1.04         87.0    4.02 #> 2      2.62 -0.595         91.7     2.01     3.29 -0.410        94.2    2.54 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: fev1_fvc_ratio <dbl>, fev1_fvc_pred <dbl>, #> #   fev1_fvc_z <dbl>, fev1_fvc_pctpred <dbl>, fev1_fvc_LLN <dbl>

what: Calculate a Suite of Renal Function, Injury, and Excretion Markers — renal_markers
title: Calculate a Suite of Renal Function, Injury, and Excretion Markers — renal_markers
text: Given routine blood urine assays, renal_markers() computes: eGFR_cr: CKD-EPI creatinine equation (2009 variant; race factor retained preserve prior behavior) eGFR_cys: CKD-EPI cystatin C equation (cystatin_C provided) eGFR_combined: CKD-EPI combined creatinine+cystatin C (provided) BUN_Cr_ratio: Blood urea nitrogen / serum creatinine FE_Urea: Fractional excretion urea (%) NGAL, KIM1, NAG, Beta2Micro, IL18, L_FABP: pass-urinary injury markers (mapped)

title: Calculate a Suite of Renal Function, Injury, and Excretion Markers — renal_markers
code: renal_markers(   data,   col_map = NULL,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Calculate a Suite of Renal Function, Injury, and Excretion Markers — renal_markers
text: data data.frame tibble renal lab data. col_map Named list mapping: creatinine -> serum creatinine (mg/dL) age -> age (years) sex -> sex indicator (1 = male, 0 = female). Also accepts "male"/"female". race -> race ("white", "black", ""). Also accepts common aliases. BUN -> blood urea nitrogen (mg/dL) optional cystatin_C -> serum cystatin C (mg/L) optional urea_serum -> serum urea (mg/dL) optional creatinine_urine -> urine creatinine (mg/dL) optional urea_urine -> urine urea (mg/dL) optional NGAL, KIM1, NAG, beta2_micro, IL18, L_FABP -> urine injury markers na_action One c("keep","omit","error") handling missing values required inputs. Default "keep". na_warn_prop Proportion (0-1) threshold high-missingness diagnostics. Default 0.2. verbose Logical; TRUE (default), prints step--step progress including column mapping, optional input availability, physiological range information (informational , values altered), list markers computed, per-column results summary.

title: Calculate a Suite of Renal Function, Injury, and Excretion Markers — renal_markers
text: tibble computed renal markers: eGFR_cr, eGFR_cys, eGFR_combined, BUN_Cr_ratio, FE_Urea, NGAL, KIM1, NAG, Beta2Micro, IL18, L_FABP. ID column detected data (e.g. id, IID, participant_id), prepended first output column.

title: Calculate a Suite of Renal Function, Injury, and Excretion Markers — renal_markers
text: Robust validation applied, including NA handling (na_action), high-missingness diagnostics, safe divisions consolidated zero-denominator warning, optional input extremes scan/cap. New arguments appended backward compatibility. Expected units (automatic conversion performed): creatinine (serum): mg/dL cystatin C (serum): mg/L BUN (serum): mg/dL urea_serum, urea_urine: mg/dL creatinine_urine: mg/dL

title: Calculate a Suite of Renal Function, Injury, and Excretion Markers — renal_markers
text: Levey , Stevens LA, Schmid CH, others (2009). “new equation estimate glomerular filtration rate.” Annals Internal Medicine, 150(9), 604–612. doi:10.7326/0003-4819-150-9-200905050-00006 .  Inker LA, Schmid CH, Tighiouart H, others (2012). “Estimating glomerular filtration rate serum cystatin C.” New England Journal Medicine, 367(1), 20–29. doi:10.1056/NEJMoa1114248 .  Inker LA, Eneanya ND, Coresh J, others (2021). “New creatinine- cystatin C-based equations estimating GFR without race.” New England Journal Medicine, 385(19), 1737–1749. doi:10.1056/NEJMoa2102953 .  Kaplan AA, Kohn (1992). “Fractional Excretion Urea Guide Renal Dysfunction.” American Journal Nephrology, 12(1–2), 49–54. doi:10.1159/000168417 .  Parikh CR, Coca SG, Thiessen-Philbrook H, others (2011). “Postoperative Biomarkers Predict Acute Kidney Injury Poor Outcomes Adult Cardiac Surgery.” Journal American Society Nephrology, 22(12), 1737–1747. doi:10.1681/ASN.2010121302 .  Vaidya VS, Ramirez V, Ichimura T, others (2010). “Kidney injury molecule-1 outperforms traditional biomarkers kidney injury preclinical biomarker qualification studies.” Nature Biotechnology, 28(5), 478–485. doi:10.1038/nbt.1623 .  Portilla D, Dent C, Sugaya T, others (2008). “Urinary liver-type fatty acid-binding protein biomarker acute kidney injury.” Kidney International, 73(4), 465–472. doi:10.1038/sj.ki.5002721 .

title: Calculate a Suite of Renal Function, Injury, and Excretion Markers — renal_markers
code: df <- tibble::tibble(Cr = 1.0, Age = 40, Sex = 1, Race = "white", BUN = 14) cm <- list(creatinine = "Cr", age = "Age", sex = "Sex", race = "Race", BUN = "BUN") renal_markers(df, cm) #> renal_markers(): reading input 'df' — 1 rows × 5 variables #> renal_markers(): col_map (5 columns — 5 specified) #>   creatinine        ->  'Cr' #>   age               ->  'Age' #>   sex               ->  'Sex' #>   race              ->  'Race' #>   BUN               ->  'BUN' #> renal_markers(): optional inputs #>   missing:  cystatin_C, urea_serum, creatinine_urine, urea_urine, NGAL, KIM1, NAG, beta2_micro, IL18, L_FABP #>   indices -> NA: #>   eGFR_cys -> NA  [missing: cystatin_C] #>   eGFR_combined -> NA  [missing: cystatin_C] #>   FE_Urea -> NA  [missing: urea_serum, creatinine_urine, urea_urine] #> renal_markers(): computing markers: #>   eGFR_cr        [creatinine, age, sex, race] #>   eGFR_cys       NA [cystatin_C missing] #>   eGFR_combined  NA [cystatin_C missing] #>   BUN_Cr_ratio   [BUN, creatinine] #>   FE_Urea        NA [urea/urine inputs missing] #> renal_markers(): results: eGFR_cr 1/1, eGFR_cys 0/1, eGFR_combined 0/1, BUN_Cr_ratio 1/1, FE_Urea 0/1, NGAL 0/1, KIM1 0/1, NAG 0/1, Beta2Micro 0/1, IL18 0/1, L_FABP 0/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 11 #>   eGFR_cr eGFR_cys eGFR_combined BUN_Cr_ratio FE_Urea  NGAL  KIM1   NAG #>     <dbl>    <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>   <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> #> 1    93.7       NA            NA           14      NA    NA    NA    NA #> # ℹ 3 more variables: Beta2Micro <dbl>, IL18 <dbl>, L_FABP <dbl>

what: Calculate saliva-based stress & glycemic markers — saliva_markers
title: Calculate saliva-based stress & glycemic markers — saliva_markers
text: Computes: log_cortisol_wake (log-transformed waking cortisol) CAR_AUC           (Cortisol Awakening Response, trapezoidal AUC 0-60 min default) log_amylase       (log-transformed salivary alpha-amylase) saliva_glucose    (raw salivary glucose)

title: Calculate saliva-based stress & glycemic markers — saliva_markers
code: saliva_markers(   data,   col_map = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   times = c(0, 30, 60) )

title: Calculate saliva-based stress & glycemic markers — saliva_markers
text: data data.frame tibble salivary markers. col_map Named list mapping required inputs. Defaults assume names: cort1   -> "saliva_cort1" (nmol/L wake) cort2   -> "saliva_cort2" (nmol/L ~30 min) cort3   -> "saliva_cort3" (nmol/L ~60 min) amylase -> "saliva_amylase" (U/mL) glucose -> "saliva_glucose" (mg/dL) verbose Logical; TRUE (default), prints column mapping, input availability, physiological range information (informational , values altered), list markers computed inputs, per-column results summary. na_action One c("keep","omit","error") required-input NA handling. Default "keep". na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) trigger high-missingness diagnostics (debug). Default 0.2. times Numeric vector sampling times (minutes) CAR AUC. Must align cort1/2/3. Default c(0,30,60).

title: Calculate saliva-based stress & glycemic markers — saliva_markers
text: tibble columns: log_cortisol_wake CAR_AUC log_amylase saliva_glucose ID column detected data (e.g. id, IID, participant_id), prepended first output column.

title: Calculate saliva-based stress & glycemic markers — saliva_markers
text: Inputs validated, missingness handled via na_action, logs made safe (<= 0 -> NA), optional extremes scan/cap available.

title: Calculate saliva-based stress & glycemic markers — saliva_markers

Salivary cortisol psychoneuroendocrine research: recent developments applications. Stress. 2004;7(1):29-37. doi:10.1080/10253890410001667205  (Cortisol awakening response) Nater UM, Rohleder N. Salivary alpha-amylase non-invasive biomarker sympathetic nervous system: current state research. Psychoneuroendocrinology. 2009;34(4):486-496. doi:10.1016/j.psyneuen.2009.01.014  (Salivary alpha-amylase marker) Scales , Freeman EW, McCoy NL, Klerman EB. Salivary glucose measure blood glucose: correlations applications. Diabetes Care. 1987;10(4):414-418. doi:10.2337/diacare.10.4.414  (Salivary glucose application)

title: Calculate saliva-based stress & glycemic markers — saliva_markers
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   saliva_cort1    = 12.5,   saliva_cort2    = 18.0,   saliva_cort3    = 16.2,   saliva_amylase  = 85,   saliva_glucose  = 4.2 ) saliva_markers(df)  # uses default col_map #> saliva_markers(): reading input 'df' — 1 rows × 5 variables #> saliva_markers(): col_map (5 columns — 5 inferred from data) #>   cort1             ->  'saliva_cort1'    (inferred) #>   cort2             ->  'saliva_cort2'    (inferred) #>   cort3             ->  'saliva_cort3'    (inferred) #>   amylase           ->  'saliva_amylase'    (inferred) #>   glucose           ->  'saliva_glucose'    (inferred) #> saliva_markers(): optional inputs #>   present:  cort1, cort2, cort3, amylase, glucose #> saliva_markers(): computing markers: #>   log_cortisol_wake    [cort1] #>   CAR_AUC              [cort1, cort2, cort3] #>   log_amylase          [amylase] #>   saliva_glucose       [glucose] #> saliva_markers(): results: log_cortisol_wake 1/1, CAR_AUC 1/1, log_amylase 1/1, saliva_glucose 1/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 4 #>   log_cortisol_wake CAR_AUC log_amylase saliva_glucose #>               <dbl>   <dbl>       <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1              2.53    970.        4.44            4.2

title: SARC-F Sarcopenia Screening Score — sarc_f_score
text: Computes SARC-F questionnaire score, quick screening tool sarcopenia risk.

title: SARC-F Sarcopenia Screening Score — sarc_f_score
code: sarc_f_score(   data,   col_map = NULL,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error", "ignore", "warn"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: SARC-F Sarcopenia Screening Score — sarc_f_score
text: data data.frame tibble SARC-F questionnaire responses. col_map Named list mapping five SARC-F components columns: strength, walking, chair, stairs, falls. na_action One c("keep","omit","error","ignore","warn"). verbose Logical; TRUE (default), emits progress messages.

title: SARC-F Sarcopenia Screening Score — sarc_f_score
text: tibble : sarc_f_score (numeric 0-10; NA component NA) sarc_f_high_risk (logical; TRUE score >= 4, NA score NA)

title: SARC-F Sarcopenia Screening Score — sarc_f_score
text: SARC-F 5 items: Strength, Assistance walking, Rise chair, Climb stairs, Falls. item scored 0 (difficulty) 2 (high difficulty). Total SARC-F score ranges 0-10. score >= 4 indicates high risk sarcopenia suggests assessment.

title: SARC-F Sarcopenia Screening Score — sarc_f_score
text: Malmstrom TK, Morley JE (2013). “SARC-F: simple questionnaire rapidly diagnose sarcopenia.” Journal American Medical Directors Association, 14(8), 531–532. doi:10.1016/j.jamda.2013.05.018 . ; Malmstrom TK, Miller DK, Simonsick EM, Ferrucci L, Morley JE (2015). “SARC-F: symptom score predict persons sarcopenia risk poor functional outcomes.” Journal Cachexia, Sarcopenia Muscle, 7(1), 28–36. doi:10.1002/jcsm.12048 .

title: SARC-F Sarcopenia Screening Score — sarc_f_score
code: df <- data.frame(Strength = c(1, 2, 0), Walking = c(0, 1, 2),                  Chair = c(1, 1, 2), Stairs = c(0, 2, 2), Falls = c(0, 1, 1)) sarc_f_score(df) #> sarc_f_score(): reading input 'df' — 3 rows × 5 variables #> sarc_f_score(): col_map (5 columns — 5 inferred from data) #>   strength          ->  'Strength'    (inferred) #>   walking           ->  'Walking'    (inferred) #>   chair             ->  'Chair'    (inferred) #>   stairs            ->  'Stairs'    (inferred) #>   falls             ->  'Falls'    (inferred) #> sarc_f_score(): computing markers: #>   sarc_f_score      [0-10 sum] #>   sarc_f_high_risk  [score >= 4] #> sarc_f_score(): results: sarc_f_score 3/3, sarc_f_high_risk 3/3 #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   sarc_f_score sarc_f_high_risk #>          <dbl> <lgl>            #> 1            2 FALSE            #> 2            7 TRUE             #> 3            7 TRUE

title: Schizotypal Personality Questionnaire (key-driven) — spq_score
text: Schizotypal Personality Questionnaire (key-driven)

title: Schizotypal Personality Questionnaire (key-driven) — spq_score
code: spq_score(   data,   col_map = list(),   key,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   missing_prop_max = 0.2,   impute = c("none", "mean"),   prefix = "SPQ",   verbose = TRUE )

title: Schizotypal Personality Questionnaire (key-driven) — spq_score
text: data Data frame containing questionnaire item columns. col_map Named list mapping canonical item IDs column names; defaults assume items already named. key List items, min_val, max_val, optional reverse subscales. na_action handle rows missing items: keep, omit, error. missing_prop_max Maximum allowed proportion missing items per row score set NA. impute Imputation strategy missing items threshold: none mean (row-wise mean). prefix Prefix output column names. verbose Logical; TRUE, emits informational messages column resolution scoring progress via hm_inform().

title: Schizotypal Personality Questionnaire (key-driven) — spq_score
text: tibble score columns (total optional subscales). Input columns included.

title: Schizotypal Personality Questionnaire (key-driven) — spq_score
text: Raine (1991). “SPQ: Scale Assessment Schizotypal Personality Based DSM-III-R Criteria.” Schizophrenia Bulletin, 17(4), 555–564. doi:10.1093/schbul/17.4.555 .

title: Schizotypal Personality Questionnaire (key-driven) — spq_score
code: spq_key <- list(items = sprintf("spq_%02d", 1:5), min_val = 0, max_val = 1) df <- data.frame(spq_01 = 0, spq_02 = 1, spq_03 = 0, spq_04 = 1, spq_05 = 0) spq_score(df, key = spq_key) #> # A tibble: 1 × 1 #>   SPQ_total #>       <dbl> #> 1         2

what: Compute tracer/DXA-based insulin sensitivity indices — tracer_dxa_is
title: Compute tracer/DXA-based insulin sensitivity indices — tracer_dxa_is
text: Uses stable isotope tracer infusion rates DXA-measured fat mass compute peripheral adipose insulin sensitivity related metrics.

title: Compute tracer/DXA-based insulin sensitivity indices — tracer_dxa_is
code: tracer_dxa_is(   data,   col_map = NULL,   normalize = NULL,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Compute tracer/DXA-based insulin sensitivity indices — tracer_dxa_is
text: data data.frame tibble containing raw measurements. col_map Named list entries (depending mode): Adipose-required: - I0: fasting insulin (pmol/L) - rate_glycerol, rate_palmitate: tracer rates (mumol/min) - fat_mass, weight, bmi: body composition - HDL_c: HDL cholesterol (mmol/L) Full mode additionally requires: - G0, G30, G120: glucose (mmol/L) - I30, I120: insulin (pmol/L) - TG: triglycerides (mmol/L) - FFA: free fatty acids (mmol/L) normalize Ignored (kept backward compatibility). na_action One c("keep","omit","error") NA handling required inputs. Default "keep". na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) trigger high-missingness warnings required inputs. Default 0.2. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress messages completion summary.

title: Compute tracer/DXA-based insulin sensitivity indices — tracer_dxa_is
text: Adipose-tibble columns: LIRI_inv, Lipo_inv, ATIRI_inv Full-mode tibble columns: I_AUC, FFA_AUC, tracer_palmitate_SI, tracer_glycerol_SI, LIRI_inv, Lipo_inv, ATIRI_inv

title: Compute tracer/DXA-based insulin sensitivity indices — tracer_dxa_is
text: Modes: Adipose-indices adipose-related keys mapped (OGTT glucose/insulin time series) Full indices otherwise Expected units: Glucose: mmol/L (internally converted mg/dL needed) Insulin: pmol/L (internally converted muU/mL via /6) TG: mmol/L (mg/dL via *88.57); HDL-c: mmol/L (mg/dL via *38.67) Tracer rates: mumol/min Fat mass, weight: kg; BMI: kg/m^2

title: Compute tracer/DXA-based insulin sensitivity indices — tracer_dxa_is
code: df <- data.frame(   I0 = c(60, 75), rate_glycerol = c(2.1, 2.8), rate_palmitate = c(1.8, 2.3),   fat_mass = c(18, 24), weight = c(72, 85), BMI = c(24, 29),   HDL_c = c(1.3, 1.1) ) col_map <- list(I0="I0", rate_glycerol="rate_glycerol",                 rate_palmitate="rate_palmitate", fat_mass="fat_mass",                 weight="weight", bmi="BMI", HDL_c="HDL_c") tracer_dxa_is(df, col_map = col_map) #> tracer_dxa_is(): reading input 'df' — 2 rows × 7 variables #> tracer_dxa_is(): preparing inputs #> tracer_dxa_is(): col_map (7 columns — 7 specified) #>   I0                ->  'I0' #>   rate_glycerol     ->  'rate_glycerol' #>   rate_palmitate    ->  'rate_palmitate' #>   fat_mass          ->  'fat_mass' #>   weight            ->  'weight' #>   bmi               ->  'BMI' #>   HDL_c             ->  'HDL_c' #> tracer_dxa_is(): computing markers: #>   LIRI_inv, Lipo_inv, ATIRI_inv #> tracer_dxa_is(): adipose-only indices #> tracer_dxa_is(): results: LIRI_inv 2/2, Lipo_inv 2/2, ATIRI_inv 2/2 #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   LIRI_inv Lipo_inv ATIRI_inv #>      <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1    -1.01      -21     -18   #> 2    -1.13      -35     -28.7

what: Calculate urine-only renal and tubular markers (research-ready) — urine_markers
title: Calculate urine-only renal and tubular markers (research-ready) — urine_markers
text: Computes (urine-): UACR (Albumin--Creatinine Ratio, mg/g) albuminuria_stage (KDIGO A1/A2/A3 UACR) microalbuminuria flag ("normal" vs "micro") UPCR (Urine Protein--Creatinine Ratio, mg/g; urine_protein available) U_Na_K_ratio (urine Na+/K+; urine_Na urine_K available) Creatinine-normalized tubular markers (present, per g creatinine): NGAL_per_gCr, KIM1_per_gCr, NAG_per_gCr, Beta2Micro_per_gCr, A1Micro_per_gCr, IL18_per_gCr, L_FABP_per_gCr

title: Calculate urine-only renal and tubular markers (research-ready) — urine_markers
code: urine_markers(   data,   col_map = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2 )

title: Calculate urine-only renal and tubular markers (research-ready) — urine_markers
text: data data.frame tibble least urine_albumin urine_creatinine. col_map Optional named list mapping canonical keys (e.g., urine_albumin, urine_creatinine) actual column names data. NULL, column names inferred automatically. verbose Logical; TRUE, prints progress messages completion summary. Default FALSE. na_action One c("keep","omit","error") handling missing values required inputs. Default "keep". na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) trigger high-missingness warnings required inputs. Default 0.2.

title: Calculate urine-only renal and tubular markers (research-ready) — urine_markers
text: tibble columns: UACR, albuminuria_stage, microalbuminuria, UPCR, U_Na_K_ratio, NGAL_per_gCr, KIM1_per_gCr, NAG_per_gCr, Beta2Micro_per_gCr, A1Micro_per_gCr, IL18_per_gCr, L_FABP_per_gCr

title: Calculate urine-only renal and tubular markers (research-ready) — urine_markers
text: Inputs validated, missingness handled via na_action, divisions safeguarded (Inf/NaN -> NA) consolidated zero-denominator warning, optional extremes scan/cap available. Expected units: urine_albumin: mg/L urine_protein: mg/L (optional) urine_creatinine: mg/dL urine_Na, urine_K: mmol/L (optional) Optional tubular markers assumed mg/L normalized per g creatinine

Liver fatty acid-binding protein biomarker acute kidney injury cardiac surgery. Kidney Int. 2008;73(4):465-472. doi:10.1038/sj.ki.5002688  (L-FABP biomarker validation)

code: df <- tibble::tibble(   urine_albumin    = 30,   urine_creatinine = 1.2,   serum_creatinine = 0.9,   plasma_Na        = 140,   urine_Na         = 100,   age              = 55,   sex              = 2,   urine_protein    = 150 ) urine_markers(df) #> urine_markers(): reading input 'df' — 1 rows × 8 variables #> urine_markers(): col_map (4 columns — 4 inferred from data) #>   urine_albumin     ->  'urine_albumin'    (inferred) #>   urine_creatinine  ->  'urine_creatinine'    (inferred) #>   urine_protein     ->  'urine_protein'    (inferred) #>   urine_Na          ->  'urine_Na'    (inferred) #> urine_markers(): computing markers: #>   UACR, albuminuria_stage, microalbuminuria [urine_albumin, urine_creatinine] #>   UPCR [urine_protein, urine_creatinine] #>   U_Na_K_ratio [urine_Na, urine_K] #>   NGAL/KIM1/NAG/Beta2Micro/A1Micro/IL18/L_FABP per gCr [optional] #> urine_markers(): results: UACR 1/1, albuminuria_stage 1/1, microalbuminuria 1/1, UPCR 1/1, U_Na_K_ratio 0/1, NGAL_per_gCr 0/1, KIM1_per_gCr 0/1, NAG_per_gCr 0/1, Beta2Micro_per_gCr 0/1, A1Micro_per_gCr 0/1, IL18_per_gCr 0/1, L_FABP_per_gCr 0/1 #> # A tibble: 1 × 12 #>    UACR albuminuria_stage microalbuminuria  UPCR U_Na_K_ratio NGAL_per_gCr #>   <dbl> <fct>             <fct>            <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 25000 A3                normal           12500           NA           NA #> # ℹ 6 more variables: KIM1_per_gCr <dbl>, NAG_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   Beta2Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, A1Micro_per_gCr <dbl>, IL18_per_gCr <dbl>, #> #   L_FABP_per_gCr <dbl>

what: Validate required inputs for a calling function — validate_inputs
title: Validate required inputs for a calling function — validate_inputs
text: Ensures required keys exist col_map non-empty mappings. Missing keys reported stable order aligned tests.

title: Validate required inputs for a calling function — validate_inputs
code: validate_inputs(data, col_map, fun_name, required_keys = NULL)

title: Validate required inputs for a calling function — validate_inputs
text: data data.frame tibble col_map named list mapping keys column names fun_name character scalar naming calling function (e.g., "lipid_markers"). Used look built-required keys required_keys supplied. required_keys optional character vector required col_map keys. supplied, takes precedence fun_name built-lookup, making function useful caller regardless fun_name.

title: Validate required inputs for a calling function — validate_inputs
text: invisibly TRUE success; otherwise aborts

title: Validate required inputs for a calling function — validate_inputs
code: df <- data.frame(TG = c(1.5, 2.0), HDL_c = c(1.2, 1.0),   LDL_c = c(2.0, 2.5), TC = c(4.5, 5.0)) # Using built-in lookup validate_inputs(df,   list(TG = "TG", HDL_c = "HDL_c", LDL_c = "LDL_c", TC = "TC"),   fun_name = "lipid_markers") # Using explicit required_keys (works for any function) validate_inputs(df,   list(TG = "TG", HDL_c = "HDL_c"),   fun_name = "my_function",   required_keys = c("TG", "HDL_c"))

title: Vitamin D Status Category — vitamin_d_status

title: Vitamin D Status Category — vitamin_d_status
code: vitamin_d_status(   data,   col_map = NULL,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error", "ignore", "warn"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Vitamin D Status Category — vitamin_d_status
text: data data.frame tibble 25-hydroxyvitamin D concentration column. col_map named list vitamin_d giving column name data 25(OH)D. na_action One c("keep","omit","error","ignore","warn"). keep/ignore: compute propagate NA omit: drop rows NA required input error: abort required input contains NA warn: like keep, emit missingness warnings verbose Logical; TRUE, emits progress via rlang::inform.

title: Vitamin D Status Category — vitamin_d_status
text: tibble one column: vitamin_d_status (ordered factor levels "Deficient","Insufficient","Sufficient").

title: Vitamin D Status Category — vitamin_d_status
text: Serum 25(OH)D standard biomarker vitamin D status. function classifies levels (assumed ng/mL) categories: Deficient (< 20 ng/mL) Insufficient (20-29 ng/mL) Sufficient (>= 30 ng/mL) Note: Ensure input units ng/mL. values appear extremely high (e.g., median > 150), might nmol/L (divide 2.5 convert ng/mL).

text: Vitamin D IoM(CtRDRI, Calcium (2011). Dietary Reference Intakes Calcium Vitamin D. National Academies Press. doi:10.17226/13050 . ; Holick MF, Binkley NC, Bischoff-Ferrari HA, et al. (2011). “Evaluation, Treatment, Prevention Vitamin D Deficiency: Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline.” Journal Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism, 96(7), 1911–1930. doi:10.1210/jc.2011-0385 .

what: Compute composite vitamin and endocrine marker ratios and z-scores — vitamin_markers
title: Compute composite vitamin and endocrine marker ratios and z-scores — vitamin_markers
text: Given serum/plasma vitamins related analytes, vitamin_markers() computes: VitD_Z: z-score 25(OH)D using provided reference mean/sd B12_Fol_Ratio: vitamin B12 / folate Ferr_TSat_R: ferritin / transferrin saturation (TSat) Cort_DHEA_R: cortisol / DHEA-S T_E2_Ratio: testosterone / estradiol TSH_fT4_R: TSH / free T4 Retinol_Z: z-score retinol using provided reference mean/sd Toco_Lip_R: alpha-tocopherol / total lipids Mg_Zn_R: magnesium / zinc Cu_Zn_R: copper / zinc Plus pass-: PIVKA_II, VitC, Homocysteine, MMA

title: Compute composite vitamin and endocrine marker ratios and z-scores — vitamin_markers
code: vitamin_markers(   data,   col_map = NULL,   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   na_warn_prop = 0.2,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Compute composite vitamin and endocrine marker ratios and z-scores — vitamin_markers
text: data data.frame tibble vitamin/analyte columns. col_map Named list mapping required keys column names: VitD, VitD_ref_mean, VitD_ref_sd, B12, Folate, Ferritin, TSat, Cortisol, DHEAS, Testosterone, Estradiol, TSH, free_T4, Retinol, Retinol_ref_mean, Retinol_ref_sd, Tocopherol, Total_lipids, PIVKA_II, VitC, Homocysteine, MMA, Magnesium, Zinc, Copper. na_action One c("keep","omit","error") required inputs. Default "keep". na_warn_prop Proportion \([0,1]\) trigger high-missingness debug notices. Default 0.2. verbose Logical; TRUE (default), prints column mapping, input availability, physiological range information (informational , values altered), list markers computed inputs, per-column results summary.

title: Compute composite vitamin and endocrine marker ratios and z-scores — vitamin_markers
text: tibble columns: VitD_Z, B12_Fol_Ratio, Ferr_TSat_R, Cort_DHEA_R, T_E2_Ratio, TSH_fT4_R, Retinol_Z, Toco_Lip_R, PIVKA_II, VitC, Homocysteine, MMA, Mg_Zn_R, Cu_Zn_R. ID column detected data (e.g. id, IID, participant_id), prepended first output column.

title: Compute composite vitamin and endocrine marker ratios and z-scores — vitamin_markers
text: HM-CS v2: Validation via hm_validate_inputs(data, col_map, required_keys, fn) User errors via rlang::abort(..., class=...) Verbosity via hm_inform(level) controlled options(healthmarkers.verbose) High-missingness diagnostics debug level

title: WHO-5 scoring — who5_score
code: who5_score(   data,   col_map = list(),   na_action = c("keep", "omit", "error"),   missing_prop_max = 0.2,   impute = c("none", "mean"),   prefix = "WHO5",   low_cutoff_percent = 50,   verbose = TRUE )

title: WHO-5 scoring — who5_score
text: data Data frame containing questionnaire item columns. col_map Named list mapping canonical item IDs column names; defaults assume items already named. na_action handle rows missing items: keep, omit, error. missing_prop_max Maximum allowed proportion missing items per row score set NA. impute Imputation strategy missing items threshold: none mean (row-wise mean). prefix Prefix output column names. low_cutoff_percent Percentage threshold low well-flag. verbose Logical; TRUE, emits informational messages column resolution scoring progress via hm_inform().

title: HealthMarkers 0.1.2
text: Multi-biobank column inference (major). internal synonym dictionary (.hm_default_col_patterns_exact()) now recognises column-naming conventions 15+ major cohort studies biobanks primary analytes: UK Biobank — _0_0 / _1_0 field naming (e.g. glucose_0_0, creatinine_0_0, vitamin_d_0_0); standing_height, 25OHD. NHANES — LBXS, LBDS, BPX, URX prefix variables (e.g. LBXGLU, LBXSCH, BPXSY1, URXUMA). Danish national registers / EHR (LABKA/OPEN) — NPU codes (NPU01994 creatinine, NPU01567 cholesterol, NPU03609 haemoglobin, etc.) Danish clinical labels (kreatinin, kolesterol, blodsukker, leukocytter, trombocytter, d_vitamin). HUNT Study Tromsø Study (Norway) — Norwegian-language terms (blodsukkerfasting, systolisk_blodtrykk, triglyserider, karbamid, kjonn, midjeomkrets). SCAPIS / TwinGene (Sweden) — Swedish-language terms (glukos, urinsyra, leukocyter, trombocyter, kön, längd, vikt). FinnGen / THL Biobank (Finland) — Finnish-language terms (glukoosi, kolesteroli, kreatiniini, hemoglobiini, sukupuoli, virtsahappo, leukosyytit, ferritiini, D_vitamiini). Estonian Biobank (EstBB) — Estonian terms (glukoos, kolesterool, kreatiniin, hemoglobiin, naatrium, kaalium, vanus, sugu). LifeLines Cohort / Rotterdam Study (Netherlands) — Dutch terms (nuchtere_glucose, totaal_cholesterol, urinezuur, ureum, leukocyten, hemoglobine, vitamine_D, tailleomtrek). Generation Scotland (GS:SFHS) — SBP_mean, DBP_mean, genetic_sex, ethnic_group. Us / OMOP CDM — LOINC codes LOINC_XXXX_X format major analytes (e.g. LOINC_2345_7 fasting glucose, LOINC_2160_0 creatinine, LOINC_718_7 haemoglobin). NAKO / KORA (Germany) — German-language terms (Cholesterin, Triglyzeride, Harnsäure, Harnstoff, Leukozyten, Thrombozyten). Added hm_col_report() — interactive column-mapping diagnostic. Call hm_col_report(your_data) running computation see formatted table internal keys matched columns data (exact synonym, case-insensitive, substring, fuzzy), plus ready--paste col_map template unmatched keys. function uses five-layer matching pipeline returns matched mappings invisibly result can passed directly col_map HealthMarkers function. Auto-derivation computed inputs. .hm_global_precompute() now automatically derives 18+ secondary columns marker computation begins, functions require (e.g.) eGFR, UACR, WHR, LDL_c, waist longer fail silently raw inputs present. Affected keys include eGFR (creatinine/age/sex via CKD-EPI), WHR (waist hip), UACR (u_albumin / u_creatinine), LDL_c (Friedewald TC/HDL_c/TG), MAP, PP, BMI (height/weight), . Expanded internal synonym dictionary (R/utils_infer-cols.R) variable groups, additionally incorporating: Inter99 / ADDITION real phenotype dataset labels Common literature spellings (TryG, TAG, TRIG, triacylglycerol; SGPT/GPT/ALAT; hsCRP/hs_CRP) Longitudinal follow-suffixes (_0, _1, _3, _5) NMR metabolomics (urine) panel, ECG markers, lifestyle covariates, cytokine multiplex proteins.

title: HealthMarkers 0.1.2
text: adiposity_sds(): fixed fn_name lookup bug caused incorrect error messages validation failed non-standard column names.

title: HealthMarkers 0.1.2
text: Renamed internal verbose-message helper hm_col_report() hm_fmt_col_map() avoid name collision new exported function.

title: HealthMarkers 0.1.1
text: Added liver_fat_markers() hepatic steatosis fibrosis index calculation. Added nfl_marker() plasma neurofilament light chain (NfL) z-score computation. Added impute_missing() within-row imputation missing biomarker values.

title: HealthMarkers 0.1.1
text: pulmo_markers(): fixed column-inference logic spirometry z-score inputs. marker functions: standardised verbose progress messages emit "debug" level verbose = FALSE, getOption("healthmarkers.verbose") controls visibility consistently across every function. Computing-phase messages now unconditionally emitted "debug" level (independent per-call verbose argument) bone_markers(), ckd_stage(), corrected_calcium(). all_health_markers(): column inference now keyed requested groups, falls back regex-based infer_cols() exact matches fail, reports per-group status (including missing optional packages) verbose summaries. Cardiovascular risk wrappers: cvd_risk_qrisk3() now prefers correctly named ethnicity column (typo tolerated backward compatibility), optional dependency errors now report missing package name consistently. Shared validation: lipid marker helpers now route hm_validate_inputs() duplicate/empty mappings caught uniformly.
